programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil seeks help from McCarthy's unlikeliest resident as he pursues his mine tour project, and Tim gets a reminder of home when Mark pays him a surprise visit

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Seven Deadly Sins
programme.desc: Actor Richard E Grant explores the origins of the seven deadly sins, examining the dramatic effect they have. He explores the roots of envy - the resentful longing for other people's possessions or qualities - and also investigates if it exists in the animal kingdom

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.desc: Dr Jeff heads to Texas to help out rescued animals after Hurricane Harvey. Back in Denver, a woman brings in Bear, who needs surgery after eating a sock

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Rocky, a young pitbull, fights for his life after being bitten by a rattlesnake, while Sassquatch the cat suffers after a fire at his owner's apartment

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: A hurricane is heading towards New Orleans. M2 and Kenani rush to prepare for baby number two and Tia and Tania's rescue mission takes a sudden turn

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy enters the Santé D'or shelter with the intention of helping an over-aggressive shelter cat who goes by the name of Pig Pen

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps the owners of Scout, who is so afraid of his tormentor Caramel that he lives inside a kitchen cabinet

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete creates a treehouse cinema, with loungers elevated into the trees to give a perfect view of the screen and food and drinks served by way of a pulley system

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on people who keep all manner of animals as pets. In the first edition, a so-called 'lion whisperer' in the Czech Republic talks about his big cats. Plus, the story of a Texan cowboy who lets buffalo roam in his house

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Fun facts about animals, including how swarms of blind bats avoid collision, whether mice really like cheese, why slugs are so slimy and what makes manatees' farts so special

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How humpback whales are able to leap so far out of the water, why dogs tilt their heads and how do spiders shed their own skin. Plus, why do animals gather in swarms?

programme.title: Tiger Shark King
programme.desc: Documentary examining the secret lives of tiger sharks, one of the most deadly predators in the oceans

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: As the battle for McCarthy wages on, a team of experts work to locate the only known entrance into the Mother Lode mine

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A Irish bar in Florida requests a pub-themed aquarium, while a jerky store in Las Vegas wants a butcher-block tank

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: When the Moon(shine) Hits Your Eye
programme.desc: Wayde King and Brett Raymer travel to Tennessee, where the Hatfield and McCoy's dinner theatre wants to replace an old moonshine still with an aquarium

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: In Mexico, the crew navigate a maze of crocodile and mosquito-infested waterways and lagoons in search of a rumoured paradise for fishing tarpon

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil's project is over budget, so his partner moves to push him out of his oldest business, while Jeremy and his boys go on a supply run and return with Jason Lobo

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A mother is sceptical when her children say they have seen a figure in the bathroom and a man covered in blood in the basement

programme.title: Tiger Shark King
programme.desc: Documentary examining the secret lives of tiger sharks, one of the most deadly predators in the oceans

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Marvin is reduced to tears by the sight of a pit bull puppy, an encounter with a snake makes Seth a prisoner in his own home and arachnophobe Jahara refuses to open any windows

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy works with a mother and daughter whose cats are having a territorial spraying match. He also meets with trouble-making cat Bowie, who has been given his last chance

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins
programme.desc: Robert travels to the Great Barrier Reef to conduct research on manta rays. The staff work together to save a baby wallaby struggling to survive

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Texas Game Wardens check hunting camps at the height of deer season and, on the Rio Grande, they stop a Mexican fisherman from poaching in American waters

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Amy meets Bobo the cat, who is badly wounded after a fight, and staff from animal control bring in neglected dog Juicy

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff performs surgery on a dog that cannot keep its food down and weighs 20 pounds less than it should. A cat is brought in with a bloody wound on its side

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: Tia, Tania and new employee Perry scramble after an injured dog during the night. A puppy's chance of a forever home is put in doubt

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: The team rescues 10 dogs abandoned in deplorable conditions, while Tia helps family members find the perfect companion for their teenage relative

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Tara, whose cat will not tolerate strangers, and Kurt, whose feline companion destroys or eats everything in sight

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy works with a couple whose cat, Zachary, sprays all over the house. He also deals with a trouble-making feline named Freddie Krueger

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: A friend asks Pete Nelson to build him a treehouse strictly for fun and relaxation, so he creates a space complete with a wine bar, loft and even a hot tub

programme.title: Meet the Penguins
programme.desc: Mummy's boy Pete refuses to leave the parental home, and options are running out for a penguin with a dislocated knee. One-eyed Edgar is desperate to please his wife

programme.title: Meet the Penguins
programme.desc: The staff battle to save a vulnerable chick, a portly penguin is put on a strict diet, and a juvenile of unknown gender gets some important test results

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: An experiment to find out if wild wolves are smarter than domestic dogs. Plus, how polar bears keep warm and whether spiders can fly

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Fun facts about animals, asking why drowned ants do not drown, whether woodpeckers get headaches and how a 15ft octopus can escape through a three-inch hole

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: Bradley and Billy are in the Mojave Desert, California, to find the bombardier beetle. When attacked or agitated, it releases a hot, noxious chemical spray

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: In the Madagascan rainforest, the team searches for a nearly invisible creature, the panther chameleon. When testing, the reptile seems to defy the laws of physics

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil breaks through to the Mother Lode mine, but the locals are too busy defending against waking bears to stop him and Jenny goes on a solo bear-hunt of her own

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett drop in on world-famous comedy magic duo Penn and Teller, who want an aquarium built in honour of their 40th anniversary together

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Howie Mandel is the Brains Behind ATM
programme.desc: Howie Mandel wants a tank, but is struggling to make up his mind on the design, leaving Wayde and Brett to complete the build without any guidance

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys tackle a treehouse cabin on the verge of collapse in Missouri. Misty constructs a greenhouse out of scavenged windows, and Matt builds a workshop

programme.title: Sharks of the Shadowlands
programme.desc: Attack survivor Jenny Oliver and researcher Kina Scollay set out to determine whether sevengill sharks are targeting divers, and changing behavioural patterns to hunt in packs

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A mother is concerned about her daughter's visions of a bloodied girl and a car crash, and wonders whether she needs psychiatric help or whether spirits lurk in their home

programme.title: Treetop Cat Rescue
programme.desc: Documentary following teams coming to the aid of distressed or missing felines. Shaun heads out on a solo night mission to rescue an animal that has been missing three weeks, and joins Tom in an attempt to rescue a feral cat

programme.title: Treetop Cat Rescue
programme.desc: Shaun and Tom investigate whether a stranded cat is really who its owner says it is. There is also an attempt to reunite a feline with its canine companion

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: A crippling fear of bats prevents Tom from leaving the house at night, Roxanne will not enter the bathroom by herself for fear of rats, and Laura strives hard to avoid cockroaches

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs host an edition of the music hall programme first broadcast in March 1976, with Ken Dodd, Sheila Steafal, Valerie Masterson, Johnny Hart and members of the Players' Theatre, London entertaining an audience at the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on December 10, 1999. Featuring Whitney Houston, Mario Piu, Jamiroquai, Celine Dion, Artful Dodger feat Craig David, Atomic Kitten and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Paul Burnett presents an edition from May 31, 1979, with performances by Quantum Jump, Chas & Dave, Sparks, Fischer-Z, Peaches & Herb, Voyager, Blondie and Hot Chocolate. Plus, dance sequences by Legs & Co

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Gary Davies present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 1 June 1989. Featuring Sinitta, Fuzzbox and Neneh Cherry

programme.title: Aretha Franklin at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of archive appearances on the BBC by Aretha Franklin. Featuring a stunning 2015 performance of You Make Me Feel (Like a Natural Woman) that had the then US president Barack Obama in tears, plus gems from the 1970s when Aretha dropped in to be the guest star on shows hosted by Cliff Richard and Lulu. These songs - and her voice - demonstrate why she remains considered as the ultimate queen of soul

programme.title: Expedition Rhino: The Search for the Last Northern White
programme.desc: With the death of the last captive male, Sudan, the Northern White Rhinoceros was officially declared extinct in 2018. But in recent years, possible sightings from a remote region in the heart of Africa have given conservationists hope. This film follows an international team of experts on an expedition to war-torn South Sudan in search of the northern white rhinoceros. An animal native to this unexplored and dangerous region in Africa, the team is taking a huge risk to realise the dream that a lost population of these incredible beasts still exists

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs host an edition of the music hall programme first broadcast in March 1976, with Ken Dodd, Sheila Steafal, Valerie Masterson, Johnny Hart and members of the Players' Theatre, London entertaining an audience at the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

programme.title: Vermiglio (2024)
programme.desc: In 1944, a Sicilian deserter arrives in a remote Alpine village and has a profound effect on the quiet, everyday lives of the devout Graziadei family, especially the daughters. Italian drama, starring Giuseppe De Domenico, Tommaso Ragno, Roberta Rovelli and Martina Scrinzi. In Italian

programme.title: Butterflies
programme.desc: Middle-aged housewife Ria worries that life is passing her by and wonders if things will ever perk up. However, she is consumed with guilt on meeting new love interest Leonard, a lonely executive with time on his hands. First episode of Carla Lane's comedy, starring Wendy Craig and Geoffrey Palmer. First aired in 1978

programme.title: Artsnight
programme.desc: The writer discusses her life and work, while her son Carl offers personal insights into his mother's career and legacy. With contributions from Liver Birds stars Polly James and Nerys Hughes as well as Geoffrey Palmer and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

programme.title: Expedition Rhino: The Search for the Last Northern White
programme.desc: With the death of the last captive male, Sudan, the Northern White Rhinoceros was officially declared extinct in 2018. But in recent years, possible sightings from a remote region in the heart of Africa have given conservationists hope. This film follows an international team of experts on an expedition to war-torn South Sudan in search of the northern white rhinoceros. An animal native to this unexplored and dangerous region in Africa, the team is taking a huge risk to realise the dream that a lost population of these incredible beasts still exists

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg looks back at the Second World War by visiting Osterley Park in Middlesex, site of the first Home Guard training school. Robert Brown, from North Yorkshire, encounters his father as a company commander, and Dad's Army co-writer Jimmy Perry explains how his experience in the Home Guard inspired him to create the hit comedy

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Golden Age of Coach Travel
programme.desc: The history of the coach, which offered a cheaper alternative to rail travel upon its arrival in the 1950s and gave greater freedom to people previously restricted to their own villages and towns due to limited funds. The documentary looks at the distinctive liveries employed by different transport companies and the activities of passengers, including singing and pub visits, as they travelled to destinations such as Blackpool, Margate and Torquay. Narrated by June Whitfield

programme.title: John Wilson's American Greats at the Proms
programme.desc: The Sinfonia of London perform a selection of US favourites at the Royal Albert Hall. The concert is conducted by John Wilson and comprises Gershwin's Rhapsody in Blue, Barber's Adagio for Strings, Copland's ballet Billy the Kid suite and John Adams' Harmonielehre. Presented by Georgia Mann

programme.title: The Magic of Dance with Margot Fonteyn
programme.sub-title: Out in the Limelight, Home in the Rain
programme.desc: The former ballerina explores the life of a dancer, from the rigours of ballet classes, rehearsals and preparation to the moment of judgement that comes with the performance. Some of Fonteyn's greatest moments on stage are relived in Salut d'Amour, which Frederick Ashton created for a gala birthday tribute. Plus, a performance of Ashton's ballet Marguerite and Armand

programme.title: Peaky Blinders: Rambert's The Redemption of Thomas Shelby
programme.desc: The Rambert Dance Company perform their version of the TV show at Birmingham Hippodrome, choreographed and directed by the company's artistic director Benoit Swan Pouffer, and adapted by the writer of the drama Steven Knight. In post-war industrial Birmingham, the Shelby family navigate decisions that determine their fate, as Tommy becomes intoxicated by mysterious newcomer Grace - unaware she is an undercover agent on a mission to get close to the heart of his gang. Benjamin Zephaniah, who died last month, and played Jeremiah Jesus in the TV show, provides narration, while the on-stage band performs popular music heard on screen, including songs by Nick Cave and the Bad Seeds, Radiohead, Anna Calvi, the Last Shadow Puppets

programme.title: Forest, Field & Sky: Art out of Nature
programme.desc: James Fox journeys through six different landscapes across Britain, meeting artists whose work explores the human relationship with the natural world, from Andy Goldsworthy's stone sculptures to James Turrell's extraordinary Skyspaces

programme.title: Reputations
programme.desc: Profile examining the heroic image of American movie star John Wayne, and why he supported the Vietnam war, yet avoided service during World War Two, supported the Hollywood blacklist and attacked civil rights groups

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Golden Age of Coach Travel
programme.desc: The history of the coach, which offered a cheaper alternative to rail travel upon its arrival in the 1950s and gave greater freedom to people previously restricted to their own villages and towns due to limited funds. The documentary looks at the distinctive liveries employed by different transport companies and the activities of passengers, including singing and pub visits, as they travelled to destinations such as Blackpool, Margate and Torquay. Narrated by June Whitfield

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall, Rick Stein, Ching-He Huang, Gennaro Contaldo and Andi Oliver, and by special guests Liz Carr and Chris Stark, to celebrate the show's 20th anniversary. Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are memorable moments from the BBC food archive

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys actress Eilish O'Carroll (Winnie McGoogan) joins Anna to explore their native Dublin City. They pay a visit to the iconic Guinness factory, where brewing began in 1759. Inspired by the visit, Anna cooks with her mum, evoking childhood memories. Eilish also takes Anna to Moore Street, where traditional markets mix with global street food. The pair later cook a dish inspired by the city's diverse flavours

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three sets of developers seek to squeeze every bit of space out of their new properties in Northampton, Leeds and Coventry

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian presents the latest contest from the Great Wetherby Racecourse Antiques Fair in West Yorkshire. Helped by experts Caroline Hawley and Colin Young, the red and the blue teams go head to head to see who can buy the most profitable items to sell at auction. Meanwhile, Danny has an intriguing mystery item to perplex fairgoers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A smashed-up wardrobe poses a carpentry conundrum for Bruce Kenneth. Can he hammer, saw and smash his way to perfection?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple who are looking for a new home in the countryside of West Sussex after 15 years of living and working in Dubai

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Florence Headlam are in Egham to transform an overgrown and unloved plot into an Islamic-themed garden that echoes the owners' trips to the Middle East

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.sub-title: Weighing Scales, Mini Lifebelts, African Birthing Chairs
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as five sellers aim to get the best price for their items, including weighing scales, miniature lifebelts, lightning rods, vases and African birthing chairs

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes quiz, with Olympian Greg Rutherford, comedian and actor Sanjeev Kohli, presenter and writer Janet Ellis and actor Louisa Lytton playing to win money for their chosen charities. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money with each correct answer

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Chris McCausland, Emmett J Scanlan, Melvin Odoom, Sue Perkins, Grace Dent and Vanessa Williams

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a comedy edition of the star-studded general knowledge quiz. Olga Koch, Chloe Petts, Arabella Weir, Jon Culshaw, Ted Robbins, Justin Moorhouse, Neil Delamere and Loyiso Gola try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Celebrity edition of the quiz, hosted by Romesh Ranganathan. Mel B, Eamonn Holmes, Shobna Gulati, Denis Lawson, Arielle Free, Briony May Williams, Nick Pickard and Rob Bent are the contestants battling it out to be crowned the strongest link and will take home the prize for their charity

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Flynn tries to secure treatment to abused soldier Rory, and confides in Stevie that the case has disturbing parallels with a tragic incident from his own military service. But Rory may be too scared to allow himself to be helped. Jodie discovers a possible link between cancer patients and the Wyvern Hill Estate, and Rash crosses a line to assuage his guilt

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe feels liberated at a Gods and Monsters party hosted by eccentric local expats Flick and James, while Dan spirals into a dark place back at the villa. News of their separation destabilises each of the friends as the party takes unexpected turns. Drama, starring Jessica Raine

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Blade Runner 2049 (2017)
programme.desc: An android-hunter working for the Los Angeles Police Department discovers a long-buried secret, which leads him to track down former detective Rick Deckard, who's been missing for 30 years and is linked to the case. Denis Villeneuve's sci-fi thriller, the sequel to Ridley Scott's 1982 classic, starring Ryan Gosling, Harrison Ford, Ana de Armas and Sylvia Hoeks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: The big names behind the big stories. Laura Kuenssberg talks to those making the news, inside and outside politics.

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents the political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.sub-title: St John's Wood United Synagogue, London
programme.desc: Religious services from various faiths. The first episode comes from St John's Wood United Synagogue in north London. Senior Rabbi Ivan Binstock welcomes viewers to an Orthodox Jewish morning prayer service, also known as Shacharit

programme.title: Spirit Untamed (2021)
programme.desc: After moving to a sleepy little town, young Lucky Prescott befriends a wild mustang named Spirit, who shares her rebellious nature. When a heartless wrangler plans to capture Spirit and his herd, Lucky and her new friends embark on the adventure of a lifetime to rescue the horse that changed her life. Animation, with the voice of Isabela Merced

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's second children's book about the accident-prone dragon. Zog is helping his friend Princess Pearl to provide much-needed medical care to all manner of creatures, assisted by knight Sir Gadabout. However, when a storm forces them to return to Pearl's home at the palace, her uncle the king does not approve of her career - at least until he falls ill himself. Narrated by Lenny Henry, with the voices of Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Aided by experts Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell, the reds and the blues go head to head at the Second Hand Warehouse in Leominster, Herefordshire, to see who can buy the most profitable items to take to auction. Presented by Eric Knowles

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits Cromer on the Norfolk coast to meet presenter Simon Thomas, as he reflects on love, loss and faith, and the defining childhood moments that shaped him

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A British couple who have been living in Canada since the 1980s are back home and keen to settle in Dorset, so Ginny Buckley shows them a handful of properties that might just do the trick. Away from the search, she also visits a farm helping to keep the elderly and retired gents fit in mind, body and soul

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: A look at the Gulf Coast's extraordinary wildlife, including an alligator teaching hatchlings how to hunt and manatees practicing a rarely filmed courtship ritual. In the Texan thornscrub, rarely seen footage shows a mother ocelot, one of the last remaining in the United States, raising her kittens

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The wildlife of the Andes, including southern Peru's marbled four-eyed frog, which is capable of freezing solid and reviving daily, captured on film for the first time. In Ecuador, a mother bear and her cubs navigate a dangerous rock face, while high in the mountains, flamingos perform mating dances on crimson lakes. The rare spatuletail hummingbirds, with only 500 males left, engage in intense competition

programme.title: Shrek the Third (2007)
programme.desc: The bad-tempered ogre finds himself in the unwelcome position of being next in line for the throne. Preferring to stay at home in the swamps, he sets out on a quest to find the rightful heir - a rebellious teenager named Artie - and persuade him to face up to his responsibilities as the future king. Meanwhile, the ambitious Prince Charming sees his chance to take the throne. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas and Rupert Everett

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Wonders of the Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Joe Crowley, Margherita Taylor and Adam Henson explore a side of the Isle of Wight few visitors ever see. Beyond the beaches and bustling holiday towns, the island's rare geology and southerly location have shaped a landscape rich in surprises, from unusual marine life and distinctive habitats to crops more often associated with warmer climates

programme.title: Tiger Island
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring a community-owned river island in Nepal that is home to a large population of tigers, with these normally solitary creatures living in close proximity to each other. Drone technology follows two tiger mothers, investigating how these solitary big cats are raising cubs on an island full of dangers, including other tigers, rhinos and elephants

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Special episode on the history of sport to coincide with the World Cup, celebrating both men's and women's football, rugby, cricket, tennis and the Olympics. The Roadshow team visits some of the UK's most famous sports grounds, including Lord's and Wimbledon. Featuring contributions from Tessa Sanderson, Caz Walton and Gareth Edwards

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: John and Janie arrive at a new age commune to investigate the murder of its founder. Janie hopes to solve the case quickly, as her estranged father is one of the members. Complicated feelings around him are brought to the fore, especially as John disregards her warnings not to trust her father

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: In Search of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Twenty-eight years on from when Scotland last qualified for the World Cup finals, the comedian embarks on a global journey to understand what football has become and whether the sport that once brought people together still has beauty at its heart. Along the way he meets former Brazil star Cafu, Scotland vice-captain John McGinn, Sao Paulo ultras, US football fans and many more as he looks at how the game is experienced across different communities and environments

programme.title: The Damned United
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police in Edinburgh receive a call from the university reporting that the family of 19-year-old international student Xiaoqiang have been unable to reach him over the past five days and are becoming increasingly worried about him. A call to Xiaoqiang's mother in China soon reveals why she is so worried, as they are a wealthy family and her son has been asking her for unusually large sums of money. In Glasgow, another woman is worried that her son may be in danger as 20-year-old Daniel has failed to return after leaving to go hiking in the mountains, and it's now nearly nightfall

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Bodies Bodies Bodies (2022)
programme.desc: When Sophie and new girlfriend Bee attend a party at a remote family mansion, drugs and alcohol flow. Bee unknowingly devours a dope-spiked cake, and tension between revellers explodes in the darkened house during a game of Bodies Bodies Bodies. However, one member of the party soon plays a victim for real. Horror comedy, starring Amandla Stenberg, Maria Bakalova and Rachel Sennott

programme.title: Love in the Flesh
programme.desc: Zara McDermott presents a dating show exploring what happens when couples who have formed relationships through apps and social media finally meet face to face

programme.title: Love in the Flesh
programme.desc: All five couples go on their first dates with no screens or filters to hide behind. Zara McDermott announces that there are two private suites and that it's up to the group to decide who should spend the night in them and why. Tension builds when one of the housemates reveals her head has been turned by another of the men in the beach house, and her online partner is left feeling crushed

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The Boiler Shop arts and performance space in Newcastle is the venue for the fourth heat, which sees comics Charlie Ashton, Murph, Zoe Cousin, Caira O'Connor and Shugufta Choudhry battling it out to book their place in this year's grand final in Bradford. Amy Gledhill hosts the proceedings and the judges for the night are Teesside local Phil Dunning, former winner Dan Tiernan and head judge Fatiha El-Ghorri

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: A overly dependent young woman's parents die in a car accident, forcing her to fend for herself. It transpires she will inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left by her parents. Chi's highly strung Auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her 'pest of a niece', but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers. Offbeat, dysfunctional family sitcom, written by and starring Lucia Keskin, with Selin Hizli

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi spectacularly fails a trial period at the pharmacy with Karen, but Dave kindly lets her shadow him on his delivery rounds. On a drop-off to a retirement home Chi finds her tribe - old people. She also falls in love with Linda, an elderly lady who seems to think Chi is her husband. Dave has a health scare and Karen starts to think she might be the fertility issue. Offbeat, dysfunctional family sitcom, starring Lucia Keskin, Selin Hizli and Daniel Fearn

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An encounter with creatures from a universe with only two dimensions leaves the Doctor trapped in side a rapidly shrinking TARDIS. Clara must take on the creatures alone to protect a group of community service workers as every image on every wall becomes a potentially lethal threat. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: In the Forest of the Night
programme.desc: One morning, the human race wakes up to find the planet has been invaded by its own trees, as overnight a forest has grown everywhere and taken back the Earth. Clara and Danny have to lead a school trip through the wilderness that has overtaken London, while the Doctor realsies the final days of humanity may have finally arrived. Peter Capaldi stars in an episode by novelist and screenwriter Frank Cottrell Boyce

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny and Miranda are taken into police custody and made to see a therapist - whose assessment technique entails staring at them in stony silence while they grow increasingly nervous and irritable. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart and Patricia Hodge

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The joke-shop owner dreams of the perfect Christmas without her parents and decides to celebrate at home with Stevie, Gary, Clive and Tilly instead. However, a lack of presents, an infuriating encounter at the post office, a run-in with some carol singers and a few arguments in the flat all begin to wear Miranda down - and she may end up back with her mother and father after all. Guest starring Tom Conti, with Miranda Hart. First broadcast in 2010

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Grandad's brother Micky is fresh out of prison and decides to stop by the house for a visit, but his family are soon suspicious when he asks for 5,000 euro to invest in a South African goldmine. Winnie is inspired by Cathy's book to change the trajectory of her life, while the boys have a heart to heart

programme.title: The Nice Guys (2016)
programme.desc: A private detective investigates the murder of a porn star in 1970s Los Angeles, only to run into a hired thug employed to scare him off. However, the mysterious disappearance of a woman connected to the case forces the unlikely duo to work together, leading them to uncover a government conspiracy. Crime comedy, starring Russell Crowe and Ryan Gosling

programme.title: Daisy Maskell: Insomnia and Me
programme.desc: For as long as she can remember, Daisy Maskell has suffered with insomnia, surviving on as little as a couple of hours a sleep a night. In this documentary, the breakfast radio host sets out to uncover the root cause of her problem and take the first steps toward treatment. She meets fellow sufferers to investigate why thousands of young people in the UK are struggling with sleep disorders, and tries a multitude of remedies and treatments in the hope of finally getting a better night's sleep

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz must choose between Donna and Janet when they give him an ultimatum to decide who he wants to be with - but as the deadline approaches, he still has doubts. Elsewhere, Louise worries Scott will run off with a groupie

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two burglars break into a house, where they come across a man already tied up. Comedy short, written by and starring Stephen Buchanan and Martin Quinn

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An encounter with creatures from a universe with only two dimensions leaves the Doctor trapped in side a rapidly shrinking TARDIS. Clara must take on the creatures alone to protect a group of community service workers as every image on every wall becomes a potentially lethal threat. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: In the Forest of the Night
programme.desc: One morning, the human race wakes up to find the planet has been invaded by its own trees, as overnight a forest has grown everywhere and taken back the Earth. Clara and Danny have to lead a school trip through the wilderness that has overtaken London, while the Doctor realsies the final days of humanity may have finally arrived. Peter Capaldi stars in an episode by novelist and screenwriter Frank Cottrell Boyce

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Walford has gathered following the drama at Vicki and Ross' wedding ceremony. Yolande helps Denise as she processes life-changing news, and Ian is blissfully unaware that his happy life is about to change

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian and Kathy arrive home in good spirits as she tells him how proud she is that he's been voted Councillor Beale, but the pair are unaware of events that are unfolding. Their happiness is short-lived when they receive a knock at the door, and Ian realises there is a chance he could stand to lose everything. Elsehwere, Max drops a bombshell which leaves Cindy shocked

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Denise attempts to be strong for Chelsea, she hides her own emotions and decides to keep her diagnosis a secret from her loved ones. Yolande covers for her when questions about her previous whereabouts arise. Max's emotions force him into a reckless decision, and George makes a worrying discovery

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: George makes a decision for the sake of his family, and the sun rises on Walford after a life-changing night. It's clear the tricky road ahead has only just begun for some. Denise continues to support Chelsea, but soon realises it's time she faces her own situation

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Things do not go to plan when Pip gives evidence in court. Connor takes part in a risky set-up in the hope of extracting information from one of Pip's old foes - and while it doesn't reveal anything about the search for Jamie it does turn up something unexpected about Cara, leading to a heated confrontation between her and Pip

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A sinister message sends Pip, Ravi and Connor to a remote location where a disturbing piece of evidence reveals they are retracing Jamie's steps. An unexpected encounter leaves Pip questioning whether she was right to discount Max as her original suspect. Max shocks everyone with his testimony in court, throwing the proceedings into further uncertainty

programme.title: Everest (2015)
programme.desc: Two expeditions set out to climb to the summit of the world's highest mountain. Both parties of mountaineers get caught in one of the fiercest snowstorms ever recorded, and face a desperate struggle to survive against the elements. Fact-based adventure, starring Jason Clarke, Josh Brolin, Jake Gyllenhaal and Keira Knightley

programme.title: Ibiza: Secrets of the Party Island
programme.desc: Zara McDermott meets Spain's top lawyer when it comes to defending drug traffickers, Ricardo Alvarez-Ossorio, and joins the Spanish Civil Guard as they tackle organised crime and drug trafficking across the country. While on a night shift with an ambulance crew, Zara speaks to a nurse who explains how Ibiza is the ideal testing ground for new drugs

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella discusses how she claimed to have been forced into smuggling in the hope of getting a reduced sentence, only to find herself spending the first of what would be many days behind bars in the cells of the police's anti-narcotics division. However, her story soon caught the attention of the international press, with reporters around the world desperate for the inside scoop

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings navigate life with Dr Feelgood while dodging Leo's suspicions. Jamie takes Tiana on an impromptu date and Ms Jenkins grows concerned about Tanika's home life

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings hire a fake Jackie to fool Ms Jenkins and Tiana clashes with Shanice while juggling work, studies and family. Dr Feelgood tries to leave drug dealing behind

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Comedy, written by and starring Dane Baptiste as a frustrated 29-year-old, still living at home with his parents. Dane dreads a party to celebrate his dad's 65th birthday, knowing his relatives are going to have awkward questions about his career and love life. When he tries to boost his confidence with the aid of a bottle of rum, it leads to some awkward revelations and an accidental proposal

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Dane decides to move out of his parents' house and get a place with Nicola. The problem is that his boss Kerry is determined to seduce him, and with so much at stake he cannot afford to get on the wrong side of her. He unwisely encourages her advances, only to find himself in a compromising position that could ruin all his plans. Katherine Ryan guest stars

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Dane dreads having to introduce Nicola's father, who despises him, to his own parents. The meeting is made even more stressful when it falls on the same day as a work football match, overseen by Kerry. Fortunately, Kadean is on hand to interrupt the tension in the family get-together with a shocking revelation

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Dane's life has finally hit rock bottom, as Nicola refuses to speak to him following an incident involving her father, while Kerry continues to make his life a misery at work. Some words of advice from one of his heroes give him the confidence to try and put things right - while Stefan comes up with a plan to solve all his problems

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals reach their end, and four of the five remaining cooks will eventually make it through to Finals Week. Anna Haugh and Grace Dent have two demanding tasks up their sleeves, and will want to see glimpses of championship-winning food in all the plates delivered. The judges have invited pastry chef Philip Khoury, and the cooks are asked to recreate one of his signature desserts

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget attempts to make all her Piri friends' secret hopes and dreams come true, but gets them the wrong way round, with funny and very unexpected consequences

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard steals Dad's special plant from the ship - and just in time

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy finds a box full of magic props but won't share, upsetting Spicy who wants to be a magician too

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The tired heroes try to find out and stop whatever is making the loud wailing noise that has kept the residents of the forest up all night

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy wants to watch TV, but the Lemmings want to have fun playing music

programme.title: Duck & Frog
programme.desc: Duck realises that dogs are a duck?s best friend when he walks a poodle. But when Frog realises a picture with a poodle could mean fame, he goes all out to snap the dog for himself

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: An encounter with a gang of Dragon Tamers leads Liko and Roy to an unexpected reunion

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: The brothers try to survive Britain's most haunted castle and four terrifying Gladiators as they attempt to avoid the tricks and collect the treats

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.sub-title: Hammer, Nitro, Fire, Phantom v the Gym Rematch!
programme.desc: Hammer, Nitro, Fire and Phantom flex their prank powers in the gym, but only one can win. Will doughnut dares, motivational mayhem or haunted gym bikes cause an early tap out?

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Football World Cup Takeover with SV2 and Manny
programme.desc: With a football World Cup takeover featuring social media stars SV2 and Manny

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Roses are stealing the spotlight at Longmeadow, and Monty Don is celebrating all the full, head-turning bloom. Ashley Edwards visits Benton End in Suffolk, a place of artistic vision and horticultural legacy. And in Penarth, Sue Kent uncovers a rich mix of gardens, personalities and inspiration

programme.title: Great Expectations (1946)
programme.desc: An orphan falls in love with the cold-hearted adopted daughter of an eccentric old woman. A mysterious benefactor gives him the chance to rise through London's high society, but in the process he forgets his humble roots and is not prepared for the truth about the girl he loves and the stranger funding his new life. Drama, based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring John Mills, Jean Simmons, Finlay Currie and Alec Guinness

programme.title: The Wooden Horse (1950)
programme.desc: British PoWs in a German camp try to escape their captors by taking up gymnastics, and using a vaulting horse to conceal the tunnel they are digging. However, even if they manage to escape the camp, the journey to neutral territory will not be an easy one. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Leo Genn, David Tomlinson, Anthony Steel and David Greene

programme.title: Ice Station Zebra (1968)
programme.desc: An American atomic submarine commander races against time to reach a Soviet spy satellite that has crash-landed near a British research station in the Arctic - before Russian troops arrive to stake their claim to it. Adventure based on Alistair MacLean's novel, starring Rock Hudson, Ernest Borgnine, Jim Brown, Tony Bill and Patrick McGoohan

programme.title: Super League Live
programme.desc: Wakefield Trinity v Hull Kingston Rovers (Kick-off 5.30pm). All the action from the match at DIY Kitchens Stadium. Rovers are the reigning champions, but Wakefield claimed a 28-12 victory when the teams last met here in in the penultimate round of regular-season fixtures in 2025, with four different players scoring tries for the victors, including Max Jowitt, who also kicked six goals

programme.title: The Game - The Story of Hurling
programme.desc: A journey through the history of hurling and what it means to Northern Ireland as a whole, looking first at the groundwork on which the sport was founded

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Great Food Made Simple
programme.desc: Suzie Lee Arbuthnot creates an easy poke bowl

programme.title: Today at the Test
programme.sub-title: England v New Zealand: First Test Day Three
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day three of the First Test in the three-match series, held at Lord's. Ben Stokes' side have won two of the last three Test series against New Zealand, as well as winning six of their last eight matches against them. The last time these two teams met in this format, England claimed a 2-1 series victory in New Zealand, as they won the first two matches by eight wickets and 323 runs respectively

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Scotland v Bolivia (Kick-off 9.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Sports Illustrated Stadium in Harrison, New Jersey. This is Scotland's final warm-up match before they face Haiti in their opening match of the FIFA World Cup on 13th June in Foxborough. This contest is likely to serve as a good test, as the Bolivians only missed out on the finals themselves after losing out to Iraq in the play-offs, having finished one place outside the automatic qualifying places in the notoriously difficult CONMEBOL group

programme.title: Weather Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood and Tomasz Schafernaker present a selection of performances of weather-related songs from the archives, including Bill Withers singing Ain't No Sunshine When She's Gone and Why Does It Always Rain on Me? By Travis

programme.title: Paris, 13th District (2021)
programme.desc: The lives of four young people intertwine as they navigate love, sex and romance in a modern city. Romance, starring Lucie Zhang and Makita Samba. In French, Mandarin and English

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A runner is found dead on the set of a Welsh TV drama. John helps Janie to investigate, and is struck by how much of an outsider he feels in what should be his natural home - especially whern he runs into an old co-star. Janie befriends the show's leading actor, leaving John concerned that she will not be able to put her personal connection aside and stay open to all suspects. Guest starring Jim Howick (Ghosts)

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the devastating defeat at Wembley, Southgate's guilt weighs heavily on his conscience. When the fans then turn on the manager, accusing him of prioritising politics over performance, he begins to doubt himself ahead of a controversial World Cup in Qatar. Drama, starring Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter F as she continues her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Oxfordshire. She visits Waterperry, where gardeners are the custodians of a formidable legacy, and chats to Mary Spiller, the first female presenter of Gardeners' World. She also meets the third-generation owner of a remarkable garden at Kingston Bagpuize, and discovers the roots of the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Adam Henson are in Lisburn, Northern Ireland, for the 157th Balmoral Show, the region's largest agricultural and food event, welcoming more than 100,000 visitors each year. Adam explores heritage crafts that the community are determined to preserve and joins families showing their prize livestock, while Sean is in charge of the stopwatch at the Tractor handling competition

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond helps a Glencoe youth hostel warden as the busy tourist season gets under way, while Robert Burns fan Cammy Wilson makes a pilgrimage to Ellisland Farm in Dumfries and Galloway. Arlene Stuart meets a Belgian family pursuing their dream by running a sheep farm in Perth and Kinross, and Leanna Fernandez is on the Solway coast searching for rare amphibian the natterjack toad

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum Clunie plants up hanging baskets, a great way to decorate a doorstep or shed, and talks about his favourite bedding plants that can be planted right now. Brian Cunningham demonstrates the best way to tackle a border that has got out of control, while Calum shows just what can be done in an average-sized garden

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipes from Gurdeep Loyal, Shivi Ramoutar and Greg Marchand, while McFly's Danny Jones faces his food heaven or hell

programme.title: Mary Berry - Cook & Share
programme.desc: Mary visits Bristol, the city where she had her first job and where her son William studied at university. While in the city, Mary shares dishes including shakshuka, Tuscan chicken, and pear and blueberry galette. She also samples a Michelin-starred menu and tries her hand at street art

programme.title: Andi Oliver's Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi visits Folkestone, where she helps local singer-songwriter Sophia Stutchbury stage a festival to celebrate the town's unsung musical heritage and cultural diversity. The chef takes on the task of devising a feast that takes inspiration from a wide range of international cuisines, including Polish, Syrian, Ukrainian and Nepalese

programme.title: Cat on a Hot Tin Roof (1958)
programme.desc: A dying plantation owner summons his two sons to put his estate in order. The older of the pair, a washed-up American football player with a drinking problem, is tormented by the death of a friend and resents his loyally supportive wife, and is forced by the reunion to come to terms with his personal demons. Drama based on Tennessee Williams' stage play, starring Paul Newman, Elizabeth Taylor and Burl Ives

programme.title: An Ideal Husband (1999)
programme.desc: A rising politician is blackmailed into pushing a dubious scheme through Parliament, a decision that threatens his career and marriage. Turning in desperation to an old and trusted friend, he ends up dragged even further into a mire of lies, temptations and secret liaisons. Oscar Wilde comedy drama, with Cate Blanchett, Minnie Driver, Rupert Everett, Jeremy Northam and Julianne Moore

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: The BAUHAUS-galan. Coverage of the fifth meeting of the campaign, held at Stockholm Olympic Stadium in Sweden. Home favourite Armand Dublantis broke his own world record here last year, clearing 6.28m in the pole vault. He has since added another 3cm to the record in Uppsala and he will be looking to break the record once again this evening. On the track, Duplantis' compatriot Andreas Almgren was victorious in the women's 5,000m, and USA's Olympic champion Rai Benjamin prevailed in the men's 400m hurdles

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Famous faces embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge. Expert assistants help them unearth bargains at shops and markets, before they head to an auction to discover whose haul raises the most money

programme.title: Today at the Test
programme.sub-title: England v New Zealand: First Test Day Four
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from the fourth day of the First Test in the three-match series from Lord's. The last time these two sides met in this format was in December 2024 in the Third and Final Test in a series in New Zealand. England had already won the series after two victories, but the Kiwi's fought back in the last game, winning an extremely one-sided contest by 423 runs

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and a team of experts head into a remote part of the Moukalaba-Doudou National Park. Cut off from the outside world by the Doudou mountains, this zone has never been explored before. Teaming up with local jungle guide Ghislain Bouassa and primatologist Alessandra Mascaro, Steve hopes to discover whether this unexplored jungle could be home to an undiscovered population of chimpanzees

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: In 2016, the parents-turned-sleuths have tracked Megan and Jean down to Disney World in Florida and are still questioning how ill the teenager really is. They can see that people are still donating to the fundraising campaign, many of them also parents of sick children, and so they resolve to raise the alarm that Meg might not be ill at all, but then a tragedy makes them question everything they have uncovered

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Taking to the stage at Alexandra Palace Theatre in north London are singer-songwriters Ellie Goulding and Mitski, singer and rapper Wesley Joseph, and bands Pigeon and Westside Cowboy. Chatting with Jools at the piano are Duran Duran's Simon Le Bon, and jazz musician Courtney Pine, who performs a Duke Ellington standard with the host

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents the political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: Unforgiven (1992)
programme.desc: Two notorious gunslingers come out of retirement to help a young assassin collect the $1,000 bounty on the heads of a pair of cowboys who attacked and mutilated a prostitute in the backwoods town of Big Whiskey. The local sheriff, however, has no intention of letting the pair impinge on his own authority. Oscar-winning Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, with Gene Hackman, Morgan Freeman, Jaimz Woolvett and Richard Harris

programme.title: If Beale Street Could Talk (2018)
programme.desc: In early 1970s Harlem, 19-year old Tish Rivers recalls the longtime relationship between her and fiancé Alonzo 'Fonny' Hunt. With a child on the way, their plans of starting a family and embracing the future are derailed when Fonny is arrested for a crime he did not commit. Director Barry Jenkins' Oscar-winning drama, starring KiKi Layne and Stephan James

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul travel to Dorset to try to catch grayling in the River Frome, focusing on a stretch of water where the British record for the fish was set, so hopes are high that they could catch something big Later, they attempt mackerel fishing on Chesil Beach, close to the feeding peak at dusk

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team head to the Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan in search of snow leopards in the remote Djangart valley Joining forces with Kyrgyz conservation group Snow Leopard Foundation, Steve and the team set out to find evidence of a breeding population of snow leopards in the hope of persuading the government of Kyrgyzstan to turn the valley into a protected nature reserve

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Niall has managed to build a new life, only for old ties to resurface when Mona reconnects with Ruben. Before long events begin to spiral toward an intense and devastating conclusion. Hard-hitting drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell, Amy Manson and Neve McIntosh

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: As Euro 2024 begins, England arrive as favourites for Southgate's final tournament. Gareth is forced to make some challenging changes to the team and puts his trust in a new generation. When the team struggles unexpectedly and pressure mounts, Gareth wonders how his story will end

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath explores creepy dolls and a haunted hearse at Nottingham's macabre museum with Dr Kate Cherrell. Kate visits the National Justice Museum, a 17th-century courthouse and prison that may still house the spirits of former inmates. Meanwhile, Andy heads to one of Britain's oldest pubs, where he learns how an unpaid bar tab contributed to the legend of a cursed galleon. Later, he embarks on a quest to find evidence that Robin Hood, Nottinghamshire's legendary hero, may have been more than just a myth

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas scrutinise an astonishing case that took place in November 2018 off the west coast of Ireland, in which multiple pilots saw bizarre lights through their cockpit windows. Ted Roe, Director of Research at the National Aviation Reporting Centre shares extraordinary insights into the sighting, while an ex-Nato air traffic controller - under the cover of anonymity - acts as a whistle-blower, spilling more secrets of the sky and the shocking lengths that governments are prepared to go to hide the truth

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Mystery Sea Beast, The Earth Is Alive and Shapeshifting UFO
programme.desc: A mysterious sea beast seen from the shore of a small seaside town in the UK. Plus, the notion that a field of massive mysterious jars were made by giants. Tony Harris and his team of experts examine the clues and make a determination about the validity of what they are being told

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les sells a valuable Breitling watch for $42,000 to a trusted watch collector that he dealt with many times before. His customer does not have the funds, so Les gives him the merchandise in exchange for an IOU. Seth has grave misgivings about the deal, but Les still has trust in the customer

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les makes a deal to get the others off his back, but when some interesting animals come through the door, it might be too much for him to resist

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: With over 40,000 wristbands to sell, stored in 126 boxes, Les decides to sell them one by one, starting at $2 apiece. Seth and Ashley remain sceptical of the idea

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Expert and presenter David Dickinson presides as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead?

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: The buyers visit Orangeville, Ontario, where Ursula is determined to prove she is a major player, while Paul and Bogart uncover an unusual item

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The Empress of Britain, an enormous, palatial ocean liner that also doubled up as a fabulous first-class cruise ship for the super wealthy. Her fame and appeal was short-lived after the Empress of Britain was called up for war duty

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The fate of the Ile de France. The luxury ocean liner was regarded as the pride of France, after saving many lives in the Second World War and then emerging from the conflict. Just before being scrapped, the ship was used as a floating prop for the 1960 Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer disaster film The Last Voyage

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Sex Life
programme.desc: The myriad of rumours, theories and disputed historical accounts of Adolf Hitler's sexual psychology, considering how it integrated into his overall twisted psyche. programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on expeditions through some of Britain's most historic landscapes in search of the richest stories from the nation's past. He begins with a 40-mile walk along the Derwent Valley in the Peak District, where the world's industrial revolution was born. En route he visits the sleepy village of Cromford to find out about the work of entrepreneur Richard Arkwright, and stops off at a 900-year-old stately home that has been used as the location for three Jane Eyre films. Brian Blessed also introduces Tony to the joys - and the significance - of the Cromford canal, before this leg of his journey ends in Derby

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on a 45-mile hike through the countryside of the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the area's rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps him relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. From there, he travels up what used to be secret paths to Hever Castle and finds out how the monarch's reign brought not only huge social, political and industrial change, but also fame and disaster to the women who caught his eye He ends this leg of his journey in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions
