programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Voiceover artist Nick, 62, is set up on a date with 52-year-old golfer Laura, and 22-year-old Jordan, a boxer for Team Great Britain, meets 23-year-old Thai boxer Chloe. Elsewhere, telecoms analyst Ketan, who has high-functioning autism and has been reluctant to reveal it to any potential partner, goes on a date with Brodie

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, critiquing The Outlaws, Family Fortunes, You, Celebrity Yorkshire Auction House and The Mating Game. They also offer their thoughts on ITV News of COP26 and other stories. Narrated by Craig Cash. From November 2021

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Reiki healer and animal communicator Jayne is set up on a date with 44-year-old children's entertainer Stuart. Meanwhile, headstrong parliamentary researcher Cecilia, who's 23, is looking for a man to match her drive and determination, and may have met her match when she meets straight-talking business development manager Viv. Student James, who has a prosthetic leg and has had his confidence knocked from dates in the past, is set up on a date with fellow student Emilie, who is making the brave move into the world of dating

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, 22-year-old transgender woman Amira, and Andy, a rugby player from Coventry, take part

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray coaches Barry for his first high school wrestling match but keeps it a secret from Beverly, who is sure to disapprove of her son taking part in such a dangerous sport. Adam blackmails Erica into waiting in line with him to buy tickets for the new Stars Wars movie

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray gets his moment in the spotlight when he is honoured by his old high school for his basketball prowess. While he and Beverly are away from home, Barry takes advantage of their absence to throw a party to impress his school friends

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: More couples from some of the programme's most dramatic weddings look back on their big days. Rachel and Adam look back on their 1980s Aussie soap wedding, when Adam attempted to recreate the magical moment Jason Donovan married Kylie Minogue in Neighbours

programme.title: Lego Masters Australia
programme.desc: The five remaining teams are each given a base plate representing a typical room in a house - from a kitchen to a bathroom and a bedroom - and have 10 hours to create minifigures living in the land of the giants. The holders of the Big Brick assign the rooms to the teams

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Featuring a variety of cats doing strange things, soldiers stuck in the sand and a dog balancing on a rope

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia
programme.desc: It's the grand finale, and in Kuala Lumpur, the top three teams are put to the ultimate test using skills they've perfected during the race so far. They're tasked with recreating a dessert with help from a python, hunting for a treasure map in a bookstore, and making their way to the other side of the city by any means possible. The duos must also recall locations from the places they've raced through, before a pit stop finish like never before

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With a fractious atmosphere in the air, the couples head off for a weekend retreat. And there are big questions looming for all of the newlyweds

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: As the retreat winds down, one couple make amends while others begin planning for the future. However, a serious roadblock forces one pair to confront deep issues and question whether their marriage can last the distance

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With Decision Day looming, the couples prepare for the biggest call of their lives, questioning love, trust and authenticity in the process. One wife is blindsided by life-changing news

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog (2020)
programme.desc: Sonic, a blue hedgehog who can run at supersonic speeds, is transported to Earth. A decade later, while enjoying life in Montana, his speedy skills create an electromagnetic pulse. Scientific genius Dr Robotnik is hired to investigate, and becomes obsessed with capturing the creature for his power. Live-action fantasy adventure based on the video games, starring James Marsden and Jim Carrey, with the voice of Ben Schwartz

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa becomes insecure about her size after she and Bart contract a bizarre infection that requires treatment from steroids that cause temporary weight gain

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Portrait of a Lackey on Fire
programme.desc: Homer offers to play matchmaker for Smithers and introduces him to jetsetting fashion designer mogul Michael de Graaf

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A teenage Homer embarks on a road trip in the company of Grampa to look for his mother

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge select Fat Tony to be Maggie's godfather, but are worried about his influence on her

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Mr Burns fight for the soul of brash young football prodigy Grayson Mathers

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a union representative at the power plant and immediately uses his new-found authority to save the company's free dental plan and avoid having to pay for Lisa's new braces. Featuring the voice of Dr Joyce Brothers

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: So It's Come to This: A Simpsons Clip Show
programme.desc: Bart's latest prank wrecks the house and lands Homer in hospital, where he and the family reminisce about all the other injuries and mishaps he has suffered in his eventful and disaster-filled life

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A particularly bad episode of Itchy & Scratchy prompts Bart and Lisa to write their own script and send it to the show's producer under Grampa's name. With the voice of Brooke Shields

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tries to persuade her family not to take part in the town's annual snake-clubbing ritual, but her words fall on deaf ears as Homer anticipates the event with glee. With the guest voice of Barry White

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Long-standing TV personality Krusty the Clown reaches the end of the road, his days of entertaining kids quashed by an alternative show - prompting his famous friends to come to the rescue. Animation, with the voices of Elizabeth Taylor, Hugh Hefner, Bette Midler and the Red Hot Chili Peppers

programme.title: Moonfall (2022)
programme.desc: A mysterious force knocks the Moon from its orbit and sends the Earth's only natural satellite on a collision course with our planet. Astronauts Jocinda Fowler and Brian Harper, who served together on an ill-fated space mission, join forces with conspiracy theorist KC Houseman to avert catastrophe. Director Roland Emmerich's sci-fi thriller, starring Patrick Wilson, Halle Berry, John Bradley and Donald Sutherland in one of his final films

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a farm restaurant in New York, a fishing-shop-turned eatery in California, and a Mexican diner with tuna tacos in Ohio

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares the good stuff with the chocolate lovers in her life, starting with a triple chocolate tiramisu, and crispy chocolate dippers to use in a chocolate shake

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares money-saving ideas for transforming random cuts of beef into four delicious dishes, including slow cooker Sunday stew, and teriyaki beef stir-fry

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes four recipes lighter and healthier in 16 minutes, including chicken parmesan, Asian noodle salad, taco skillet and lighter fried pork chops with white beans and spinach

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree spends time with her son, Bryce, making beef fajita nachos, white chicken chili for dinner and orange-vanilla monkey bread as a breakfast treat

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond welcomes a new cowboy to the ranch with a frontier-style dinner kit of his favourite food, including short rib sandwiches, and salted caramel frosted brownies

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree cooks up tuna noodle casserole, meatballs in sweet and sour sauce, glazed apple dumplings and Welsh rarebit

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue is joined by chef Tommy Banks, who owns two Michelin-starred restaurants in North Yorkshire. He cooks home-reared hogget with faggots, greens and pesto. Prue makes two-tone soups and galette with goat curd and roasted tomatoes, and reveals a handy tip for making cakes with an unexpected ingredient

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver rustles up a frugal paella with chicken thighs, spicy chorizo and prawns, and uses leftovers from a Sunday roast pork to make midweek crispy Mexican tacos and French Vietnamese banh mi. Plus, Sicilian-inspired meatballs and tomato 'Norma' sauce and a twist on beans on toast with broad beans and peas from the freezer. The chef also demonstrates how to reap the benefits of a well-stocked store cupboard

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver prepares a thrifty curry using chicken drumsticks bought for half the price of breast, but full of flavour. He also makes a Mexican omelette with a guacamole-style slaw, a giant vegetable rosti with poached eggs and crispy Cajun fishcakes, and 'snake in the hole', a cross between a roasted meatloaf and meatballs with Yorkshire pudding

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James goes on a culinary journey around the UK, with fellow chef and friend Galton Blackiston joining him as he begins in the Isles of Scilly. The pair don diving suits to swim with seals and hire an unusual mode of transport to take them around the islands

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Galton Blackiston for a tour of Cornwall. They sample some world-class ice cream before visiting Newlyn fish market to buy cod, which James cooks with oysters and tartare sauce. The trip concludes with a visit to Sennen beach, where they practice their oil painting and break out the barbecue

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel Allen shows viewers how to get the most out of their shopping budgets and ingredients, using recipes designed to make money and food go further. She begins with a visit to her local market, where she gathers both inspiration and produce for a variety of dishes. Back in the kitchen, Rachel cooks using potatoes and Dublin Bay prawns

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel Allen cooks at home for her family, and adds a new and delicious twist to everyone's favourite - roast chicken. Any left-overs are put to good use in a summery salad, followed by a dessert of iced strawberry meringue cake, and she reveals ways in which to impress guests

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: The chef rustles up food for hot sunny weather, including a nutty quinoa salad, a potato, smoked salmon and dill tart and a spinach, bacon and Gruyere frittata. Plus, more tricks of the trade from Rachel's school kitchen and a glimpse of her working life as she visits London

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: The chef conjures up a slow recipe using lamb, and a plum pie

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: The chef conjures up meals that are suitable for the palates of younger eaters. Recipes include cheesy chorizo dough balls and cookies and cream cheesecake

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: The chef offers meal ideas for those who work from home, including a roasted butternut squash salad with flatbreads, Swedish cardamom cookies, and red lentil soup with cumin and coriander. Rachel also offers an insight into her own working life and there are more skills to be learnt in the cookery school kitchen

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: The chef explores the advantages of cooking with home-grown ingredients, preparing honey-roast duck with red cabbage, duck spring rolls with plum sauce and sesame pak choi, and raspberry upside-down cake. She also offers further hints and tips in the cookery school kitchen

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: The chef prepares a series of recipes designed to provide a boost of energy, including smoked salmon and lemon risotto with asparagus and easy arancini with creme fraiche herb sauce. She also demonstrates how to rustle up an indulgent dessert of chocolate mousse with salted caramel sauce and shortbread biscuits

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Following the chef on a day out with her son, before joining her in the kitchen for hearty steak fajitas, classic cheesy meatballs with spaghetti, and a satisfying butterscotch apple pudding. Rachel also shares more secrets, tips and advice from the cookery school kitchen

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark gets a lesson in oyster shucking and James uses Jersey black butter to create a black butter tartlet

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig introduce a special round of the baking challenge in which contestants must create confectionery inspired by the Roaring Twenties. The signature challenge involves a decorative twist on custard pies, while the technical round will be a test of the bakers' timing and consistency. Finally, for the Shows, they must create a three-tiered masterpiece inspired by their tipple of choice. Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith are on hand to assess the results of their efforts and decide who will be leaving the tent

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host dessert week as the baking contest crosses the half-way point. The signature challenge is an elegant and challenging bake with a lot of delicate layers, while the technical round will test the bakers' ability to pull off delicate assembly and precise decoration. Finally, for the showstopper, they must create a spherical celebration cake. Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood are on hand to taste the results - and decide who will be crowned Star Baker and who will be leaving the tent this week

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host a special festival-themed round of the culinary competition. The episode kicks off with a signature challenge in which the bakers must draw inspiration from celebrations across the world. Moving on to the technical round, the contestants find the makings of a classic deep-fried Italian dish lurking beneath the mysterious gingham cloths. Finally, the Showstopper puts the bakers' precision and creativity to the test before judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith decide who will be crowned StarBaker

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host as the remaining bakers' knowledge of pastry is tested. The Signature challenge is a savoury take on a French classic, there's a multi-layered Moroccan Technical, and a towering Showstopper that requires not only an expertise with pastry, but also a solid grasp of structural engineering. With a place in the semi-final at stake, who will win the coveted Star Baker title from judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood?

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef embarks on a culinary journey through Spain, discovering the history and culture of the country's diverse regions through food. He starts out in San Sebastian, where he tries whole grilled turbot, samples local cider and shops at the fish market. Along the way he cooks hake, lamb, Basque stew and pintxos

programme.title: The Bounty (1984)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the mutiny on board HMS Bounty against Commanding Lieutenant William Bligh. As naval discipline breaks down while docked in Tahiti, the commander resorts to harsh measures to keep the crew in line. However, his seamen respond to his brutality with insurrection, and Bligh and his loyalists are cast adrift in a lifeboat, while the mutineers go in search of a haven safe from retribution. With Mel Gibson, Anthony Hopkins and Laurence Olivier

If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Plans to take the legendary Liberty Bell on a tour of America are thwarted by an unbalanced scientist threatening to destroy it

programme.desc: Kuroda asks Steve to join a search for a mysterious youth reported to be living with wolves. Sci-fi drama, starring Lee Majors

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: Shalako (1968)
programme.desc: A party of European aristocrats on a hunting trip in New Mexico falls foul of marauding Apaches and the treachery of a guide, who abandons the group and makes off with their possessions. However, a US army scout knows of their presence in the territory and sets out to the rescue. Western, with Sean Connery, Brigitte Bardot, Jack Hawkins, Honor Blackman, Woody Strode, Peter Van Eyck and Eric Sykes

programme.title: The Gambler, the Girl and the Gunslinger (2009)
programme.desc: A ranch owner loses half his home in a poker game, meaning he now has to share the property with his rival gambler. The pair struggle to cope with living and working together, and when they both take a liking to the same woman, the conflict only gets worse. Comedy Western, starring Dean Cain, James Tupper and Allison Hossack

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A dancer confined to a hospital bed has a recurring dream about Room 22. Guest starring Barbara Nichols and Fred Wayne

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: The crew of a jetliner en route to New York start travelling backward and forward in time

programme.title: Uncommon Valor (1983)
programme.desc: A retired American colonel, frustrated by a decade of wading through red tape, takes the law into his own hands and recruits an elite team of Vietnam veterans to rescue his son from a PoW camp in Laos. Action adventure, starring Gene Hackman, Patrick Swayze, Robert Stack and Fred Ward

programme.title: Drop Zone (1994)
programme.desc: Federal marshal Pete Nessip escorts a convicted computer hacker to a new facility. Events soon spiral out of control and the convict escapes, killing Pete's brother on the way. The tough cop teams up with a professional skydiver to capture the fugitive and avenge his sibling's death. Action thriller, starring Wesley Snipes, Gary Busey, Yancy Butler and Michael Jeter

programme.title: Lucky Number Slevin (2006)
programme.desc: A case of mistaken identity plunges an ordinary man into the middle of a war between crime syndicates. With no means of proving who he really is, he finds himself forced to pay off a friend's debt to an underworld kingpin by assassinating a rival mobster. Thriller, starring Josh Hartnett, Bruce Willis, Lucy Liu, Morgan Freeman and Ben Kingsley

programme.title: A Reason to Live, A Reason to Die (1972)
programme.desc: During the American Civil War, a Union officer faces disgrace for handing over a fort to the Confederates. Determined to regain his honour, he rescues seven men condemned to death and offers them a stay of execution if they help him retake the fort. Spaghetti Western, starring James Coburn and Telly Savalas

programme.title: T.J. Hooker

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Stacy and Hooker search for a vice girl who hold the key to posthumously clearing a murdered officer accused of taking bribes. Eighties police drama, starring William Shatner and Adrian Zmed

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Former partner Jim Cody asks Hooker to help him crack a drug-related murder, only to find the case has unfortunate personal repercussions. William Shatner and Jim Brown star

programme.title: T.J. Hooker

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Stacy poses as a high-class fashion model to catch the killer responsible for murdering two catwalk queens. Heather Locklear and William Shatner star

programme.title: MegaFault (2009)

A seismologist teams up with an explosives expert to prevent the world being completely destroyed by the quakes. Disaster movie, starring Brittany Murphy, Eriq La Salle and Bruce Davison

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: The War Lord (1965)
programme.desc: An 11th-century Norman knight is sent by the duke he serves to defend a druid village. Permitted under feudal law, he takes another man's wife, who falls in love with him and has no intention of returning to her husband. However, her vengeful spouse is outraged and ignites a civil war by influencing the knight's brother to turn against him. Medieval drama, with Charlton Heston, Rosemary Forsyth, Richard Boone and Maurice Evans

programme.title: The Curse of the Werewolf (1961)
programme.desc: An orphan turns into a hairy horror when the moon is full and spreads terror in a Spanish town. Hammer horror, starring Oliver Reed, Clifford Evans, Catherine Feller and Anthony Dawson

programme.title: Night of the Big Heat (1967)
programme.desc: Invaders from outer space take over a remote Scottish island and raise the temperature to an unbearable level, giving residents little or no chance of survival. Sci-fi adventure, starring Christopher Lee, Peter Cushing and Patrick Allen

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A timid little man is given superhuman strength by a two-headed scientist from Mars

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A man dusts off his ancient radio, only to find it mysteriously broadcasts programmes from 20 years ago

programme.title: The International (2009)
programme.desc: An Interpol agent and a district attorney discover a major international bank is involved in a vast range of illegal activities, from insider trading to arms deals and bringing down governments. As they battle to expose the truth, they find the corrupt organisation not only has the law on its side, but will kill to keep the secrets safe. Thriller, starring Clive Owen, Naomi Watts and Armin Mueller-Stahl.

programme.title: Blitz (2011)
programme.desc: An uncompromising police detective's career is under threat when his violent methods are exposed by a reporter. However, he is kept on duty as he is considered the only man capable of stopping an elusive serial killer, one who is preying on police officers. Crime thriller, starring Jason Statham, Aidan Gillen, Paddy Considine and David Morrissey

programme.title: Drive (2011)
programme.desc: A stuntman who moonlights as a getaway driver tries to protect the wife and child of an imprisoned criminal. Upon his release, the convict is forced to rob a pawnshop to pay off his gangland debts, with the driver offering his assistance. However, the job goes awry and the driver is drawn into a conflict with the Mafia. Crime drama, with Ryan Gosling, Carey Mulligan and Bryan Cranston

programme.title: Robin Hood: The Rebellion (2018)
programme.desc: With his true love captured by the villainous Sheriff of Nottingham, the legendary Robin Hood and his crew of outlaws execute a daring rescue to save her. Action adventure, starring Ben Freeman, Brian Blessed, Martyn Ford and Kristian Nairn

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics and the air ambulance rush to save an elderly man who has life-changing injuries following a fall from a ladder. At the same time in Birmingham, a newly qualified paramedic struggles to treat a man who has had three major epileptic fits and is in a delirious and combative state. In Coventry, paramedics Neil Vann and Laura Perkins help a young girl who has passed out and needs urgent medical attention

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A drunk man kicks off at the paramedics in the back of the ambulance. A taxi driver is cut out of his vehicle after a road traffic collision. Back pain leaves a landscape gardener unable to get up off the floor. A 21-year-old woman who hasn't passed urine in over 20 hours is complaining of severe pain in her kidneys, and the construction of a summer house goes badly wrong when sheets of plywood land on the builder's head

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors investigate the underlying medical conditions that may have caused a motorist to crash into a lamppost, while the patient's son and his friend recount the influence she has had on both their lives. A man who fell during the night is treated for possible head trauma, and the father of a three-year-old patient reveals how his stepfather inspired him to be the best parent he can be

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping critics' penultimate stop is at Mynydd Derwydd, a wild camping site on the edge of Clocaenog Forest in south-west Wales. With 250 acres of stunning scenery to explore, but next to no facilities, campsite owner Llyr tries to win them over with a bushcraft skills workshop, a lamb barbecue and a breakfast delivery of fresh farm produce

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by husband and wife Ken and Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sandy and Andy dream of eventually retiring to the lakes and mountains of Lazio in Italy, but for now want to use a property there to share holidays with their sizable family of five children and six grandchildren. Danni Menzies works with a budget of £70,000 to hunt out a traditional-style house with plenty of space and a big enough garden to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Brannie and Paul are planning a permanent move to Portugal, and are seeking a three-bed house that offers plenty of privacy but is still walkable to amenities. With a budget of £225,000, Danni Menzies sets out to find the pair the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Eco-enthusiasts Karen and Peter from Liverpool can't wait to find their dream Spanish holiday home in the stunning region of Tarragona in Catalonia. They want a property that allows them to live off-grid, with enough space to accommodate their planned yoga retreat. Danni Menzies lines up five tempting options for their budget of £180,000, hoping that at least one will meet the couple's needs

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kent couple Alison and Lee are making a permanent move to the stunning countryside of Albox in Almería. They need a garden that's secure enough for their four dogs to roam safely, and the property must be big enough to host their six grown-up children and three grandchildren. Ben Hillman lines up some impressive properties for their budget of £150,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty teacher Ashleigh throws the second of the week's parties in Co Durham, she hopes a menu of sophisticated dishes - along with a helping of hearts and love - will help her win the cash prize. However, cracks were already beginning to show in the diners' genial facade yesterday, and the hostess realises she must play peacemaker if she is to keep everyone from falling out

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Call centre complaints officer and part-time farmer Stephen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham. He hopes to win the £1,000 prize money by impressing his guests with an evening on his farm, featuring a drinks reception and rustic cuisine. However, self-confessed snob Tina makes it perfectly clear what she thinks of his efforts

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Tina is the fourth host in Co Durham. She hopes to impress her guests with a sophisticated French menu including salmon crepes, chicken and posh peas and pear dessert. But having been highly critical of everyone else's food so far, will they now take the chance to get their own back or can she win them round?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final evening of gatherings in Co Durham sees IT manager David stage a French-themed soiree in the hope of securing the £1,000 prize. However, he soon finds his main challenge is to keep all his guests on friendly terms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Married couple Roy and Brian kick this competition off at the Plough Inn in Ripple in Kent. They're hoping their breakfast, which is 'somewhere between Little Chef and The Ritz', will have them onto a winner. However, their chances take an immediate dent when guests Simone and Anke find an old bathmat. Can a wet and wild afternoon of seal spotting on the River Stour help the hosts turn things around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers head to Faringdon in Oxfordshire, where best friends Simone and Anke host the week's second visit at The Old Crown Coaching Inn. Roy and Brian find the furnishings fit for a king, but some of the guests find the cleanliness standards to be less-than sparkling. In the afternoon, a trip up a historic tower proves a step too far for Brian. Over a well-received breakfast the next morning, two of the guests ramp up expectations for their own visit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's third visit takes the B&Bers to Great Bromley in Essex, where married couple Richard and Colleen host at their B&B, The Courthouse. The hosts reckon they're onto a winner with their breakfast, but it's the extra touches in the rooms that really get the B&Bers singing their praises. Tensions are released during a trip to a rage room in nearby Colchester, before Richard gets teary during feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to Samsara Retreat in Kent, where Lori hosts with her friend Mike. Lori hopes to provide a relaxing, re-energising stay, and sets about giving her guests an immediate taste of what her place is all about, with a yoga, meditation and gong bath session. However, come the next morning, some of their guests aren't feeling particularly rested

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers sharpen their metaphorical knives ready for Payment Day. The Plough Inn want to get to the bottom of their cleanliness comments, before Simone and Anke try to clean up Roy and Brian's marks about their mattress. Richard from The Courthouse is brought to tears by some feedback, before Lori and Mike put their sleep scores under the spotlight. After some heated debate, the payments are revealed and the winners announced

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode follows the stories of people who have lived through tragic, life-changing events, and explores how these individuals endure with the strength and support of their loved ones. An 86-year-old man is rushed to St Georges by paramedics after a suspected stroke, while an 80-year-old woman is seen by doctors after falling and sustaining an open fracture to her leg. On the paediatric ward, a mother has brought her 14-year-old daughter to see a doctor after she hurt her ankle during gymnastics

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The story of 22-year-old police officer Hazel, who is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics Jas Panesar and Amy Cullen rush to a woman who has had a severe allergic reaction at a restaurant. In Birmingham, paramedics Naomi Whatley and Mark McDonagh attend to a man who has fallen unconscious after a severe diabetic attac, while in Warwick, paramedics treat a man who has collapsed with a suspected heart attack

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode follows the stories of people who have lived through tragic, life-changing events, and explores how these individuals endure with the strength and support of their loved ones. An 86-year-old man is rushed to St Georges by paramedics after a suspected stroke, while an 80-year-old woman is seen by doctors after falling and sustaining an open fracture to her leg. On the paediatric ward, a mother has brought her 14-year-old daughter to see a doctor after she hurt her ankle during gymnastics

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Ending their tour of north Wales in search of the perfect pitch, the seasoned campers arrive at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey. Marine biologist-turned-campsite host Shaun welcomes the campers with freshly caught oysters and his own oyster gin, before sending them on an aquatic tour of the nearby Puffin Island. The week's winner of the coveted plaque and £2,000 cash prize is then announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition visits the Nottingham and Derby area. American Hali kicks proceeding off with a travel-inspired menu, including Moroccan chicken cigars and an unusual dessert combination. With a very gregarious gathering of guests, the food plays second fiddle to a fun night of impressions, dance moves and kitchen chaos

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition continues in the Nottingham and Derby area. Car salesman Alistair hopes to step the competition up a gear. However, after narrowly avoiding an argument last night with Lesley, the veteran of the group, he's treading on thin ice. And he's serving up a bizarre menu, including chilli soup - for dessert - and prunes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottingham, fun-loving funeral arranger Kim hosts an evening packed with games galore and a mixed bag menu. Fifty-seven-year-old Lesley is again the butt of car salesman Alistair's geriatric jibes and takes great delight in watching him put on the spot during a dance-off

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Customer relations associate and part-time drag queen Adam plans a theatrical night of flamboyance as the culinary competition continues in the Derby and Nottingham area. Can he get his guests begging for an encore, will Alastair and Lesley ever kiss and make up and what will Adam's guests think of his secret hobby as a ufologist?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final soiree in the Nottingham and Derby area sees 57-year-old Lesley counting on a night of glamour and glitz - plus some good home-cooked food - to secure the £1,000 prize. However, before any food is even dished up, the guests make a surprising discovery about the host

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Poppy and her father Philip realise their dream of finding a holiday home in inland Andalucía that all the family can use to enjoy the Spanish sunshine. Utilising the pair's budget of £50,000, Danni lines up five fantastic options to show them

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Engaged couple Ray and Ali seek their dream holiday home in northern Tuscany. Now that Ray is retiring, they're looking to spend time cycling and exploring the great outdoors, as well as enjoying Italian food and wine. The couple have a £180,000 budget to buy a house that's ideally detached, with a large garden and a pool, so call upon the services of property expert Sara Damergi to locate five potential properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Myra and Eric from Glasgow have been holidaying in Portugal for over 20 years, and of all the places they've been, nowhere else has drawn them back year after year like the Silver Coast. Their dream is to spend their retirement in the sunshine. Ben Hillman helps the couple find a suitable property within their budget of £300,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Rick and Inge cannot wait to fulfill their dream of buying a holiday home in Tala in western Cyprus, near the beautiful beaches of Coral Bay. The cat-crazy couple ask Jean Johansson to help them find a detached villa for their budget of £260,000, with plenty of space for a feline family and a 'man cave' for Rick's DIY

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Derek and Jane from Lancashire yearn to own a dream property in Chiclana de la Frontera where they can enjoy their very own bit of Spanish sunshine. For their budget of £220,000, they want a property with at least four bedrooms to accommodate all of their grown-up children, plus a private pool. Danni Menzies sets out deliver a splash by delivering the right property

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Tuck family in Long Eaton, Derbyshire, who have grown weary of their cramped, three bedroom semi-detached home. Kirstie offers her thoughts on ways the family could change the downstairs of the property, while Phil sets out to find a suitable house they could buy instead

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The architect is in Stratford-upon-Avon, where he helps Laura and Jonnie to transform their Edwardian mock Tudor house into a modern family home. George also meets Nicki, whose 1920s cottage in Essex needs major changes to make it more accessible

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week begins at the Dog Inn in Longridge, Lancashire, where married couple Stu and Ali hope to impress their guests with a trip to a farm to see some baby lambs. However, an unwelcome discovery during breakfast could throw a spanner in the works

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Ullswater View in the Lake District, where Julie and her friend Sarah plan to give the B&Bers a touch of luxury. The group brave wet, windy conditions during a boat trip on the lake, and vibrant views from the breakfast room boost everyone's spirits. Until a sausage causes a problem

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is at String of Horses in Cumbria where hosts Sue and Judith hope their guests enjoy the community feel of the place

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to East Lothian, where husband and wife Gavin and Rosie host at Cove Farm. They're hoping to persuade their guests of the merits of glamping, convincing their guests to embrace the great outdoors with a wood-fired hot tub. However, tensions rise during a trip to a local brewery - and they're not eased by a complaint about the sleeping situation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day finds the B&Bers trying to get their heads around all manner of things, from facilities faults to shoddy sleep scores. After a tension-filled visit last time around, there are plenty of scores to be settled, but whoever will reign supreme when the payments are revealed is anything but certain

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Emma fears for a cyclist who has been trapped under a tram for 15 minutes after a car clipped her wheel and launched her over the handlebars of her bike. A 21-year-old man with a history of sinus issues is bleeding profusely from his nose and the back of his throat, and doctors David and Michael treat a 34-year-old motorbike rider with a badly smashed-up knee

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 74-year-old man is brought in after being found in a confused state by his son. Doctors discover signs of a brain injury, leading his children to reveal the tragedy that befell their father and the shattering effect it has had on his life. A woman who fell down the stairs at her granddaughter's 16th birthday is also in need of treatment, and her daughter is reminded of a phone call that changed their lives forever. Meanwhile, in paediatrics, a talkative nine-year-old is treated for a rash on his ankle

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Birmingham try to stop hypothermia from setting in when a woman collapses in the snow. At the same time, Mark and Adi attend to a woman whose waters may have broken at 27 weeks and could be hours away from giving birth. In Coventry, crewmates Neil and Laura treat an elderly man who has a life-threatening infection

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Emma fears for a cyclist who has been trapped under a tram for 15 minutes after a car clipped her wheel and launched her over the handlebars of her bike. A 21-year-old man with a history of sinus issues is bleeding profusely from his nose and the back of his throat, and doctors David and Michael treat a 34-year-old motorbike rider with a badly smashed-up knee

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 74-year-old man is brought in after being found in a confused state by his son. Doctors discover signs of a brain injury, leading his children to reveal the tragedy that befell their father and the shattering effect it has had on his life. A woman who fell down the stairs at her granddaughter's 16th birthday is also in need of treatment, and her daughter is reminded of a phone call that changed their lives forever. Meanwhile, in paediatrics, a talkative nine-year-old is treated for a rash on his ankle

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The story of 22-year-old police officer Hazel, who is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team head for the Vegan Aisle for all the news on animal-free food. Helen Lawal visits Tesco to find out how they keep up with all the plant-based burger trend, John Whaite seeks out the secret to the snap in a vegan sausage skin, Kate Quilton finds out how exactly you milk an oat, and Briony May Williams learns how to make a quiche without egg

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics Jas Panesar and Amy Cullen rush to a woman who has had a severe allergic reaction at a restaurant. In Birmingham, paramedics Naomi Whatley and Mark McDonagh attend to a man who has fallen unconscious after a severe diabetic attac, while in Warwick, paramedics treat a man who has collapsed with a suspected heart attack

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors investigate the underlying medical conditions that may have caused a motorist to crash into a lamppost, while the patient's son and his friend recount the influence she has had on both their lives. A man who fell during the night is treated for possible head trauma, and the father of a three-year-old patient reveals how his stepfather inspired him to be the best parent he can be

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping critics' penultimate stop is at Mynydd Derwydd, a wild camping site on the edge of Clocaenog Forest in south-west Wales. With 250 acres of stunning scenery to explore, but next to no facilities, campsite owner Llyr tries to win them over with a bushcraft skills workshop, a lamb barbecue and a breakfast delivery of fresh farm produce

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by husband and wife Ken and Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sandy and Andy dream of eventually retiring to the lakes and mountains of Lazio in Italy, but for now want to use a property there to share holidays with their sizable family of five children and six grandchildren. Danni Menzies works with a budget of £70,000 to hunt out a traditional-style house with plenty of space and a big enough garden to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Brannie and Paul are planning a permanent move to Portugal, and are seeking a three-bed house that offers plenty of privacy but is still walkable to amenities. With a budget of £225,000, Danni Menzies sets out to find the pair the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Eco-enthusiasts Karen and Peter from Liverpool can't wait to find their dream Spanish holiday home in the stunning region of Tarragona in Catalonia. They want a property that allows them to live off-grid, with enough space to accommodate their planned yoga retreat. Danni Menzies lines up five tempting options for their budget of £180,000, hoping that at least one will meet the couple's needs

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kent couple Alison and Lee are making a permanent move to the stunning countryside of Albox in Almería. They need a garden that's secure enough for their four dogs to roam safely, and the property must be big enough to host their six grown-up children and three grandchildren. Ben Hillman lines up some impressive properties for their budget of £150,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty teacher Ashleigh throws the second of the week's parties in Co Durham, she hopes a menu of sophisticated dishes - along with a helping of hearts and love - will help her win the cash prize. However, cracks were already beginning to show in the diners' genial facade yesterday, and the hostess realises she must play peacemaker if she is to keep everyone from falling out

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Call centre complaints officer and part-time farmer Stephen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham. He hopes to win the £1,000 prize money by impressing his guests with an evening on his farm, featuring a drinks reception and rustic cuisine. However, self-confessed snob Tina makes it perfectly clear what she thinks of his efforts

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Tina is the fourth host in Co Durham. She hopes to impress her guests with a sophisticated French menu including salmon crepes, chicken and posh peas and pear dessert. But having been highly critical of everyone else's food so far, will they now take the chance to get their own back or can she win them round?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final evening of gatherings in Co Durham sees IT manager David stage a French-themed soiree in the hope of securing the £1,000 prize. However, he soon finds his main challenge is to keep all his guests on friendly terms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Married couple Roy and Brian kick this competition off at the Plough Inn in Ripple in Kent. They're hoping their breakfast, which is 'somewhere between Little Chef and The Ritz', will have them onto a winner. However, their chances take an immediate dent when guests Simone and Anke find an old bathmat. Can a wet and wild afternoon of seal spotting on the River Stour help the hosts turn things around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers head to Faringdon in Oxfordshire, where best friends Simone and Anke host the week's second visit at The Old Crown Coaching Inn. Roy and Brian find the furnishings fit for a king, but some of the guests find the cleanliness standards to be less-than sparkling. In the afternoon, a trip up a historic tower proves a step too far for Brian. Over a well-received breakfast the next morning, two of the guests ramp up expectations for their own visit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's third visit takes the B&Bers to Great Bromley in Essex, where married couple Richard and Colleen host at their B&B, The Courthouse. The hosts reckon they're onto a winner with their breakfast, but it's the extra touches in the rooms that really get the B&Bers singing their praises. Tensions are released during a trip to a rage room in nearby Colchester, before Richard gets teary during feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to Samsara Retreat in Kent, where Lori hosts with her friend Mike. Lori hopes to provide a relaxing, re-energising stay, and sets about giving her guests an immediate taste of what her place is all about, with a yoga, meditation and gong bath session. However, come the next morning, some of their guests aren't feeling particularly rested

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers sharpen their metaphorical knives ready for Payment Day. The Plough Inn want to get to the bottom of their cleanliness comments, before Simone and Anke try to clean up Roy and Brian's marks about their mattress. Richard from The Courthouse is brought to tears by some feedback, before Lori and Mike put their sleep scores under the spotlight. After some heated debate, the payments are revealed and the winners announced

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode follows the stories of people who have lived through tragic, life-changing events, and explores how these individuals endure with the strength and support of their loved ones. An 86-year-old man is rushed to St Georges by paramedics after a suspected stroke, while an 80-year-old woman is seen by doctors after falling and sustaining an open fracture to her leg. On the paediatric ward, a mother has brought her 14-year-old daughter to see a doctor after she hurt her ankle during gymnastics

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics and the air ambulance rush to save an elderly man who has life-changing injuries following a fall from a ladder. At the same time in Birmingham, a newly qualified paramedic struggles to treat a man who has had three major epileptic fits and is in a delirious and combative state. In Coventry, paramedics Neil Vann and Laura Perkins help a young girl who has passed out and needs urgent medical attention

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Emma fears for a cyclist who has been trapped under a tram for 15 minutes after a car clipped her wheel and launched her over the handlebars of her bike. A 21-year-old man with a history of sinus issues is bleeding profusely from his nose and the back of his throat, and doctors David and Michael treat a 34-year-old motorbike rider with a badly smashed-up knee

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Ending their tour of north Wales in search of the perfect pitch, the seasoned campers arrive at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey. Marine biologist-turned-campsite host Shaun welcomes the campers with freshly caught oysters and his own oyster gin, before sending them on an aquatic tour of the nearby Puffin Island. The week's winner of the coveted plaque and £2,000 cash prize is then announced

programme.desc: Air ambulance crews respond to headteacher Andy who has collapsed after a cardiac arrest in Essex and warehouse employee Rob who is trapped under a pallet of granite in Ipswich. Katie, who is 13-weeks pregnant, calls for help when she keeps having seizures and the Essex and Herts Air Ambulance crew help Jo who has been lying on the bathroom floor with a broken ankle for nine hours

programme.title: Bar Rescue
programme.desc: Jon Taffer sends chef Jason Santos to Chesterfield, Michigan to rescue an owner who drinks excessively in his own bar while his kids try to pick up his slack

programme.title: Catfish UK
programme.desc: Oobah is joined by Catfish US royalty Kamie to help Hayley finally meet Alex, after five months of avoiding phone calls and cancelling on her

programme.title: Help I Sexted My Boss

programme.desc: Two British soldiers in 19th-century India travel to an unexplored land, where one of them fools the natives into believing he is a god. However, his growing delusions of grandeur put him at loggerheads with his comrade-in-arms. Adventure based on the Rudyard Kipling story, directed by John Huston, starring Sean Connery, Michael Caine, Saeed Jaffrey, Christopher Plummer and Shakira Caine.

programme.title: The Name of the Rose (1986)
programme.desc: Medieval friar William of Baskerville investigates a series of murders at a remote monastery. While the monks are quick to declare the deaths the work of the Devil, William is convinced a human killer is at large, and sets out to expose the truth - a mission that brings him into conflict with the Inquisition. Mystery based on Umberto Eco's novel, starring Sean Connery, Christian Slater and F Murray Abraham.

programme.desc: Great Slaughter's twinning with a German town is met with animosity by some members of the twinning committee and townsfolk. Karl Fischer, the German mayor, with his wife Elsa and a group of German soldiers led by General Udo Von Stark arrive for the ceremony. programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries

programme.title: RTÉ News: One O'Clock and Weather
The Good Ship Murder
programme.desc: When the ship docks at his friend and crewmate Jamil's hometown of Casablanca, singer Jack volunteers to moonlight as a tour guide and invites himself for dinner with Jamil's family. But the sun-drenched alleys and markets hide sinister secrets, and soon a charity helping street kids is mired in scandal and a murder has been committed. Crime drama, starring Shayne Ward and Catherine Tyldesley

programme.title: Masters: Keepers of Tradition
programme.desc: The work of thatcher Marika Leen. She came from the Netherlands as a teenager, climbed onto a leaking roof in rural Galway on the instructions of three old bachelors below, and never really came down. Forty-five years later, she is one of Ireland's finest thatchers. In this edition, Marika returns to Inis Oírr to re-thatch the last thatched cottage on the island

programme.title: Ireland in Music
programme.desc: The return of the programme featuring mixture of traditional folk songs and original material performed by Irish musicians at notable locations around the country. programme.title: Ice Station Zebra (1968)
programme.desc: An American atomic submarine commander races against time to reach a Soviet spy satellite that has crash-landed near a British research station in the Arctic - before Russian troops arrive to stake their claim to it. Adventure based on Alistair MacLean's novel, starring Rock Hudson, Ernest Borgnine, Jim Brown, Tony Bill and Patrick McGoohan

programme.title: Wild Conamara
programme.desc: Part two of two. Eoin Warner heads inland on the second stage of his wild journey and finds one of the last remaining pockets of Atlantic Rainforest in Conamara - Derryclare woods. The woods are home to pine martens, red squirrels, and the newly arrived woodpecker. Crossing vast boglands, Eoin goes in search of lost birds, before heading to the highest of the 12 Bens, Binn Bhán

programme.title: Keys to My Life
programme.desc: Veteran broadcaster Gloria Hunniford takes Brendan Courtney from her humble beginnings in Portadown to the idyllic Dalkey apartment she shared with her husband Stephen who died in 2024

programme.title: Fair City
programme.desc: Fergal pressures Cass to reveal who told the council, but Nikki panics when Jacinta finds out. Mondo struggles to find a grand gesture to celebrate Kira's birthday. Babs hits the ground running on her first official day as a Garda, and Dean and Max get competitive, upsetting the team spirit

programme.title: The Museum of Me
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Maia Dunphy takes on the role of curator to tell her own story, using personal objects that have stayed with her over the years. She charts the experiences that have shaped her wit, honesty and unmistakable voice

programme.title: Book Club: The Next Chapter (2023)
programme.desc: Four best friends embark on a journey as they take their book club to Italy for the fun girls trip they never had. Romantic comedy, starring Diane Keaton and Jane Fonda

programme.title: Joan
programme.desc: Boisie and Joan buy an engagement ring while the police search for the missing Stubbs painting at Boisie's store, but find nothing and Joan is adamant that they have to get rid of the artwork and go straight. As the law closes in, Boisie struggles to find a buyer for the painting and turns to Albie for help, while Joan is dreaming of her own business.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: As Benjamin continues his search for Gloria, Nikki makes a disturbing discovery that brings them closer to the truth. Plus, a new team member joins the Lyell and Lauren and Gary find themselves unknowingly caught up in a scam

programme.title: We Hunt Together
programme.desc: When former child soldier Baba rescues Freddy from a sexual assault, something dark is ignited in them. The pair's lust for each other takes over and creates a truly deadly duo. DS Lola Franks and DI Jackson Mendy get thrown together to investigate the murder of the pair's first victim. Jackson's history of working in police anti-corruption infuriates Lola, so too does his firmly held belief that no one can ever be held entirely responsible for their actions. Crime drama, starring Babou Ceesay and Eve Myles

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk travel around the UK fixing historical treasures, with this edition seeing them visit the Scottish Boat Building School in Irvine, North Ayrshire, to restore a three-piece canoe designed in the 1930s. Will also calls in to Barr Castle in Galston, East Ayrshire, to repair a windowpane with a hand inscription by Robert Burns

programme.title: The Museum of Me

Presented by Dermot Whelan

programme.title: Truelove
programme.desc: Phil and Ken are left reeling by the truth about Marion. Phil must do everything she can to save Ken from ruining the rest of his life, and make amends for a love she let pass her by 50 years earlier. Last episode of the drama, starring Lindsay Duncan, Clarke Peters, Phil Davis, Peter Egan and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: We Hunt Together
programme.desc: When former child soldier Baba rescues Freddy from a sexual assault, something dark is ignited in them. The pair's lust for each other takes over and creates a truly deadly duo. programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final day of the game of treachery and trust. The remaining contenders have survived every banishment and murder in Claudia's castle of treachery, but it all comes down to this day. Will the Faithful weed out all the Traitors and split the prize pot between them, or will any Traitors remain undetected, and take the life-changing sum of money all for themselves?

programme.title: International Football Friendly
programme.desc: Canada v Republic of Ireland (Kick-off 12.45am). Live coverage of the friendly from Saputo Stadium. The Republic are playing their first away game since losing out to Czechia in the FIFA World Cup play-offs against a team preparing for the finals as co-hosts

programme.title: news2day Weekly
programme.desc: The stories making the headlines both at home and abroad during the past seven days

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf's booked an appointment with his local GP following his loss of license. Richie and Abigail go out on their date, both dressed to the nines

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Sonny returns home from physio, but is feeling on the outer. When Tane wakes up, he tells Jo he doesn't want to be with her

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie demands to know why Tane broke up with Jo, Sonny suggests he move back in with Dana, but she doesn't feel it's possible

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr set about renovating the bathrooms. Amanda has big plans converting one of the medieval tower rooms into a double height sumptuous en-suite complete with a Botticelli mural, roll-top bath and a gold resin floor, while Alan has the challenging task of transforming the small-sized bathroom into a minimalist wet room. Things don't get off to the best of starts for Alan as he dismantles the old toilet, while Amanda discovers the ceiling she hoped to take down holds the tower together. To lift their spirits, the duo send time at the picturesque medieval town of San Gimignano

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden meets her match as she aims to turn the dark second bedroom into a country chic boudoir, inspired by the Tuscan sunrise. To help bring her vision to life, local friends Luca and Alessandro invite her and co-renovator Alan Carr to visit their recently renovated 19th-century farmhouse to gather inspiration. Keen to create a piece de resistance, Amanda ropes in Scott to build a bespoke headboard and takes her first steps into the world of upholstery. Meanwhile, Alan secretly signs up to a painting masterclass with local artist Tony to surprise Amanda with a showpiece of his own

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Kate and Rob, who have lived in a 1940s prefab house built by Rob's grandfather. Originally designed to be lived in for just 10 years, this factory-built home is well past its sell-by date. Now, Kate and Rob are at last in a position to replace it with a bespoke, modern, warm home - which will also be built in a factory using volumetric modular construction. Over just six weeks, their new house will be manufactured on a production line, complete with insulation, tiling and kitchen installation

programme.title: UCL Magazine Show
programme.desc: Including reviews of recent Champions League matches, a round-up of the latest news, plus interviews with leading players and coaches

programme.title: The Saturday Game Live
programme.desc: Carlow v Laois (Throw-in 3.45pm). Joanne Cantwell presents coverage of the Joe McDonagh Cup final at Croke Park. Laois are looking to go one better than in each of the last two years when they were runners-up, while Carlow won this competition in 2023

programme.title: The Saturday Game Live
programme.desc: Dublin v Galway (Throw-in 6.00pm). Coverage of the Leinster Senior Hurling Championship final from Croke Park. Dublin were runners-up to Kilkenny when they last reached this final two years ago, and Galway were beaten by the same team in the 2025 showpiece match

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days for Freedom
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. This second of two programmes concludes events, considered day by day, up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of moving testimonies and spectacular colourised archives

programme.title: The Boys in the Boat (2023)
programme.desc: A group of underdogs, at the height of the Great Depression, are thrust into the spotlight, taking on elite rivals from around the world as part of the American rowing team at the 1936 Berlin Summer Olympic Games. Inspirational true-life story, starring Joel Edgerton, Callum Turner and Peter Guinness. Directed by George Clooney

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: It's the penultimate day in the Traitors' castle and, as the game nears the end, the players are all focused on who they can really trust. With time running out to raise the prize pot, the surviving contenders embark on a monumental mission - where one player is faced with an important decision. As they race to the final, will the Faithful rid themselves of a Traitor or let another Faithful fall before the final hurdle? As usual, Claudia Winkleman monitors events

programme.title: Louis Theroux: The Settlers
programme.desc: Fourteen years after his first visit to the West Bank, Louis spends time with the growing community of Israeli religious-nationalist settlers, who since the Hamas-led attacks of October 7. 2023, have been expanding their areas of control by pursuing a campaign of violence against local Palestinian communities. He meets prominent settlers, including the 'godmother' of the movement Daniella Weiss, and travels throughout the territory in an attempt to understand the consequences of their activity

programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Roo's on the hunt for Alf, worried he's driven without his license, and Dana's a little concerned that Sonny moving back into her place will be troublesome for his recovery

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Levi interrogates Abigail after Richie drops her home in the police car. Roo explains to Alf she feels she has to step in - if he's not going to look after himself, someone has to

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Preview
programme.desc: A look ahead to the tournament, which takes place in the USA, Mexico and Canada

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Preview
programme.desc: A look ahead to the tournament, which takes place in the USA, Mexico and Canada

programme.title: The Sunday Game Live
programme.desc: Cork v Limerick (Throw-in 2.00pm). Joanne Cantwell presents all the action from the Munster Senior Hurling Championship final at SuperValu Páirc Uí Chaoimh. These teams also met in the 2025 final, with Cork prevailing to lift this trophy for a record-extending 55th time and end Limerick's run of six consecutive triumphs in the competition

programme.title: Scannal 20 Bliain
programme.desc: Garry Mac Donncha presents a look back at sporting scandals, including Lansdowne Road football riot of 1995 when English hooligans ripped up the stadium, and the controversy that exploded after Thierry Henry's handball in a World Cup play-off in Paris in 2009. With contributions from sports broadcasters Seán Bán Breathnach, Cárthach Bán Breathnach, Gráinne McElwain and Cuán Ó Flatharta

programme.title: Animal Control
programme.desc: Victoria and Patel run into fish and game officers while they're on a call, while there are reports of an aggressive attack dog on the loose

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: A Springfield Summer Christmas for Christmas
programme.desc: Despite disliking festive movies, producer Mary Tannenbaum comes to shoot a Christmas film in Springfield, and Lisa decides to make a behind-the-scenes documentary when Mary stays with her family. Skinner falls in love with the new arrival, but will Tannenbaum choose her fiance instead?

programme.title: Octopussy (1983)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates the mysterious death of a British agent in East Berlin, a mission that leads him to a glamorous, international jewel thief. He discovers one of her criminal cohorts, an Indian gangster, is in league with a crazed Soviet general who plans to heat up the Cold War by detonating a nuclear bomb in an American Air Force base. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore, Maud Adams, Steven Berkoff and Louis Jourdan

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: A look at Mexico's extraordinary animal behaviours and environments. Featuring pygmy owls guiding chicks from their nest during daylight hours, orcas using complex team strategies to hunt dolphins, and orchid bees gathering up to 40 different scent. Cameras also venture into caves that are less explored than the moon

programme.title: The Sunday Game
programme.desc: Jacqui Hurley is joined by guests to present highlights and analysis of the weekend's GAA Championship. Among the contests taking place were Cork v Leinster in the Munster Senior Hurling Championship final, Dublin v Galway in the Leinster Senior Hurling Championship final, and Carlow v Laois in the Joe McDonagh Cup final

programme.title: Shortscreen
programme.sub-title: Jill and Lill Do Knick Knacks
programme.desc: After an innocent game of knick-knacks spirals into unfathomable destruction, two teenage girls learn a hard lesson about the importance of taking responsibility for their actions

programme.title: Changing Ends
programme.desc: A sex education lesson strikes fear into Alan's heart, while an impending testosterone-fuelled school disco gets him all hot and bothered - and not in a good way as Kay is feeling amorous and discussing kisses at every opportunity. programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr set about renovating the bathrooms. Amanda has big plans converting one of the medieval tower rooms into a double height sumptuous en-suite complete with a Botticelli mural, roll-top bath and a gold resin floor, while Alan has the challenging task of transforming the small-sized bathroom into a minimalist wet room. Things don't get off to the best of starts for Alan as he dismantles the old toilet, while Amanda discovers the ceiling she hoped to take down holds the tower together.
