programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job

To help bring her vision to life, local friends Luca and Alessandro invite her and co-renovator Alan Carr to visit their recently renovated 19th-century farmhouse to gather inspiration. Keen to create a piece de resistance, Amanda ropes in Scott to build a bespoke headboard and takes her first steps into the world of upholstery. Meanwhile, Alan secretly signs up to a painting masterclass with local artist Tony to surprise Amanda with a showpiece of his own

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Kate and Rob, who have lived in a 1940s prefab house built by Rob's grandfather. Originally designed to be lived in for just 10 years, this factory-built home is well past its sell-by date. Now, Kate and Rob are at last in a position to replace it with a bespoke, modern, warm home - which will also be built in a factory using volumetric modular construction. Over just six weeks, their new house will be manufactured on a production line, complete with insulation, tiling and kitchen installation

programme.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

programme.title: The Guest Wing

Meanwhile, Sir Benjamin Slade hopes a murder mystery weekend at Maunsel House in Somerset will help pay off a sizeable electricity bill

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capital's love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the city's first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Lara plans a lavish birthday celebration for Axe, while Wendy deliberates her invitation, and Chuck faces external pressure to drop a case

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: The Miniature Wife
programme.desc: Time rewinds to look back on the wedding day of Les and Lindy 20 years earlier, when the couple got a rude awakening at what family life could look like if they followed in their parents' footsteps

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: As Prior digs into the links between Tsalal and mining company Silver Sky, Navarro rescues Leah from a local protest that has turned violent. At a meeting with Silver Sky executve Kate McKitterick, Danvers balks when Connelly shares the official cause of the Tsalal men's deaths - and gets a warning against pursuing the case any further. Later, Hank receives a quid pro quo offer

On the eve of the launch of his revolutionary AI, Dr Alex Hoffman is attacked, but not before receiving an antique book from an anonymous sender. Thriller, starring Josh Hartnett

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: Peggy gets welcomed back to 61st Street by almost everyone, while George informs Oscar of his decision and sends Clay to meet with union leader Mr Henderson at his steel mill

programme.desc: Kip and his friends watch on TV as Scott discusses his public coming out while accepting the MVP award. Shane drives to his cottage with Ilya, who shares the trauma of his mother's death by suicide

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: A medical emergency forces Sabi to go to the ER, Wolf cracks when pressured to reveal secrets to Raffo and Aqsa has an emergency of her own

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: At Northumberland's Chillingham Castle, Sir Humphrey Wakefield prepares to hand over the estate to his apprehensive son Maximilian. Hard-working staff at Carlton Towers in North Yorkshire get ready for the arrival of 120 caravan owners for the annual rally and dinner dance

programme.title: The Natural
programme.desc: A baseball prodigy's dreams of sporting stardom are cut short when he gets shot. Once he has recovered from his injuries, he rejoins his team as a rookie recruit at an age when most players are thinking of retiring - only to enjoy an unexpected run of success. Drama, starring Robert Redford, Glenn Close, Kim Basinger, Robert Duvall and Barbara Hershey

programme.title: Lethal Weapon (1987)
programme.desc: An unstable Vietnam veteran cop teams up with a by-the-book Los Angeles police detective to investigate the apparent suicide of the daughter of a wealthy businessman, leading them to a violent and bloody confrontation with a ruthless drug cartel under the control of a retired US general. Action thriller, starring Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Gary Busey, Mitchell Ryan and Tom Atkins

programme.title: The Cinema List: Spielberg
programme.desc: This edition of the Cinema List celebrates Steven Spielberg. From the new Disclosure Day to Jurassic Park, these blockbuster adventures are top tier

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
programme.desc: An unassuming hobbit is plunged into a battle against the forces of darkness when his uncle bequeaths him a magical ring of immense power. He joins a fellowship of heroes to embark on a perilous quest to destroy the ring before it falls into the hands of its creator - a dark lord who needs it to regain his power and conquer the world. The first part of Peter Jackson's epic fantasy trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Elijah Wood, Ian McKellen, Viggo Mortensen, Sean Astin, Christopher Lee, Liv Tyler and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: Interstellar (2014)
programme.desc: In the future, Earth has been devastated by an environmental catastrophe and will soon become incapable of supporting human life. A former astronaut is recruited to carry out a desperate mission - piloting the last remaining spacecraft through a wormhole in search of another planet that can become humanity's new home. Sci-fi drama, starring Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway and Michael Caine

programme.title: Dunkirk (2017)
programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolan's wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: Sinners (2025)
programme.desc: Hoping to escape their troubled pasts, twin brothers return to their hometown to start over, only to find an even darker force waiting to welcome them back. Writer/director Ryan Coogler's Oscar-winning horror thriller, starring Michael B Jordan, Miles Caton and Saul Williams

programme.title: One Battle After Another (2025)
programme.desc: Bob is a washed-up revolutionary who lives in a state of stoned paranoia, with his spirited and self-reliant daughter, Willa. When his evil nemesis resurfaces and Willa goes missing, he scrambles to find her as both battle the consequences of their pasts. Paul Thomas Anderson's Oscar-winning action thriller loosely based on Thomas Pynchon's novel Vineland, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, an Academy Award-winning Sean Penn, and Benicio del Toro

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Captain Phillips (2013)
programme.desc: An American cargo ship sets a dangerous course around the coast of Somalia, while inland, four men are pressed into service as pirates by the local warlords. The captain is taken hostage when the raiding party hijacks the vessel, resulting in a tense five-day crisis. Fact-based thriller, starring Tom Hanks and Barkhad Abdi

programme.title: Hannibal (2001)
programme.desc: Fugitive serial killer Dr Hannibal Lecter has gone into hiding as an academic in Italy, but a disfigured victim is determined to find him and inflict a gruesome revenge. FBI agent Clarice Starling is once again drawn into the doctor's deadly orbit as she finds herself being used as bait for the avenger's trap. Ridley Scott's thriller sequel, starring Anthony Hopkins, Julianne Moore, Gary Oldman and Ray Liotta

programme.title: True Romance (1993)
programme.desc: A shy clerk marries a hooker and kills her pimp, and together they go on the run to Los Angeles with $5million of cocaine they plan to sell. However, the drugs belong to the Mob and hitmen are soon on their trail. Tony Scott's thriller, written by Quentin Tarantino and starring Christian Slater, Patricia Arquette, Dennis Hopper, Gary Oldman, Val Kilmer, Brad Pitt and Christopher Walken

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 2 (1989)
programme.desc: Headstrong cop Martin Riggs and his cautious partner Roger Murtaugh stumble across a plot by a South African diplomat to launder drugs money by shipping gold coins into the US via his embassy. Protected by diplomatic immunity, the crook looks certain to evade the law - while his chief henchman has a connection to Riggs' traumatic past. Action thriller sequel, with Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Joe Pesci, Joss Ackland, Derrick O'Connor and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers (2002)
programme.desc: Frodo and Sam continue their quest to destroy the all-powerful ring, bringing them face to face with the magical artefact's twisted former owner Gollum. Meanwhile, their allies prepare to make a desperate last stand against the vast armies of the corrupted wizard Saruman, who has declared war on a neighbouring kingdom. Part two of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Sean Astin, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen, Billy Boyd, Dominic Monaghan, Andy Serkis and Christopher Lee

programme.title: Sinners (2025)
programme.desc: Hoping to escape their troubled pasts, twin brothers return to their hometown to start over, only to find an even darker force waiting to welcome them back. Writer/director Ryan Coogler's Oscar-winning horror thriller, starring Michael B Jordan, Miles Caton and Saul Williams

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: One Battle After Another (2025)
programme.desc: Bob is a washed-up revolutionary who lives in a state of stoned paranoia, with his spirited and self-reliant daughter, Willa. When his evil nemesis resurfaces and Willa goes missing, he scrambles to find her as both battle the consequences of their pasts. Paul Thomas Anderson's Oscar-winning action thriller loosely based on Thomas Pynchon's novel Vineland, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, an Academy Award-winning Sean Penn, and Benicio del Toro

programme.title: Nosferatu (2024)
programme.desc: In 1838 Germany, Thomas Hutter is sent to the Carpathian mountains to secure the signature of enigmatic nobleman Count Orlok on a property sale in the German city of Wisborg. Thomas leaves his bride Ellen in the care of friend Friedrich Harding and his wife Anna. The young man barely survives the exhausting trip to Europe and stumbles home to find Ellen driven to delirium by a telepathic bond to the Count. Director Robert Eggers' version of the Dracula-inspired horror, starring Bill Skarsgard, Lily-Rose Depp, Nicholas Hoult and Aaron Taylor-Johnson

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw 'acquires' an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennell's drama based on Emily Bronte's romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: True Romance (1993)
programme.desc: A shy clerk marries a hooker and kills her pimp, and together they go on the run to Los Angeles with $5million of cocaine they plan to sell. However, the drugs belong to the Mob and hitmen are soon on their trail. Tony Scott's thriller, written by Quentin Tarantino and starring Christian Slater, Patricia Arquette, Dennis Hopper, Gary Oldman, Val Kilmer, Brad Pitt and Christopher Walken

programme.title: A Clockwork Orange (1971)
programme.desc: Sadistic young gang leader Alex leads a carefree life indulging his love of rape, violence and classical music. When he is finally arrested, he is subjected to a sinister form of aversion therapy that brainwashes him into being physically unable to use violence. However, on his release, he finds friends and family have turned on him, while others seek to exploit him to further their political ends. Drama, starring Malcolm McDowell and Warren Clarke

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 3 (1992)
programme.desc: As Murtagh counts down his last few days on the force before retirement, he and Riggs go on the trail of a former LAPD officer who is using his insider knowledge to steal confiscated weaponry and ammunition, then selling it on to criminal gang bosses. The duo are assigned to work with internal affairs investigator Lorna Cole, while they also get help from old friend - and reformed small-time crook - Leo Getz. Action thriller sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Joe Pesci and Rene Russo

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle (1993)
programme.desc: A widowed father is coping badly with his wife's death, but his son is convinced that what he really needs is some new romance in his life. He arranges for his dad to go on a radio phone-in show, and his touching story moves a reporter to respond, even though she is already engaged. She returns to her fiance, but the boy, certain she is the woman for his father, plots to bring them together. Romantic comedy, with Tom Hanks and Meg Ryan

programme.title: One Last Deal (2026)
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent scrambles to land one last high-stakes deal for his star client, with both their futures on the line. Drama, starring Danny Dyer and Elliott Rogers

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: The Cinema List
programme.desc: It's time to kick off your Sunday shoes with remakes in this edition of the Cinema List. From Footloose to The Italian Job, revisit familiar favourites with a fresh twist

programme.title: Tafiti - Across the Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Sisu: Road To Revenge (2025)
programme.desc: During the Second World War, Finland reluctantly cedes territory to the invading Red Army. Former Army commando Aatami Korpi is among the affected and returns to his family plot to deconstruct his timber-framed home and relocate the wooden beams on a truck across the new border separating the Soviet Union and Finland. The legend of the 'the man who refuses to die' remains a thorn in the side of the Soviet army, and a nameless KGB officer agrees to liberate high-ranking brute Igor Draganov from a Siberian prison in exchange for killing Aatami. Finnish action sequel, starring Jorma Tommila, Stephen Lang and Richard Brake

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: We Bury the Dead (2024)
programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: Dead Man's Wire (2026)
programme.desc: Tony Kiritsis is convinced mortgage broker ML Hall cheated him out of profits from a land development deal and he vows revenge. Tony walks into a branch of the Meridian Mortgage company for a meeting with ML, only to find his target is on holiday and the appointment will be with ML's son Richard instead. Regardless, Tony reveals a shotgun and holds Richard hostage for his father's supposed crimes. Director Gus Van Sant's thriller, starring Bill Skarsgård, Al Pacino and Dacre Montgomery

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Sisu: Road To Revenge (2025)
programme.desc: During the Second World War, Finland reluctantly cedes territory to the invading Red Army. Former Army commando Aatami Korpi is among the affected and returns to his family plot to deconstruct his timber-framed home and relocate the wooden beams on a truck across the new border separating the Soviet Union and Finland. The legend of the 'the man who refuses to die' remains a thorn in the side of the Soviet army, and a nameless KGB officer agrees to liberate high-ranking brute Igor Draganov from a Siberian prison in exchange for killing Aatami. Finnish action sequel, starring Jorma Tommila, Stephen Lang and Richard Brake

programme.title: Dead Man's Wire (2026)
programme.desc: Tony Kiritsis is convinced mortgage broker ML Hall cheated him out of profits from a land development deal and he vows revenge. Tony walks into a branch of the Meridian Mortgage company for a meeting with ML, only to find his target is on holiday and the appointment will be with ML's son Richard instead. Regardless, Tony reveals a shotgun and holds Richard hostage for his father's supposed crimes. Director Gus Van Sant's thriller, starring Bill Skarsgård, Al Pacino and Dacre Montgomery

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: One Last Deal (2026)
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent scrambles to land one last high-stakes deal for his star client, with both their futures on the line. Drama, starring Danny Dyer and Elliott Rogers

programme.title: The Plague (2025)
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp as his anxiety spirals into psychological turmoil over the summer. Thriller, starring Joel Edgerton and Everett Blunck

programme.title: Him

programme.desc: A student's strange disappearance becomes a murder investigation, and detectives face a tough task to prove the suspect's guilt

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of former aspiring model Tracie Andrews, who stabbed her fiance 42 times in 1996 and then blamed the attack on a fictional road rage assailant

programme.title: The Real Manhunter
programme.desc: The story of Michael Danaher, who found himself deep in debt, prompting him to draw up a list of famous and wealthy robbery targets. But his plans resulted in vicious murder

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood presents a report on a gang stealing cars from disabled victims and a crash course in crime scene investigation

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood recounts the stories of a ram-raid on a hairdresser's that was far from cut and dried, a shocked couple who discovered an intruder downstairs and a criminal who was left panting when a dog picked up his scent

programme.title: QPR: The Four Year Plan
programme.desc: An insight into the running of Queens Park Rangers football club, as cameras were given unprecedented access to the boardroom and the first team. In 2007, the club was facing relegation from the Championship as well as financial ruin, before being bought by F1 supremo Bernie Ecclestone and Italian businessman Flavio Briatore. Soon after, they were joined on the board by Indian steel magnate Lakshmi Mittal, and together the new owners promised a return to glory for the club and its supporters

programme.title: Football Bomber: The Dortmund Bus Attack
programme.desc: Documentary recounting the attack on Borussia Dortmund's team bus in 2017, featuring interviews with the players involved

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and career of Academy Award-winning American film director Alexander Payne, a man who possesses his own unique film-making style

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the London-born actress, who was nominated for an Academy Award for Hamlet and won the Golden Globe for Best Actress for Guys and Dolls

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American actor, who shot to fame in The Godfather, before starring in Serpico, Scarface and Scent of a Woman, for which he won an Academy Award

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American actor, the star of Ghostbusters and Groundhog Day who earned an Oscar nomination for his role in Lost in Translation

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of director Christopher Nolan, whose films include Memento, Insomnia, the Dark Knight trilogy, The Prestige, Inception, Interstellar, Dunkirk and Tenet

programme.title: MoviePass, MovieCrash
programme.desc: The story of MoviePass, the company that offered cinema tickets at a discounted rate. It was a movie lover's dreaam, but in just eight years it went from being the fastest-growing subscription service since Spotify to total bankruptcy. This documentary chronicles the visionary mission of its co-founders, its early success and how it finally failed due to mismanagement and corporate greed

programme.title: Laurel and Hardy: Their Lives and Magic
programme.desc: Documentary shedding new light on the turbulent private lives of Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy and the genuine friendship between them

programme.title: After the Bite
programme.desc: Documentary investigating the surge in great white sharks patrolling Cape Cod's shallow waters and the fallout from one young man's death

programme.title: My Icon
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career

programme.title: McEnroe
programme.desc: Set over the course of one night in New York, this documentary invites John McEnroe to reflect on his life and career. McEnroe's children and wife Patty Smyth offer their personal insights, enriched with intimate home video footage and previously unseen archive material from big matches

programme.title: Benetton Formula
programme.desc: The history of the Formula 1 team led by Alessandro Benetton, told through the memories of its key members and archive footage

programme.title: Busby (2019)
programme.desc: A profile of former Manchester United manager Matt Busby, who transformed football through a career of great talent and tragedy. Featuring previously unseen archive footage and interviews with those who knew the man best

programme.title: Bobby
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans

programme.title: Lionesses: How Football Came Home
programme.desc: The inside story of the England team's triumph at the UEFA Women's Euro 2022, featuring interviews with players as well as previously unseen footage and official highlights

programme.title: Everton: Howard's Way (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary film telling the story of Everton and how they reached their their trophy-winning peak in the 1980s under the stewardship of Howard Kendall

programme.title: Stasi FC
programme.desc: Tracing a decade of football, this documentary tells the story of the East German secret service's control of BFC Dynamo, a tale of betrayal, murder and manipulation

programme.title: The Loneliest Whale: The Search for 52 (2021)
programme.desc: Documentary about the search to find a rare whale that calls out at a frequency unrecognised by other whales. This mysterious creature is believed to have lived its life in complete solitude, pointing to a greater awareness of human loneliness than ever before

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actor who rose to fame in Sergio Leone's Spaghetti Westerns and won Oscars for directing Unforgiven and Million Dollar Baby

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of writer and film-maker Nora Ephron, who was nominated for Academy Awards for her screenplays for Silkwood, When Harry Met Sally and Sleepless in Seattle

programme.title: Jackie Stewart
programme.desc: Documentary about Formula 1 great Jackie Stewart, from his humble beginnings outside Glasgow to winning three World Drivers' Championships

programme.title: Man on Wire (2008)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary about French street performer Philippe Petit. In 1974 he undertook a daring 45-minute tightrope walk and acrobatic routine on a wire stretched between the two towers of the World Trade Centre. Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge

programme.title: Schmeichel
programme.desc: Documentary profile of Peter Schmeichel, the goalkeeper who won the Euros with Denmark in 1992 and was part of Manchester United's 1999 treble-winning squad

programme.title: My Icon
programme.desc: The former Bradford Bulls and St Helens star discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and sporting career

programme.title: Bobby Robson: More Than a Manager (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the career of the much-loved former England, Barcelona and Newcastle United manager. Via the Hand of God and Paul Gascoigne's tears, to titles across Europe, Robson overcame the most extreme challenges - including fighting for his life after being diagnosed with cancer - before returning to football to manage his beloved Newcastle

programme.title: Gascoigne (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary about Paul Gascoigne, which charts his rise to fame from his humble roots in the North East to the world stage and his subsequent personal problems. The film features an extensive interview with the troubled former footballing superstar, as well as comment from those who admired his skills, including Wayne Rooney, Jose Mourinho and Gazza's ex-England and Tottenham Hotspur team-mate Gary Lineker

programme.title: Premier League 360 - Big Sam's Bolton

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Antoine Fuqua, whose films include The Replacement Killers, Training Day, Olympus Has Fallen, The Equalizer and The Magnificent Seven

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The hundreds of gigantic lines, some in the shape of animals, geometric patterns and even an alien-like being, that extend for several miles throughout a remote Peruvian desert. Known as the Nazca Lines, some theorists believe the markings have an extraterrestrial connection, and that the ancient people of the region possibly created these figures in tribute to the gods who visited them

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The story of nurse Beverley Allitt, who was thought to be a caring and astute worker, until it was revealed she killed four children and tried to murder 11 others

programme.title: Combat Ships

programme.title: Combat Ships

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Investigations into whether prophets such as Elijah and Joseph Smith communicated with a heavenly source, or came into contact with extraterrestrials

Known as the Nazca Lines, some theorists believe the markings have an extraterrestrial connection, and that the ancient people of the region possibly created these figures in tribute to the gods who visited them

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The murders of 16 people in Hungerford, west Berkshire, in August 1987, when unemployed labourer and antiques dealer Michael Ryan went on a shooting spree. The community went into virtual lockdown as emergency teams and journalists flocked to the town. The gunman eventually barricaded himself in a classroom and committed suicide later the same day

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The story of Yorkshire Ripper Peter Sutcliffe, who murdered 13 women during a five-year killing spree in the late 1970s and into 1980. The programme features an interview with his brother

programme.title: Apocalypse D-Day
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. A titanic fleet took on the beaches of Normandy, intent on liberating Europe from Hitler's Nazis. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.title: Apocalypse D-Day
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. A titanic fleet took on the beaches of Normandy, intent on liberating Europe from Hitler's Nazis. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Happisburgh in Norfolk, where the discovery of rare artefacts provides evidence of the earliest human occupation in Britain, challenging the conventional belief that mankind would not have been able to survive in northern Europe one million years ago. She also joins a team at Forteviot in Scotland, where the uncovering of a Bronze Age grave holds a big surprise

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do the Brits Win Every War?
programme.desc: The presenter is joined by Bruno Tonioli to look at key battle tactics during the Roman Invasion. The pair contemplate the physical diffixcult of invasion - such as wading through the waves in armour - and examine the power struggle the Celts went through to hold onto their identity, as well as the complexity of firing a catapult

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: Important figures from the Roman Empire, beginning with General Marius, who defeated a barbarian invasion but then offered his own people a grim ultimatum

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: How Spartacus led a revolt of 70,000 slaves against General Crassus's army, and how these events were linked to the eventual demise of the Roman Republic

programme.title: Colosseum
programme.desc: Drama-documentary using the Colosseum to tell the thrilling story of the rise and fall of the Roman Empire, through the lens of the men and women who fought and died there. In AD80, the Colosseum is open for business. Roman emperor Titus plans 100 days of games to commemorate, including an epic battle to the death between gladiators Priscus and Verus

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: On July 1, 2000, eight-year-old Sarah Payne went missing while playing in a field near her grandparents' home in West Sussex, and her disappearance prompted a nationwide search. On July 17, her body was found near Pulborough, and soon after convicted paedophile Roy Whiting was charged with her abduction and murder. In this documentary, Sara Payne relives the day her daughter went missing and recounts the horror and agonising days that unfolded in its wake. She also speaks about her fight to change the system in the UK so parents could find out if a child sex offender was living in their area

programme.title: Colosseum

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed with Danny Trejo
programme.desc: From clandestine nuclear weapons programs to a secret Cold War bunker hidden in the mountains, or even Second World War technology at the bottom of the sea, an exploration of the unlikely people and surprising places behind the discoveries of the most top-secret information

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Earth's neighbour Venus has volcanoes, clouds of acid rain and a surface temperature hot enough to melt lead. Experts contemplate whether this makes Venus Earth's 'evil twin'

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Investigations into whether prophets such as Elijah and Joseph Smith communicated with a heavenly source, or came into contact with extraterrestrials

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: Treblinka, Majdanek and Sobibor, the camps used in Operation Reinhardt, a murder campaign in which the Nazis murdered almost two million people over two months in 1942 and 1943

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The crucial role ships have played in international conflict over the past 500 years, beginning with the battleship's declining influence during the 20th century

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.desc: Karl Ude-Martinez investigates weapons used during 1916's Battle of the Somme, including machine guns and poison gas

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: A Turkish drought reveals a long-lost ancient Basilica and its secrets. A church spire punches through the surface of a toxic sludge in Romania, while the hull of a massive ship in the North Sea answers decades-old questions

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The story of Travis Walton, who claims that in 1975 he was taken aboard a space ship inhabited by extraterrestrial beings. Plus, an analysis of the the Hebrew Bible, which is filled with stories of abductions by otherworldly beings

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: An analysis of ancient monuments, questioning what compelled man to move colossal stones long distances to assemble remarkably similar configurations in several locations around the world. programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Documentary series following the events that led to some of the country's most heinous crimes, continuing with doctor Harold Shipman. The general practitioner was convicted of murdering 15 patients in January 2000, though it is believed he may have killed up to 250 during his career

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: In 1996, two girls and their mother enjoying a walk in the Kent countryside were the victims of a frenzied hammer attack by Michael Stone, who left them for dead. Lin Russell and her six-year-old daughter Megan died from their injuries, but nine-year-old Josie survived and made a remarkable recovery. This film examines the events surrounding the murders and features interviews with Shaun Russell - Lin's widower and the girls' father - Josie's close friend Lucy Gregson, her former primary school teacher Lynda Roberts and police family liaison officer Pauline Smith

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of moving testimonies and spectacular colourised archives

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. This second of two programmes concludes events, considered day by day, up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of moving testimonies and spectacular colourised archives

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts travels to Bamburgh, Northumberland, to learn about the generations of Anglo Saxons who lived and worked there, gaining an insight into the illnesses they suffered and the societies in which they lived. She visits the Sutton Hoo site, as well as a pioneering project in Sittingbourne, Kent, which was set up following the unearthing of an Anglo Saxon cemetery, and looks at the Staffordshire hoard discovered by a metal detector in 2009

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. In Buckinghamshire, Simon Mays and Jill Eyers use a recent rediscovery of artefacts from a 1912 excavation to investigate what appears to have been a mass infanticide during the Roman era. Alice also meets the team examining one of the largest discoveries of Roman coins ever made in Britain, and hears about a mystery in Dorset

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: Over-powerful generals and money corrupted the Roman Republic, with the empire burdened with civil war and violence, and murder run rampant in the streets. Against this backdrop, Julius Caesar, desperate for fame and honour, embarked on a brutal decade-long campaign to annex Gaul and build his own reputation

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: A look at Rome's most influential characters, as they led an empire slowly sliding to its own destruction, and the Barbarian leaders who brought about that destruction. In 9AD, a ferocious Germanic chieftain, Arminius, slaughtered the Roman General Varus' army at Teutoburg Forest in a landmark battle that would mark the edge of the empire

programme.title: Colosseum
programme.desc: Drama-documentary that uses the Colosseum to tell the thrilling story of the rise and fall of the Roman Empire. As the Roman Empire continued to expand, so did the diversity of exotic beasts brought back to entertain the Colosseum crowds. Over one million animals were slaughtered in the arena by trained beast masters, none more famous than Carpophorus

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Police officers and witnesses reflect on David Copeland's series of nail bomb attacks in 1999, when blasts around London killed three people and injured more than 100

programme.title: Colosseum

programme.title: Killer Kings
programme.desc: The case against Ivan IV, also known as Ivan the Terrible, the infamous tsar of Russia, whose reign was characterised by violence and a strong, autocratic rule. After leading a miserable childhood, he had his first rival put to death while aged just 13. He was Grand Prince of Moscow from 1533 to 1547 and Tsar of All the Russias from 1547 until his death.

programme.desc: A customer is furious when Seth rumbles him for trying to flog fake goods. Later, panic grips the staff as a farmer lets an animal loose in the shop

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft

programme.title: Kevin Costner's The West
programme.desc: An epic journey through the birth of the American West, examining the relentless competition for land, power, and identity that forged a nation's history, spanning a century of pivotal events. The host reflects on late 18th-century war leader Chief Little Turtle's battle to resist American expansion into the Northwest Territory. This represented a struggle for sovereignty and survival for the Native American tribes during a time of great change. The stakes were high as Chief Little Turtle rallied his people, showcasing their bravery and determination

programme.title: Kevin Costner's The West

programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to discover what life was like during the Bronze and Iron ages before the Romans invaded. In Norfolk, she helps to uncover a prehistoric monument that has been hidden for 2,000 years, and examines the Hebridean skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies, learning each is made up of parts from different people

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to learn more about mankind's Stone Age ancestors, and travels to Jersey to meet a team hoping to shed new light on the Neanderthals. In the Preseli Hills in Pembrokeshire, she learns about an investigation into the mystery surrounding Stonehenge, and hears stories of cannibalism and the ritual use of human skulls during the Ice Age

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of moving testimonies and spectacular colourised archives

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A fresh look at the Third Reich from a global and current perspective, telling how the Nazis conquered Germany and then half of Europe within a few years of losing the First World War. programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: How the failed coup on November 9, 1923 eliminated Hitler and then movement of some 55,000 members. The Führer and his National Socialist German Workers' Party achieved a miserable result in the 1928 Reichstag elections

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A fresh look at the Third Reich from a global and current perspective. This episode recalls how a social protest turned into a racist and anti-Jewish movement during the 1920s. The NSDAP deliberately used emotions to gain the loyalty of the people

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: Events of 1933 and 1934 when President Hindenburg signed a decree in 1933 that suspended freedom of speech. It would be one of his last actions before his death from lung cancer. Also in this era, the first concentration camps for political opponents were set up

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists' understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. With the help of cutting-edge technology and visual effects, the programme brings the creatures to life, demonstrating how they survived

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canada's Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous period

The programme explores how young elephants, which rarely leave their mothers' side for the first eight years of life, play to develop social skills, while macaques hone their mental agility by playing games with stones. Plus, a zoologist who looks after orphaned cheetahs reveals how play can help cubs learn to hunt

programme.title: Animals at Play

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Part two of two. With the Stargate transformed into a time bomb and the team unable to contact its off-world allies for help, Earth's only hope lies in a desperate gambit that could result in the permanent loss of the portal. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part one of three. Wraith warships stage a deadly attempt to capture Atlantis, forcing the Stargate team to find a place to flee - and a way to destroy the city. As the countdown to destruction ticks away, McKay tries to activate the Ancients' orbital weapons platform. Starring Joe Flanigan, Torri Higginson and David Hewlett

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part two of three. The Wraith close in and the future of the city looks doomed, until help arrives in the form of the Stargate SG-1 vessel Daedalus, bringing a new military contingent from Earth. Joe Flanigan stars

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part three of three. Colonel Caldwell takes charge of Earth's latest hi-tech battle cruiser and saves Major Sheppard from his suicide run on the Wraith ships. McKay and the team race to protect Atlantis before their enemies arrive to conquer the city. Starring Joe Flanigan, Mitch Pileggi and David Hewlett

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The battle cruiser Daedalus becomes infected with a virus that may allow the Wraith to take over the ship, and McKay fights to prevent this from happening. Meanwhile, a meeting at the SGC reveals the Pentagon's plans to replace Major Sheppard with Colonel Caldwell. David Hewlett stars

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Teyla and Sheppard are taken captive by a former soldier whose entire unit was wiped out by the Wraith. Caldwell warns that Ford should be treated as a threat to the base's security - and McKay soon has a close encounter with the infected lieutenant. Sci-fi drama, starring Joe Flanigan

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Glenn and Tara are determined to reach Terminus, but Abraham is reluctant. Daryl struggles as he tries to adjust to the Claimers' rules. Post-apocalyptic drama following the exploits of a former County Sheriff struggling to survive in the aftermath of a zombie epidemic. Starring Andrew Lincoln

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: After spending so long on the run from both the re-animated dead and ruthless fellow survivors, Rick and his acquaintances hope to find peace at Terminus, the mysterious community welcoming any and all in need of shelter. However, lessons learned at Woodbury continue to weigh heavily on everyone's minds, and it remains to be seen whether the so-called sanctuary will deliver on its promise of safety. Post-apocalyptic drama, starring Andrew Lincoln

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Trubel receive a trunk full of relics from a fellow Grimm who has recently been murdered, and Renard orders Wu to spy on Adalind

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: Evolutionary zoologist Nick Cutter investigates a sighting of a mysterious animal and soon finds himself confronted by a gigantic creature believed to be extinct. As he enlists the help of his team to discover how it managed to travel to the 21st century, a deadly predator goes on the rampage. First episode of the fantasy drama, starring Douglas Henshall and Hannah Spearritt

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Renard is rushed to hospital after being shot. Nick is forced to rely on Trubel to help him track down a new creature after losing his powers

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: In the final episode, druid brothers Veran and Harka finally face each other in a dramatic challenge. Meanwhile, Cait also talks with Quane

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Return of the historical drama. In the wilds of Britannia, Cait considers her next move, while Ania and Willa broker a meeting between Veran and Aulus

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Investigations into the paranormal. Medium Johnnie Firori goes ghost-hunting at Coalhouse Fort in Essex, where sightings of mists and orbs of light, as well as sudden unexplained drops in temperature, have been reported. Yvette Fielding presents

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Psychic Brian Shepherd joins Yvette Fielding and the team at Morecambe Winter Gardens to see if the spirit of actress Thora Hird still lingers there

programme.title: Flushed Away (2006)
programme.desc: A pampered pet rodent is flushed down a toilet and ends up in a vast underground rat city in the sewers of London. He is befriended by a quick-witted rat who offers to help him find his way home, but first the hapless pair have to escape the clutches of an evil toad bent on ridding the rodent metropolis of its residents. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Kate Winslet, Ian McKellen, Shane Richie, Jean Reno and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Am I OK? (2022)
programme.desc: Lifelong pals Lucy and Jane face a test of friendship when one of them embarks on a personal journey. Romantic comedy drama, starring Dakota Johnson, Sonoya Mizuno and Jermaine Fowler

programme.title: A Nice Indian Boy (2024)
programme.desc: Naveen brings his fiancé Jay home to meet his traditional Indian family, and they come to terms with his white-orphan-artist boyfriend while planning the wedding of their dreams. Romantic comedy, starring Karan Soni and Jonathan Groff

programme.title: The Cinema List: Family Favourites
programme.desc: Discover some of the very best Sky Originals to watch with the whole family, from Monster Family to The Amazing Maurice

programme.title: The Wild Thornberrys Movie (2002)
programme.desc: The animal-loving family heads for Africa, where Eliza discovers unscrupulous poachers are plotting to kill an entire herd of elephants with the aid of a deadly electric fence, leaving her and ever-present pal Darwin to save the day. Animated adventure, with the voices of Lacey Chabert, Tim Curry, Tom Kane, Cree Summer, Lynn Redgrave and Jodi Carlisle

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets 2 (2019)
programme.desc: Max the terrier encounters canine-intolerant cows, hostile foxes and a scary turkey when he visits the countryside. Luckily for the lovable dog, he soon catches a break when he meets Rooster, a gruff farm dog who tries to cure the pooch of his neuroses. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Patton Oswalt, Kevin Hart and Harrison Ford

programme.title: Pets on a Train (2025)
programme.desc: When a gang of animal crooks set out on what should be a simple con, they're swept into a train robbery. It falls to Falcon, a small-time raccoon thief, and Rex, a by-the-book police dog, to put their differences aside and protect the passengers aboard a runaway high-speed train. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Wyatt Bowen and Daniel Brochu

programme.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs (2009)
programme.desc: An incompetent scientist finally invents something that works - a machine that turns water into food. When the device gets launched into the atmosphere, it has a strange effect on his town's weather, causing giant meals to fall from the sky to the delight of the residents - until it gets out of control. Animated comedy, with the voices of Bill Hader, Anna Faris and James Caan

programme.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs 2 (2013)
programme.desc: A scientist believes he has saved the world from his invention, a machine that accidentally turned water into food. However, he learns the device is still active and has created a race of food/animal hybrids that he must stop from running wild across the world. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Bill Hader, Anna Faris and James Caan

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: Dog Man (2025)
programme.desc: A half-human canine is sworn to protect and serve as he faces feline supervillain Petey the Cat. Animated comedy, with the voices of Pete Davidson, Poppy Liu, Lil Rel Howery and Isla Fisher

programme.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (2018)
programme.desc: After being bitten by a radioactive arachnid, student Miles Morales develops incredible abilities, and becomes a version of Spider-Man. When a super collider device opens a gateway to other dimensions, Miles crosses paths with five counterparts to stop a threat for all realities. Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Shameik Moore and Nicolas Cage

programme.title: Deep Sea (2023)
programme.desc: Shenxiu was abandoned by her mother when she was a child. Since then, she has bad dreams about a monster called Nightmare. One day, she travels on a cruise ship with her family. On a stormy night, she falls and breaks into the fancy Deep Sea restaurant. She meets the Chef, Nanhe, and embarks on a mysterious journey. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Brian Flaccus and Angela Malhotra

programme.title: Bee Movie (2007)
programme.desc: A bee wearies of a life dedicated to producing honey and decides to leave his hive. During his foray into the wider world, he befriends a florist. However, when he realises what becomes of the honey his species toils so hard to make, he resolves to sue the entire human race for stealing from his fellow creatures. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jerry Seinfeld, Renee Zellweger and Chris Rock

programme.title: DC League of Super-Pets (2022)
programme.desc: Krypto the Super-Dog fights alongside Superman in Metropolis, thwarting the evil schemes of arch-nemesis Lex Luthor. Disharmony ripples across Metropolis just as Luthor's former laboratory guinea pig, Lulu, enacts her scheme to abduct the Man of Steel and members of the Justice League. Krypto temporarily loses his abilities and places his trust in a misfit team of newly superpowered animal shelter escapees to save the day. Animated comedy adventure, featuring the voices of Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart, Kate McKinnon, Vanessa Bayer and Natasha Lyonne

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: The Pirates! In an Adventure with Scientists! (2012)
programme.desc: The bungling leader of a band of inept buccaneers is determined to prove his worth to his rivals and win the coveted Pirate of the Year award. An encounter with the naturalist Charles Darwin gives him the chance to pull off a daring crime - if he can survive coming face to face with the pirate-hating Queen Victoria. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Grant, Martin Freeman, Imelda Staunton and David Tennant

programme.title: Cúl Stáitse - Oireachtas 2025
programme.desc: Since it first began over 125 years ago the Oireachtas festival has hosted some of the most prestigious competitions in traditional arts and has grown from an event frequented by the hundreds to now been one of the most important cultural events in Ireland. This year's festival in Belfast had over 1,000 competitors taking to the stage in song, music, dancing and storytelling competitions. This documentary follows the competitors, some taking to the stage for the first time, others returning veterans, while all strive for perfection and a place in the history books

programme.title: Pobal na hEaragaile

programme.title: Carna an Cheoil agus na nAmhrán
programme.desc: For over a century, music collectors have flocked to Carna in Connemara, drawn by its rich Gaelic traditions. Here, songs, music, dance, and storytelling have been preserved, offering a rare glimpse into Ireland's ancient cultural roots. The echoes of those early collectors still linger in Carna today, and their recordings continue to shape its cultural identity. This programme examines Carna's vibrant heritage, exploring the treasures passed down through generations and meeting the new wave of locals proudly keeping their native culture alive today

programme.title: Croí na Féile
programme.desc: Hugh Carr heads to Belfast to volunteer at the multicultural Mela Festival which celebrates the music, dance, culture and food of all nations living in the city. The event kicks off with a colourful parade

programme.title: Blue Valentine (2010)
programme.desc: Teenagers Dean and Cindy meet and instantly fall in love, marrying soon after. Years later, they have a child but their relationship is floundering as resentments have built up between them. They spend a night together in a hotel room, making a final attempt to revive their failing marriage. Drama, starring Ryan Gosling and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Furia
programme.sub-title: Lose Yourself and All Is Lost
programme.desc: A policeman moves with his daughter to a small, picturesque town in western Norway. However, there are darker forces lurking under the idyllic surface and soon Asgeir is mixed up in something much bigger and more dangerous than just small-town crime. Events lead him to cross paths with the mysterious woman, Ragna. Drama, starring Ine Marie Wilmann, Pål Sverre Hagen and Preben Hodneland. In Norwegian and German

programme.title: Transatlantic Sessions - Togha agus Rogha
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh present highlights from two decades of the legendary music show, featuring musical stars from Ireland, the UK and the US, including Rosanne Cash, Mary Chapin Carpenter and Teddy Thompson. Fans of Transatlantic Sessions can see Sarah Jarosz and Alison Krauss sing Run Away and Sharon Shannon and Jim Murray give an uplifting performance of Dreamcatcher - Off the Hook. Karen Matheson is joined by Cara Dillon to close the programme with a rousing performance of The Diamond Ring

programme.title: Hector Caribbean
programme.desc: Hector Ó hEochagáin gets to grips with the multicultural influences of life in the Dominican Republic, starting with a visit to a local baseball club, a cornerstone of Dominican life. Next, he experiences the unique rhythms of 'son', a distinctive musical genre of the island, and he even shows off a few moves. Hector tours a plantain farm where he learns how this staple of Caribbean cuisine is grown, before visiting the border with Haiti, where he watches as hundreds of people cross the bridge between the two countries to engage in trade

He plans on visiting the principal looking like JC to confess 'his' crime, but the power doesn't just give him JC's appearance - it gives him his personality too!

programme.title: 50/50 Heroes

Chef, Aoife Ní Chiobháin discusses her constant search for fresh local fish, and long established Iasc Uí Chatháin, have a new project in the works

programme.title: Glúin Z

programme.title: Scoil Samhraidh Willie Clancy
programme.desc: Traditional music from Scoil Samhraidh Willie Clancy in July 2024, featuring entertaining performances and interesting conversation. The Willie Clancy Summer School represents all aspects of the tradition - the learners, the festival enthusiasts, the dancers, the singers, the discerning listeners, the young musicians and the professionals. This programme features music from Noel Hill, Liam O' Connor, Tim Dennehy, Eimear Howley, the O'Loughlin family and Andrew Caden

In this episode, the duo travel from Longford into Roscommon and Leitrim

programme.title: Goirt Órga

programme.title: Iománaíocht Hollywood
programme.sub-title: Cluichí Gaelacha ar scannán (Gaelic Games on the Big Screen)
programme.desc: An insight into how Ireland, Irish people and Gaelic games have been depicted in the cinema since the beginning of the last century. The first of two programmes examines the portrayal of Gaelic games in film from the silent period, through newsreels, short films and feature productions. Featuring rare and classic productions, combined with fascinating interviews with relevant sporting, film and academic contributors

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: There's a Girl in My Souk
programme.desc: Travis has finally been allowed to fly the nest on a gap year to Spain, but nobody has heard from him in four days and he was last seen getting on a ferry to Morocco with his girlfriend. Tracey is convinced something terrible has happened to him so she heads off to Tangier to track him down and bring him back home for Christmas, accompanied by Sharon and Dorien. On arrival they end up in a bar run by Vince, one of Dorien's old flames, who reviews the CCTV footage and spots Travis being arrested in a police raid. Festive special of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with Martin Kemp

Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits East Wittering in Sussex, where he takes a tour of a seaside garden. The two designers set about interpreting these very different, almost conflicting ideas in their pitches before the successful proposal is chosen and acted upon

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of locals living along Yorkshire's coastline. This episode features members of the Robson family from Bridlington, who've been fishing the North Sea for generations, as they head up the coast to Whitby for a boat repair. Volunteers at Bempton Cliffs work to protect and preserve almost half a million seabirds, and a group of sea swimmers take a full-moon sea swim at Scarborough

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke are creating their latest tipple from tasty foraged seaside ingredients. In Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp, while newbie hoteliers Steph and Luke prepare for the busiest weekend of the summer season. Down the coast in Filey, military vehicle restorer Duncan preps his Second World War American Jeep for parade duty

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Fisherman Darren heads into the North Sea from Staithes in search of lobster and crab. Shellfish are now a mainstay of the catch on the Yorkshire coast, with conservationists ensuring that the lobster population remains buoyant by breeding and releasing them from a purpose-built hatchery in Whitby. Plus, a new recruit joins the team running the historic tramway in Saltburn, and an ambitious couple launch the first wine grown in their Robin Hood's Bay vineyard

programme.title: Futtock's End (1970)
programme.desc: A general invites a motley group of guests to stay at his country estate - and chaos ensues. Silent comedy. starring Ronnie Barker, Michael Hordern and Roger Livesey

programme.title: Biggles: Adventure in Time (1986)
programme.desc: A New York businessman is mysteriously transported back in time to the First World War, where he meets the famed British flying ace Biggles. The time traveller discovers he has been sent back to help the daredevil pilot destroy a German secret weapon that could change the course of history. Sci-fi adventure, starring Neil Dickson, Alex Hyde-White and Peter Cushing in his final film role

programme.title: Buster (1988)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime drama about the life of Buster Edwards, a member of the gang responsible for the Great Train Robbery. Going on the run after the notorious heist, he flees to Mexico with his family. However, his homesick wife struggles to adapt to her new life, leaving the outlaw with a difficult choice. Starring Phil Collins, Julie Walters, Sheila Hancock, Larry Lamb and Ralph Brown

programme.title: Heartbreakers (2001)
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Max and Page develop a canny system of conning rich bachelors out of a fortune. Max marries the target, then Page seduces him, allowing for a lucrative divorce settlement. However, the scheme is threatened when Page falls in love, while a former victim tracks Max down hoping to give their marriage another chance. Comedy, starring Sigourney Weaver, Jennifer Love Hewitt, Gene Hackman, Ray Liotta and Jason Lee.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull head to Marlborough to design and create a courtyard garden for Nancy and Simon. With a budget of just £4,000, the team try to make a space that reminds them of their old home in Beverly Hills

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Garden makeover show, with Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull. In Port Sunlight, the Wirral, they turn Emma and Nathan's scruffy cottage garden into a Balinese paradise. Having spent the pandemic indoors, they would love an outdoor escape - a tropical oasis, complete with lush planting and water feature

programme.title: Escort Girls (1974)
programme.desc: Seven people who are alone in London on Christmas eve turn to an escort agency for comfort. However, things do not work out as simply as planned, as they wrestle with their personal demons and endure traumatic experiences of violence, racism and crime. Drama, starring Maria O'Brien and David Dixon

programme.title: Can You Keep it Up for a Week? (1974)
programme.desc: A workshy man's fiancee challenges him to hold down a steady job - a situation that puts him squarely in the path of temptation. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Jeremy Bulloch, Neil Hallett and Jill Damas

programme.title: Die Screaming Marianne (1971)
programme.desc: Blackmailers try to reach a crooked judge through his daughter, a nightclub dancer in Portugal. Thriller, starring Barry Evans, Susan George and Judy Huxtable

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A self-proclaimed nosy neighbour cares for a woman's Great Dane and is accused of spaying her unnecessarily.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman grieves her loss of musical enjoyment when her violin teacher allegedly sells her a subpar violin.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man claims his girlfriend broke out his car window and attacked him in a fit of rage and later went on a drunken rampage over his 'likes? on Facebook.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: When a woman violates probation, her ex-husband comes to her rescue and saves her from 180 days in jail. He takes her to court for payback.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man claims the mother of his children emptied his bank account and sold his tools used to make cars for wounded warriors; she claims he abandoned her.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A boat owner claims the Captain of the SS Enterprise damaged his smaller vessel after a fishing 'turf war? on the high seas.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A 12-year-old, with a history of discipline issues, claims she was pinched and insulted by an adult visiting her classroom.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman takes issue with her boyfriend renting a car for a female friend; he defends himself by saying he's a sucker for those in need.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A concerned neighbour takes a sick puppy to the vet to save its life ; she expects the dog's owner to pay her back for medical bills.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man is accused of destroying a fence with his car but points the finger at his brother who allegedly stole his car the night of the accident.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A near-collision in a driveway due to alleged recklessness escalates into a heated exchange and vandalism.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Kidney surgery, calls to the police and an alleged assault come into play when ex-lovers fight over a disastrous living environment.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A child allegedly suffers a concussion while playing in a league tournament; the team's coach claims they were paired with the wrong team.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man accused of a series of unpaid loans by his ex-lover can't decide if he gave her a trip as a gift or as loan payback.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman accuses her former friend of theft and an illegal lockout; her ex-friend claims she brought in an illegal tenant.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman is accused of illegally using a professional photo of her grandchild to make a statement.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: An attempt to reign in a roommate's sloppiness ends in an assault, an arrest and a plea bargain.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Boarded Barn Murders
programme.desc: Award-winning author Simon Toyne travels to Cheshire to investigate the opportunistic crimes that took place at a remote barn house in Congleton.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: The Tenant From Hell
programme.desc: Geoffrey Wansell revisits the case of David Ellis, who murdered landlord Alec Warburton in Dolwyddelan, North Wales, before fleeing to Ireland.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: The Lonely Killer
programme.desc: Professor David Wilson revisits the case of Dennis Nilsen, who willingly confessed to the murder of 15 young men and delighted in sharing every detail with his interviewers.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Confession of Murder
programme.desc: The case of Katie Rackliff, who was murdered by Sharon Carr in Farnborough. Katie was found the next day, it took nearly five years - and Carr's own confession - to solve the case.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: The Plumstead Ripper
programme.desc: Jackie Malton examines the murders of Rachel Nickell and Samantha Bissett in London, for which innocent man Colin Stagg was imprisoned before Robert Napper was convicted.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: The Head On The Beach
programme.desc: Geoffrey Wansell revisits the case of the greed-driven Lithuanian ex-soldier Vitas Plytnikas and an accomplice who murdered Jolanta Bledaite in Scotland.

programme.title: Britain's Countryside Killers
programme.desc: The story of Lindsay Birbeck, who, in August 2019, was murdered by 16-year-old Rocky Marciano Price after he targeted her during a walk.

programme.title: Killers: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: The story of Angela Craig, who was poisoned to death by her husband putting cyanide in her protein shakes.

programme.title: Fatal Fall: Murder or Accident?
programme.desc: The tragic case of John Chipp who was thrown 85ft to his death after a night out in Sunderland celebrating his 18th birthday.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Punchy reality show about a female judge that has made a real impact on the American justice system. Judge Judy is real, honest and keen to get to the bottom of emotional cases.

programme.title: Judge Judy
