programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is determined to find out why the body of a young man was taken from his graveyard. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: While Sarah embarks on a wilderness adventure, Charlie and Rex investigate the murder of a college professor

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the death of a well-known Toronto lawyer, after his body is delivered in a trunk with a threatening note addressed to Inspector Brackenreid. Meanwhile, Murdoch's analytical approach to his work catches the eye of the chief constable who suggests to him that he may be the ideal candidate for a vacant position at a higher level. Drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective tries to prove the innocence of a woman suspected of murdering her husband, a well known boxer. Despite being found standing over his dead body holding a gun, Murdoch believes the blood splatter evidence suggests someone else is to blame. Drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Dominion of New South Mimico
programme.desc: Murdoch investigates a murder at the Newsome Estate, which has declared itself a nation

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: While investigating a fire in a pawnshop, Murdoch discovers the dead proprietor and believes a stolen shipment of opium might have led to the murder

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch's past comes back to haunt him when the body of a woman found in a drum of lye turns out to be his former lover, who had spent years fleeing the Blackhand crime syndicate

programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke
programme.desc: While the Duke lies unconscious in hospital, Eliza's thoughts turn to how they first met when Duke came down to London from Glasgow and was taken in by her father

programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke
programme.desc: Things are going well for Eliza until a familiar face shows up. Meanwhile, Duke receives an offer that brings their relationship to a crisis point

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Benedict Dacre rules the roost at his coffee plantation, although all is not plain sailing - and soon after he breaks the news that he is selling up and it is time for his family to move on, he is shot dead. As DI Mooney and the team investigate, they soon have reason to suspect all the victim's relatives. But which one of them had enough reason to pull the trigger? Meanwhile, Ruby confesses to JP she was once arrested and the Commissioner is concerned about illegal crab racing. Anna Chancellor guest stars as the victim's sister - and one of the suspects

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is determined to find out why the body of a young man was taken from his graveyard. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest sets out to solve the murder of a local aeronautics magnate's son, and disaster strikes when Lady Felicia loses her singing voice. Robert Daws guest stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The clerical sleuth encounters a voodoo priest who is intent on winning back the woman he loves - no matter the cost. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Benedict Dacre rules the roost at his coffee plantation, although all is not plain sailing - and soon after he breaks the news that he is selling up and it is time for his family to move on, he is shot dead. As DI Mooney and the team investigate, they soon have reason to suspect all the victim's relatives. But which one of them had enough reason to pull the trigger? Meanwhile, Ruby confesses to JP she was once arrested and the Commissioner is concerned about illegal crab racing. Anna Chancellor guest stars as the victim's sister - and one of the suspects

programme.title: Saint-Pierre
programme.desc: Arch and Fitz race against time to save a kidnapped flight attendant, and things get tense when one of their own is kidnapped next

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Rex sniffs out the secrets behind a groundbreaking gin recipe and the heirs to a distillery after the patriarch is drowned in his own spirits

programme.title: Allegiance
programme.desc: A drive-by shooting thrusts Sabrina and Vince into the lives of a community volunteer trying to curb the violence, a gangster trying to escape it and a teen eager to embrace it

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part One
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, a fishing community is excited about the arrival of their festival queen for an annual celebration, but when her boat arrives with only her dead body on board, DI Mooney is left, yet again, with a baffling mystery to solve. What could a missing loaf of bread have to do with the crime? Meanwhile, Florence and Patrice look forward to their wedding, which is only a week away, but a huge strain is put on their relationship when the groom is drawn into the murder case as a former employer and long-time friend of the victim

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team are left stunned by another murder connected to the killing of festival queen Tiana. But there is a new lead - a phone call relating to their initial suspect - so DI Jack and his officers travel over to nearby Rosalie Island, where the owner and employees of a lucrative investment company all fall under suspicion as they are questioned about the mysterious call. Meanwhile, with emotions running high, Ruby encourages her uncle, Commissioner Patterson, to show his emotional side for once. Guest starring Angus Deayton and Saskia Reeves

programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team are left stunned by another murder connected to the killing of festival queen Tiana. But there is a new lead - a phone call relating to their initial suspect - so DI Jack and his officers travel over to nearby Rosalie Island, where the owner and employees of a lucrative investment company all fall under suspicion as they are questioned about the mysterious call. Meanwhile, with emotions running high, Ruby encourages her uncle, Commissioner Patterson, to show his emotional side for once. Guest starring Angus Deayton and Saskia Reeves

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is determined to find out why the body of a young man was taken from his graveyard. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest sets out to solve the murder of a local aeronautics magnate's son, and disaster strikes when Lady Felicia loses her singing voice. Robert Daws guest stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The clerical sleuth encounters a voodoo priest who is intent on winning back the woman he loves - no matter the cost. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.sub-title: I No More Desire a Rose
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to unmask a 'Voice in the Dark' who threatens to destroy Wintermas, and Sebastian faces his first solo case

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.sub-title: Such a Mad Marriage Never Was Before
programme.desc: Comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives who investigate crime in Stratford-upon-Avon. Tensions rise as Frank and Lu are forced to reunite and work together on the case of a married couple in strife

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank, Lu and Sebastian go undercover at a remote team building centre to root out a corporate spy trying to steal the details of an important new contract

programme.title: Allegiance
programme.desc: Star rookie police officer Sabrina Sohal must grapple with the limits of the justice system as she serves her diverse hometown. On Sabrina's first day with Serious Crimes, an investigation into a woman's murder leads her into the sinister side of the tech world as she adjusts to a more dangerous role

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: A death on a survival show's live broadcast uncovers a murder disguised as a bear attack, and the team must set up a televised trap to catch the killer

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch quickly realises that the boy who tried to avenge Anna's murder is his son. Ogden is stunned but eager to accept the boy out of love for Murdoch

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A vaudeville comic's death leads Murdoch to suspect that Charlie Chaplin was the intended target. Meanwhile, Henry Higgins is elated to hear Ruth's news

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Inspector Brackenreid learns that his youngest son, Bobby, is wanted for murder, while Murdoch and Crabtree suspect old enemies are returning to harm them

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from university with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Documentary-maker and reigning Strictly champion Stacey Dooley, presenter Alex Jones, comedian and musician Rachel Parris and stand-up comic Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. They all reveal amazing stories, but are they telling the truth, or making it all up?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Denise Lewis, Richard Osman, Robert Rinder and Katherine Ryan to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack return with previously unseen material from the latest run of the comedy panel show. Featuring appearances by guests including David Baddiel, Ed Balls, Jo Brand, Steve Davis, Susie Dent, Ade Edmondson, Sheila Hancock, Stephen Mangan, Bob Mortimer, Ore Oduba, Anita Rani, Fay Ripley, Stacey Solomon, Jordan Stephens, Claudia Winkleman and Kimberly Wyatt

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: Host Mel Giedroyc drops two teams of comedians, led by Ed Gamble and Nish Kumar, into four bespoke, themed escape rooms with one simple instruction: the winner is the first team to find 'the way out'. Two pairs of comedians flounder through an escape room, braving the elements, cracking slippery clues and working to get a fish shop open, as their teammates cheer them on

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc sends the teams on a daring gallery break-in, cracking cryptic clues and dodging security in a race to steal the Mona Lisa in the fastest time possible

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc traps the teams in an off-the-scale world where nothing is as it seems, forcing them to decipher distorted clues and tackle a ticking time bomb.

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: It's a fiery finale as the teams race to rescue the hapless Donna from a burning flat. With a blaze to put out and puzzles to ponder, they must keep calm as the heat rises

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, TV presenters Gabby Logan and Melvin Odoom and Cold Feet star Fay Ripley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Clash of the Comics
programme.desc: Professional wrestling action, with some of the biggest names in comedy wrestling against each other and some of the UK's top wrestlers at the Hammersmith Apollo in London. The card features Ed Gamble wrestling against Phil Wang in the headline title match, James Acaster issuing an open challenge to fight any comedian or wrestler who can handle him, and Maisie Adam facing multi-time champion wrestler Nina Samuels. Other comedians taking part include Max Olesker, Ivan Gonzalez, Ania Magliano, Harriet Kemsley, Amy Gledhill, Sarah Keyworth, Abi Clarke, Max Fosh, Mark Silcox, Adam Kay, Rose Matafeo and Rosie Jones. There is commentary in the venue from Greg James, Sara Pascoe and Nish Kumar, backstage reporting from Olga Koch and Matthew Crosby, Ivo Graham as the event emcee, and Joe Lycett as the event's CEO.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Frank announces his engagement, but Lee cannot believe that any woman would want to marry his father so Lucy invites Frank and his beau Carol to dinner to allay Lee's misgivings. When Carol turns out to be perfect, Lee becomes even more nervous fearing that if she dumps his dad, it'll be his shoulder that he will cry on. In an effort to determine Carol's motivations, Lee joins Frank's dating app and puts himself forward as a potential date for her. If she bites, he will know she's up to no good. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Bobby Ball and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance by Susie Blake

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ria Lina hosts the comedy show filmed at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience before introducing stand-up routines by fellow comedians Lucy Beaumont and Josh Pugh

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The clan's visit to the Second World War graves in Normandy gets off to a bad start when Lucy discovers that her grandfather was not the man she thought he was. In short, this seems to be a piece of family history best left buried. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts an extended episode of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jamie MacDonald and ITV's Deputy Political Editor Anushka Asthana joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: Dave Gorman performs a series of witty stand-up jokes about the digital footprints people leave behind when surfing the internet

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie starts a wilderness group just for girls, in the hope of rivalling Ron's male club. Meanwhile, Ben receives a lesson in relaxation from Tom and Donna. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Aziz Ansari, Rashida Jones and Nick Offerman

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: As well as Heavy D, Nat has to deal with some voodoo magic, which all proves too much for her. Thankfully auctioneer Sean Kelly is on hand with an emergency vehicle

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Barry, Darren and Scotty get up front and personal, while Boudicca and Linda go nose to nose over an uplifting prize. Meanwhile, John gives Nat a hard time for blind bidding

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and the team put track cars the Mitsubishi Evo VIII and the Subaru Impreza STi through their paces, Vauxhall's VX220 Turbo is road-tested and the search for the greatest car focuses on Stuart Hall's love of the Rolls-Royce Silver Cloud. Richard Whiteley is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team explores driving safety, and Jeremy Clarkson goes on manoeuvres in a Hummer - a military-style jeep - and Sweden's contribution to the world of supercars is revealed. Neil Morrissey is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and his wife Suki Webster are in the Lake District, the first time they have had a holiday there. Their first destination is Kendal, but first they have to negotiate their motorhome under a low bridge. Later they go to the Kendal mint cake factory, and after crossing Windermere by ferry from Bowness, they head to a beautiful campsite on Coniston Water

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Comedy actors Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer travel to the picturesque River Usk in south Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. The duo discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives and values. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar. Once outside in the picturesque environment, their quarry is the wild brown trout

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high-tech equipment to catch the biggest fish they can - and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to. The pair discuss the true meaning of a mid-life crisis and decide to visit a beauty clinic to see whether there are any suitable treatments available to re-capture their youth. Bob reveals his secret recipe to make the perfect scrambled eggs - but it's not the only thing that shocks Paul

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A wanted fugitive tries to escape as officers take her fingerprints, and a doctor entering America is caught smuggling in prescription medication worth thousands of dollars

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Britain's most talented detectives tackle a dangerous arsonist in Bristol, a drug smuggling operation in Manchester, and a family committing shocking animal cruelty in Cheshire

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts an extended episode of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jamie MacDonald and ITV's Deputy Political Editor Anushka Asthana joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions about rocks, rolls and rock 'n' roll, leaving Bill Bailey, Katy Brand, Eshaan Akbar and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Laura Lexx and Glenn Moore. programme.title: Mock the Week

Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Lucy Porter, Frankie Boyle and Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'next' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.desc: Dave Gorman focuses on the role faith plays in modern life as he performs a series of witty stand-up shows

programme.desc: The comedian performs another round of witty stand-up shows, beginning as he gets involved in shredding some naughty magazines, helps a gerbil to fulfil its destiny, and is surprised by the pictures that develop from an old-fashioned camera he finds

Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Denise Lewis, Richard Osman, Robert Rinder and Katherine Ryan to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack return with previously unseen material from the latest run of the comedy panel show. Featuring appearances by guests including David Baddiel, Ed Balls, Jo Brand, Steve Davis, Susie Dent, Ade Edmondson, Sheila Hancock, Stephen Mangan, Bob Mortimer, Ore Oduba, Anita Rani, Fay Ripley, Stacey Solomon, Jordan Stephens, Claudia Winkleman and Kimberly Wyatt

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: Host Mel Giedroyc drops two teams of comedians, led by Ed Gamble and Nish Kumar, into four bespoke, themed escape rooms with one simple instruction: the winner is the first team to find 'the way out'. Two pairs of comedians flounder through an escape room, braving the elements, cracking slippery clues and working to get a fish shop open, as their teammates cheer them on

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc sends the teams on a daring gallery break-in, cracking cryptic clues and dodging security in a race to steal the Mona Lisa in the fastest time possible

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc traps the teams in an off-the-scale world where nothing is as it seems, forcing them to decipher distorted clues and tackle a ticking time bomb.

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: It's a fiery finale as the teams race to rescue the hapless Donna from a burning flat. With a blaze to put out and puzzles to ponder, they must keep calm as the heat rises

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian and writer David Baddiel, former MP and Strictly Come Dancing 2016 star Ed Balls, stand-up and actress Jo Brand and ex-Pussycat Doll and Celebrity MasterChef 2015 winner Kimberly Wyatt join host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Porridge star Mark Bonnar, actress Sheila Hancock, actor Stephen Mangan and radio and TV presenter Anita Rani join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, TV presenters Gabby Logan and Melvin Odoom and Cold Feet star Fay Ripley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Clash of the Comics
programme.desc: Professional wrestling action, with some of the biggest names in comedy wrestling against each other and some of the UK's top wrestlers at the Hammersmith Apollo in London. The card features Ed Gamble wrestling against Phil Wang in the headline title match, James Acaster issuing an open challenge to fight any comedian or wrestler who can handle him, and Maisie Adam facing multi-time champion wrestler Nina Samuels. Other comedians taking part include Max Olesker, Ivan Gonzalez, Ania Magliano, Harriet Kemsley, Amy Gledhill, Sarah Keyworth, Abi Clarke, Max Fosh, Mark Silcox, Adam Kay, Rose Matafeo and Rosie Jones. There is commentary in the venue from Greg James, Sara Pascoe and Nish Kumar, backstage reporting from Olga Koch and Matthew Crosby, Ivo Graham as the event emcee, and Joe Lycett as the event's CEO.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Ed Gamble, Ed Byrne, Maisie Adam, Rhys James and Ria Lina join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from university with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The clan's visit to the Second World War graves in Normandy gets off to a bad start when Lucy discovers that her grandfather was not the man she thought he was. In short, this seems to be a piece of family history best left buried. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.desc: As well as Heavy D, Nat has to deal with some voodoo magic, which all proves too much for her. Thankfully auctioneer Sean Kelly is on hand with an emergency vehicle

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Barry, Darren and Scotty get up front and personal, while Boudicca and Linda go nose to nose over an uplifting prize. Meanwhile, John gives Nat a hard time for blind bidding

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and the team put track cars the Mitsubishi Evo VIII and the Subaru Impreza STi through their paces, Vauxhall's VX220 Turbo is road-tested and the search for the greatest car focuses on Stuart Hall's love of the Rolls-Royce Silver Cloud. Richard Whiteley is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team explores driving safety, and Jeremy Clarkson goes on manoeuvres in a Hummer - a military-style jeep - and Sweden's contribution to the world of supercars is revealed. Neil Morrissey is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and his wife Suki Webster are in the Lake District, the first time they have had a holiday there. Their first destination is Kendal, but first they have to negotiate their motorhome under a low bridge. Later they go to the Kendal mint cake factory, and after crossing Windermere by ferry from Bowness, they head to a beautiful campsite on Coniston Water

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Comedy actors Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer travel to the picturesque River Usk in south Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. The duo discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives and values. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar. Once outside in the picturesque environment, their quarry is the wild brown trout

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high-tech equipment to catch the biggest fish they can - and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to. The pair discuss the true meaning of a mid-life crisis and decide to visit a beauty clinic to see whether there are any suitable treatments available to re-capture their youth. Bob reveals his secret recipe to make the perfect scrambled eggs - but it's not the only thing that shocks Paul

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey is disturbed when her son falls for an older woman, but when his mother and new girlfriend discover how much they have in common, Garth is the one left feeling disconcerted

Ian McShane and Caroline Langrishe star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A deranged murderer with a vendetta against Lovejoy kidnaps and threatens to kill Charlotte, trying to lure the antiques dealer into a trap via a series of fiendish puzzles. Christopher Fairbank (Moxey in Auf Wiedersehen, Pet) guest stars, with Ian McShane and Caroline Langrishe

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Concern grows after a seemingly depressed Tinker goes missing without explanation. Lovejoy eventually tracks him down to his sister's hotel on Burgh Island, which is about to be lost to a property developer. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: As the Gibraltar locals reel from the murder of shipping agent Gordon Fletcher, Brian and Gerry - last seen being carried off in a shipping container - find themselves stranded on the side of a Spanish mountain, and now face the problem of how to get back to the rest of the team. As Sandra attempts to unravel secrets stretching back over three decades and link what has now become three murders, she still has doubts about the innocence of charming entrepreneur Harry Truman - but that doesn't stop her joining him for lunch on his luxury yacht. Conclusion of a two-part story, with regulars Amanda Redman, Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson joined by Vincent Regan and George Irving

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unusual advertisement appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local gazette, purporting to reveal details of a forthcoming murder, including the date, time and venue. It appears to be a morbid practical joke, but proves shockingly accurate. Puzzled by the mystery, and with more suspects than he can shake a stick at, Inspector Craddock quickly enlists Miss Marple's help in solving the crime. Joan Hickson stars

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The impact of the war begins to be felt in Great Paxford as the women are forced to make sacrifices for the salvage effort. But soon matters on the personal front replace their efforts on the home front as Kate and Jack's relationship intensifies, Pat's marriage deteriorates and Claire finally plucks up the courage to ask Spencer out - unaware he is hiding a secret that could ruin their relationship before it has even started. Meanwhile, Alison resorts to desperate measures to pay a bill, and soon realises her actions will cost her much more. Wartime drama, with Claire Rushbrook, Daisy Badger, Rachel Hurd-Wood and Fenella Woolgar

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries

With the press and police in a frenzy, Sam and Ruth go undercover at a retreat run by the absent man's wife and brother to investigate. When an equally curious journalist is murdered, Sister Boniface is brought in to help with the inquiry. Lorna Watson stars

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: A look at the beauty of the region's forests and waterways through the seasons, with ingenious ravens working together to outsmart golden eagles to get a meal, while osprey swoop down to catch fish from lakes shaped by beavers. In the spring, a wandering male lynx leaves his scent on a twisted pine, hoping to attract a mate, while a mother bear emerges from hibernation and tries to teach her playful cubs to climb

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: The dramatic extremes of the Nordic territories - volcanic Iceland, the high tundra and Arctic Svalbard - where polar bears, arctic foxes and musk ox must all survive in a world that ranges from total darkness to midnight sun. For some, winter is about resilience and scraping through until the summer feasts, but for others it's the summer heat that brings the greatest danger.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps

programme.desc: Mrs Boswell bonds with new neighbour Celia, and Billy manages to upset Adrian in the middle of a poetry recital in the kitchen

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The actor is joined by crime fiction author LJ Ross on a trip along the Northumbrian Coast. The duo visit Holy Island - the setting for the writer's first novel - where they join a tour guide to find out more about the religious connections with the area. The actor is then joined by his partner Zoila for a caravan stay on a cattle farm before taking to the skies in a lightweight aircraft

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo. They are on their way to meet experts who have discovered ancient cave art thought to be among the oldest on the planet. But they have also heard rumours that the mountain range contains further unmapped caves - and Steve wants to be the first to track them down, and whatever is hidden within

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, with Harry as Il Postino Pat, and Dawn French as his beloved Signora Goggins. Also featuring the Self-Righteous Brothers and Kevin the Teenager

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Tim puts the flat on the market in the wake of Kate's departure, forcing Lee to come up with a plan to allow him to stay put. However, moving the landlord's sister Lucy in proves far from ideal when the pair end up in a bidding war. Comedy, starring Lee Mack

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke. Featuring characters including Kevin and Perry, Tim Nice-But-Dim and the Self Righteous Brothers

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike receive some exciting news about their guesthouse business, but the fallout soon sees tensions running high between the pair. Pat and Thomas learn about the challenges of veganism, Kitty attempts to adopt a new way of communicating that sparks confusion between her and Alison. Lady Button is delighted by a new arrival that allows her to tap into a past passion. Robin however is less charmed by the new addition. Julian is confronted by a hard truth, but is determined to prove everyone wrong. Alison and Mike reach boiling point as Robin comes face to face with his biggest fear

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of how the British sitcom was made, featuring unseen footage of Victoria Wood, and interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotter brothers are guests at a wedding, where they are intrigued to hear their dear departed mother inherited the ill-gotten gains of an East End bank robber - but nobody has ever found the loot. Christmas special of the long-running comedy, originally shown in 1987, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield, with a guest appearance by Joan Sims

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. Unfortunately, as a result of such upbeat thinking, the Trotters' cash flow hits an all-time low and an angry punter is left baying for blood. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Raquel invites her estranged parents around for dinner, and Del-Boy is keen to make an impression - but a mix-up with the gravy boat puts a damper on proceedings. However, things soon take a turn for the better when Del's 'father-in-law' finds an old watch among the Trotters' junk, which he claims could net them a pretty packet at auction. But nothing can quite prepare them for the shock they have in store. The final part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Miami Twice - Part One: The American Dream
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Trotters get a chance to escape Peckham for a holiday in the Florida sun - but it means Del leaving his newborn son behind. David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst star, with Buster Merryfield, Tessa Peake-Jones, Gwyneth Strong, Denis Lill and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Miami Twice - Part Two: Oh to Be in England
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Trotters arrive in Florida, and walk straight into disaster after their camper van is burgled and local Mafiosi take a shine to Del-Boy, who bears more than a passing resemblance to their boss. What Del does not know is that they are planning to have him killed as a way of faking the godfather's death to help him escape being sent to prison. Feature-length episode of the comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst, with a cameo appearance by Bee Gee Barry Gibb

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotter brothers are guests at a wedding, where they are intrigued to hear their dear departed mother inherited the ill-gotten gains of an East End bank robber - but nobody has ever found the loot. Christmas special of the long-running comedy, originally shown in 1987, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield, with a guest appearance by Joan Sims

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. Unfortunately, as a result of such upbeat thinking, the Trotters' cash flow hits an all-time low and an angry punter is left baying for blood. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice suggests an unconventional nativity play starring herself and Hugo as Mary and Joseph, and adds an extra touch of authenticity by going into labour mid-performance. The coincidence is so striking that she asks Geraldine whether she has given birth to the Son of God - even though her baby is a girl. Festive episode from 1999, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice and Hugo's baby is christened. The godparents are chosen, the Bishop of Mulberry officiates, and David asks Geraldine to marry him. She consents to his proposal - until sense prevails. Comedy, guest starring Sean Bean, with Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A summer water shortage presents problems for the locals, especially Alice, whose lack of clean clothes forces her to wear a Wonder Woman outfit instead. The parched residents soon see events turned on their head as the water company offers to resolve the problem by flooding the village to create a reservoir

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The home-front heroes grow sick and tired of Frazer's incessant grumbling, so Mainwaring lets him give the orders, gambling that a taste of authority will give him a more responsible attitude. Vintage comedy, starring Arthur Lowe, John Laurie and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Lister yearns for intelligent conversation with people he actually likes, but with only Rimmer for company, he is forced to take desperate action - sitting the chef's exam. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles and Norman Lovett

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Rimmer is sent into a frenzy of scientific research when Holly recovers an unidentified pod floating in space, which the hologram is convinced is of alien origin. Meanwhile, Lister is thrilled to discover he was regarded as a god by Cat's people until he finds out what has been done in his name. Comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Danny John-Jules, Craig Charles and Norman Lovett

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: The Wobbly Randy Old Ladies take a trip to the petrol station to fill the car up. Frank Doberman lectures Damon Hill on abiding by the Highway Code, and the Scousers visit London and get a lot more than they bargained for. Comedy sketches, featuring Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: As Lee Mack brings the popular sitcom to an end, a chance to see some of the unintentionally comic moments during filming that ended up on the cutting-room floor

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The Browns await the fruits of their fraud after faking Grandad's death to get a payout, but the insurance company starts to grow suspicious. Agnes struggles to keep him out of sight, as she has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate to get a place of their own

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: The comedians host Christmas dinner, but someone steals the turkey and they must put their heads together to capture the thief. Comedy, starring Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke. Featuring characters including Kevin and Perry, Tim Nice-But-Dim and the Self Righteous Brothers

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike receive some exciting news about their guesthouse business, but the fallout soon sees tensions running high between the pair. Pat and Thomas learn about the challenges of veganism, Kitty attempts to adopt a new way of communicating that sparks confusion between her and Alison. Lady Button is delighted by a new arrival that allows her to tap into a past passion. Robin however is less charmed by the new addition. Julian is confronted by a hard truth, but is determined to prove everyone wrong. Alison and Mike reach boiling point as Robin comes face to face with his biggest fear

programme.desc: The Browns await the fruits of their fraud after faking Grandad's death to get a payout, but the insurance company starts to grow suspicious. Agnes struggles to keep him out of sight, as she has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate to get a place of their own

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: The comedians host Christmas dinner, but someone steals the turkey and they must put their heads together to capture the thief. Comedy, starring Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Lauren gets a tattoo of her boyfriend, only to be immediately dumped, and Nan is introduced to her grandson's girlfriend. programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina finally gets round to redesigning her kitchen following the blaze caused by chain-smoking best friend Patsy - and embarks on a whirlwind visit to New York in search of the perfect doorknob. Award-winning comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley, Julia Sawalha and Jane Horrocks

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy's sister, who has a reputation for wild living, arrives in time to throw the glamorous socialite's New Year party plans into disarray. Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley star, with guest Kate O'Mara

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: On her first shift as a sergeant, Jo is called to the scene of an aggravated robbery in which the homeowner died in a particularly violent and bloody way. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A letter bomb addressed to the manager of a successful company proves to be harmless but, after talking to the witnesses, Grace has a hunch that worse is to follow. The intended 'victim' turns out to be cheating on his pregnant wife with his secretary, and a courier bears a grudge after being thrown out of the office, but the main suspect is the manager's own brother, who had recently been sacked

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Callum is off duty when he intervenes in an argument and is badly beaten by a man. The assailant is released due to lack of evidence and Callum takes matters into his own hands

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A witness gives Ben and Nate a lead after their patrol car is hit by a stray bullet, but no evidence can be found to identify the owner of a vehicle seen speeding from the scene. Meanwhile, morale is at a low ebb at Sun Hill over the lack of support offered to Callum over the recent accusations of assault

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Mother ends up home alone when Edina goes away to discover herself at a retreat, Saffy moves out, and Patsy gets a job in New York. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and June Whitfield

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers attend a fund-raising auction at Michael's school, where Ben quickly gets into a bitter bidding war with one of his former patients, and Susan wins them a free session with a therapist

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Being the only woman in the house starts to take its toll on Susan in the aftermath of Janey's departure, forcing her to ask son Michael's opinion on clothes and make-up. The imminent arrival of Ben's cousin's daughter Abi promises to be the ideal solution to her dilemma, but ends up causing even more problems

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers star in a documentary about family life as part of Abi's latest college assignment, but while Susan strives to present a facade of domestic bliss, Ben is more concerned with ridding the house of mice - and catching up with Nick, who has embarked on a shopping spree with his father's bank card

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan and Ben visit Janey at her new university, but receive a frosty reception and decide to use the excursion as an opportunity to spice up their relationship. Booking into a local hotel under assumed names, they pretend to be strangers meeting for the first time - but their planned romantic interlude is ruined by a mix-up over which room they are actually staying in

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: The Second Greatest Story Ever Told
programme.desc: Susan attends an audition, but is outraged to land only the minor role of shepherd number two, especially when Nick manages to win the part of Jesus. Meanwhile, a nagging toothache plagues Ben as he prepares to give an important speech at a conference - but he refuses to trust another dentist to relieve his pain

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael takes a shine to classmate Bex, much to the horror of his mother Susan, who does not have a very high opinion of the girl. It seems a father-and-son chat is in order - until the Harpers realise they are starting to sound just like their own parents

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Great Dane is rushed to Fitzpatrick Referrals after losing the use of its back legs due to a slipped disc, with complications leaving the dog fighting for its life. A woman and her two children bring their pet spaniel to the practice after it is hit by a car, and a 10-year-old collie with hip dysplasia visits the surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick tends to a Newfoundland called Billy, who clocks up thousands of miles travelling back and forth from his home in Aberdeen for treatment on its legs. He also attempts to help Barney, a Yorkshire terrier that has a deformity in its neck that is compressing its spinal cord. For owners Linda and Barry, Noel is their last chance, but the operation involves high-risk surgery with no guarantee of success

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats chocolate Labrador Inca, who has a history of developmental elbow disease and osteoarthritis that has led to her elbow joints becoming worn out. Two-year-old Cavapoo Nala, who has hip dysplasia, and Great Dane Charles, who is unsteady on his feet due to a squashed spinal cord, are also on the patients list. Charles' owner Steve knows only too well that spinal surgery is a hugely serious undertaking, as he himself had multiple surgeries following a bike accident a decade earlier

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick fears the worst when a young cockapoo is hit by a lawnmower, and he faces the challenge of saving as much of its leg as possible. Meanwhile, a seven-year-old labrador struggles with pain in its elbows, and a German Shepherd-Collie cross is suffering with a bulging disk in his spin and hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador that is both a guide for the blind and a seizure alert dog, and has become lame in both its front legs. Invasive surgery could mean that the dog's owner, Toni, is without her lifeline. Meanwhile, a couple hope that surgery will save their Rhodesian Ridgeback's leg from a cancerous tumour, and Fitzpatrick Referrals receptionist Natalie's Tibetan terrier becomes unwell

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews attend a playground head injury, a patient with multiple fainting episodes, a man with suspected DVT, and visit a convent to treat a nun with chest pain

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Oxford, crews help a child with signs of meningitis and take an injured cyclist to hospital. In Portsmouth, they help a man who's fallen and a patient with Crohn's Disease

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: An eight-year-old is having an operation to straighten his squint, a man needs an ultrasound to confirm whether he has cancer and a new mum is having her wisdom teeth removed

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A 15-year-old has four baby teeth that have not fallen out and need removing, a man is having his urethra widened so that a camera can pass into his bladder to check his cancer has not returned and a woman needs an operation to stop her persistent nose bleeds

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren gets a tattoo of her boyfriend, only to be immediately dumped, and Nan is introduced to her grandson's girlfriend. New bride Victoria makes a memorable wedding speech in which she discloses the number of relationships she has had in the 15 years since she became sexually active

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse appears as Sam's dad in the comedy sketch show, while Elaine Figgis turns to the internet to find her perfect partner

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Who Killed Jenni Rivera?
programme.desc: Rocked by threats in her inner circle, Jenni made major life changes before the crash. Police learn two people on the plane had alleged ties to the cartel

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina starts a TV company - and pops up on daytime television as a result - while daughter Saffy tries for a job with New Labour. Sozzled Sixties throwback Patsy seeks medical help to disguise the ravages of middle age, while former Blue Peter presenter Katy Grin struggles to get back on telly

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy's choice of landscape gardener gives Eddie something to gawp at, and she vows to get her claws into the hunk. However, Patsy soon turns up with a shock revelation - it seems the green-fingered guru is actually a card-carrying member of the aristocracy, sending Eddy's imagination into overdrive

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy jumps at the opportunity to relive the modelling days of her distant past when she joins a fashion shoot in Paris - but soon tires of the fresh-faced mannequins who haunt her at every turn.
