programme.desc: An eight-year-old is having an operation to straighten his squint, a man needs an ultrasound to confirm whether he has cancer and a new mum is having her wisdom teeth removed

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A 15-year-old has four baby teeth that have not fallen out and need removing, a man is having his urethra widened so that a camera can pass into his bladder to check his cancer has not returned and a woman needs an operation to stop her persistent nose bleeds

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren gets a tattoo of her boyfriend, only to be immediately dumped, and Nan is introduced to her grandson's girlfriend. New bride Victoria makes a memorable wedding speech in which she discloses the number of relationships she has had in the 15 years since she became sexually active

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse appears as Sam's dad in the comedy sketch show, while Elaine Figgis turns to the internet to find her perfect partner

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Mouthy schoolgirl Lauren questions her teacher's sexuality, and guest star Peter Kay pays Nan a visit for a rendition of Bridge Over Troubled Water

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Children's TV presenters Radzi Chinyanganya and Karim Zeroual travel around Wales looking for item to take auction, with the help of experts Paul Martin and Catherine Southon. Radzi decides to quite literally go for gold as he gambles more than half his budget on the precious metal, while Karim hopes there is money to be made from a 1950s stone duck

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina starts a TV company - and pops up on daytime television as a result - while daughter Saffy tries for a job with New Labour. Sozzled Sixties throwback Patsy seeks medical help to disguise the ravages of middle age, while former Blue Peter presenter Katy Grin struggles to get back on telly

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy's choice of landscape gardener gives Eddie something to gawp at, and she vows to get her claws into the hunk. However, Patsy soon turns up with a shock revelation - it seems the green-fingered guru is actually a card-carrying member of the aristocracy, sending Eddy's imagination into overdrive

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy jumps at the opportunity to relive the modelling days of her distant past when she joins a fashion shoot in Paris - but soon tires of the fresh-faced mannequins who haunt her at every turn. Meanwhile, Edina hopes the French capital is just the place for a bonding session with Saffy - if only she can get her to change her frumpy blouse. Sacha Distel guest stars

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie slips and falls overboard on Heartbreak Hill. Also, Eddie 'the Stove Man' and his enormous German Shepherd fix the fireplace on the old Naughty Lass

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Nothing screams 1970s more than a Mk3 Ford Cortina 2000E. Derek Mathewson heads to Brigg to value a 1974 example with classic Coke bottle styling and a sweet Pinto engine

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole is rather taken with a throne salvaged from an exotic nightclub and Simon O'Brien's restoration of a Victorian doll's house fails to go to plan

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Engineering enthusiast Guy Martin celebrates the workers of the Industrial Revolution by recreating some of the 19th century's most impressive technical achievements. Along the way he focuses on the unsung heroes of the time - the men, women and children who worked 14 hours a day for little pay to create extraordinary marvels that changed the country for ever. He begins by overhauling a steam locomotive currently in use on the Severn Valley Railway, repairing its boiler, safety valves and one of its two-ton wheels. He also lays some track using the same methods as the notorious 'navvies' - the notorious, hard-living labourers who put together Britain's entire railway infrastructure by hand

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy Martin visits Llandudno in north Wales to try to return the seaside resort to its former Victorian glory. Over the course of the winter, Guy gets stuck into restoration work on the town's pier, rebuilds an original helter skelter and services the town's funicular tramway. He learns how the appetite for sea-bathing began when word spread that it was good for the glands and finds out how engineering developments in factories also led to a revolution in musical instruments

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy Martin works on a machine that can be credited with helping to kick-start the Industrial Revolution. Joining a team from the Black Country Living Museum, he helps to restore a Newcomen engine - an 18th-century steam-powered device used principally to pump water out of mines. As the rotting timber structure is replaced, worn parts repaired, the boiler cleaned and crumbling brickwork rebuilt, the presenter also learns about the lives of the men, women and children who mined coal

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Car enthusiasts Gus and Will want to buy a VW Beetle. They locate a guy selling a project car, but fear it's almost too good to be true at less than £1,000

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory find an orange Reliant Kitten that an engineer has given up on - the body is detached from the chassis, it doesn't run and most of the bits it needs are in boxes in the boot. However, the guys are sure there's money to be made from it

programme.title: D-Day: Last Words
programme.desc: The recollections of those involved in D-Day on June 6, 1944, when Canada, Britain and the US stormed the Normandy coast to wrestle back Europe from Nazi control. Including the memories of a British sailor who laid underwater charges that would blow up German defences, as well French resistance fighters and German defenders

programme.title: D-Day: Last Words
programme.desc: The recollections of those involved in D-Day on June 6, 1944, when Canada, Britain and the US stormed the Normandy coast to wrestle back Europe from Nazi control. Including the memories of a British sailor who laid underwater charges that would blow up German defences, as well French resistance fighters and German defenders

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings, filmed in Normandy, France, and the D-Day Museum Portsmouth. The story of the landings are told through various items brought in by visitors and rare artefacts held in various museums across the UK, and there are eyewitness accounts from those who played a part, including radio operator Marie Scott and veteran Ken Scott

programme.title: World War Two from Above
programme.desc: Experts uncover a WWII German trench system revealing a daring commando raid to capture it. And, the sunken remains of a tank show how dangerous preparations for D-Day were

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A cherished wartime iron workhorse needs to be hauled 160 miles from the Churnet Valley Railway in Staffordshire to a winter extravaganza in Oxfordshire

programme.title: Churchill's Toyshop
programme.desc: Documentary examining how Winston Churchill brought together a group of scientists and engineers to come up with ingenious secret weapons during the Second World War

programme.title: Guy Martin's Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter experiences the training of a Second World War fighter pilot, ultimately taking to the skies in a Hawker Hurricane for a dogfight against a Messerschmitt 109. In the first part, he learns how to fly in a Tiger Moth bi-plane, before progressing to the much faster Harvard aircraft and combat manoeuvres. He also tries his hand at clay pigeon shooting, which was used as a training exercise to help rookie pilots shoot fast-moving targets

programme.title: Guy Martin's Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Part two of two. Guy's training ramps up at the UK's only aerial combat school, where he must master dogfighting at 200mph, handling extreme g-force, and flying upside down. He enlists the help of the Parachute Regiment for a unique experiment demonstrating the firepower of Second World War aircraft machine guns, before being allowed behind the joystick of the world's only two-seater Hawker Hurricane to face a Messerschmitt 109 in the skies over Kent

programme.title: Guy Martin's D-Day Landing
programme.desc: The presenter undertakes a challenge to reenact the kind of parachute jumps undertaken by 23,000 Allied soldiers during D-Day. The first step is to restore one of the planes used in these missions, before undergoing training with the Red Devils parachute display team and the modern parachute regiment. Guy is then ready to make his a descent into the Normandy countryside. Featuring contributions from veterans including a flying nurse, a special forces pathfinder, and a German soldier

programme.title: Robson Green: World's Most Amazing Walks
programme.desc: The actor walks along the north coast of Normandy, France, tracing the footsteps of the soldiers who took part in the D-Day landings

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team buy a confusing 1984 French Matra Murena and send it to Citroen specialists. Next, they take on a Bond classic - no, not that one - a 1977 Arctic Wetbike!

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie sets off from Runcorn on the Bridgewater Canal, but a series of unfortunate mishaps soon tests his patience - from lost camera footage to closed canals.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Making Dave go weak at the knees is a very rare eighties hot hatch - a Citroen Visa GT Tonic

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave Mathewson admires a Punto that's two decades old but has just 42 miles on the clock

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson is on the road to pick up a funky little Nissan Figaro and a sublime Ferrari 250 GT tribute car, and Paul Mathewson gets to try out his spanking new Scania T Cab

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster that hasn't seen the light of day for 36 years, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy Martin helps with the £500,000 restoration of the oldest surviving Brixham sailing trawler, a vessel that launched the modern fishing industry and transformed the way a nation ate. Guy learns about the boat's design, tests his own version of an Industrial Revolution life jacket, and makes rope using the original machines used for Nelson's HMS Victory. He also experiences deep-sea trawling and discovers how Britain acquired its taste for fish and chips

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy Martin helps to restore Birmingham Botanical Gardens to its former glory and tries to learn how to blow glass to use in the enormous greenhouses. He tries to rebuild one of the first lawnmowers and uses a little-known 19th-century technique to make his mark on the rockery, before he is given the job of turning on his newly installed fountain at the grand opening ceremony

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a journey from Lucknow in Uttar Pradesh to Kolkata in West Bengal, Michael Portillo charts a course through India's history from the days of The East India Company to the dawn of independence. In Lucknow, he tastes the famous kebabs, stops off at Bodh Gaya, where he meditates on its Buddhist heritage, and In Kolkata, he glimpses colonial Calcutta in the grand Great Eastern Hotel

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Reno, Nevada to California's Napa County, taking to the skies over the Sierra Nevada mountain range and trying his hand at cowboy dressage

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey through California takes him to San Francisco, where he travels through the city's streets by cable car, before heading across the bay to Sausalito

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie sets off from Runcorn on the Bridgewater Canal, but a series of unfortunate mishaps soon tests his patience - from lost camera footage to closed canals.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie slips and falls overboard on Heartbreak Hill. Also, Eddie 'the Stove Man' and his enormous German Shepherd fix the fireplace on the old Naughty Lass

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie mends a leaky pump on the Naughty Lass on the Grand Union Canal. In Watford, he visits a TV legend's home and buys gas from a friendly fuel boat

programme.title: Joanna & Jennifer: Absolutely Champers
programme.desc: In Absolutely Fabulous, Patsy and Edina were rarely seen without a glass of champagne in their hands. And it turns out their real-life counterparts, comedy collaborators and great friends Joanna Lumley and Jennifer Saunders, also like nothing more than getting together to share a glass of something chilled and bubbly. Now the pair head across the channel to explore the Champagne region of north-eastern France and find out how the tipple is made. Along the way, they also quiz each other about their lives, revealing previously unheard stories and anecdotes

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie sets off from Runcorn on the Bridgewater Canal, but a series of unfortunate mishaps soon tests his patience - from lost camera footage to closed canals.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie slips and falls overboard on Heartbreak Hill. Also, Eddie 'the Stove Man' and his enormous German Shepherd fix the fireplace on the old Naughty Lass

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Frank has problems with a hi-tech toilet while staying with his brother-in-law, but the bungler's attempts to put it right result in more than a few crossed wires. Slapstick comedy, starring Michael Crawford and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Frank is given the seemingly simple task of crossing the road to call the doctor - but finds a way to bring mayhem to his mission. Betty then mistakenly locks the hapless hero out of the house, prompting a neighbour to mistake him for a burglar. Classic comedy, starring Michael Crawford

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.sub-title: Have a Break, Take a Husband
programme.desc: Frank and Betty travel to the seaside for a second honeymoon, hoping to make up for the catastrophes of the first. All seems to be going to plan - until they reach the hotel. Classic comedy, starring Michael Crawford and Michele Dotrice. Originally broadcast in 1973

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The documentary going behind the scenes of the London airport returns for a fifth season. Inside the terminal, seasoned pro Demi dishes out deals for delayed passengers, while outside on the airfield, Ian is on the runway as it goes into lockdown with a full armed police presence. For the first time this series, viewers will also see the work of the Border Force's canine drugs detection team - Rachel and her dog Cole, who in his career has intercepted more than £1million worth of class-A contraband. Narrated by Julie Walters

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Staff at Heathrow airport contend with drunken passengers, drugs and drones, while Sue and Demi deal with an extra load of passengers after a closure at Gatwick airport. Elsewhere, Border Force officer Bob returns to the job, working to protect Britain's biggest border

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The former Republic of Ireland midfielder talks about his life in professional football and his battles with addiction

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Staff have to deal with 20,000 football fans descending on the western network bound for the Champions League Final in Cardiff. Extra trains have been laid on, but getting everyone home after the match is complicated by a major terrorist incident at London Bridge

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: From a record-breaking skyscraper to one of the world's most ambitious mega roads, this landmark series returns to reveal the extraordinary innovations that enable engineers to build these remarkable superstructures, along with the inner secrets of how they work. Each episode charts the construction of one epic project from start to finish, with a mix of CGI animation, character-driven actuality, and bodycam, drone and time-lapse footage helping to break down the construction and transform our understanding of these incredible engineering wonders

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Max, Denison and Callum go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges hoping to win the affections of Newcastle model Sophiah

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Men from Manchester, Liverpool and Doncaster compete for the affections of property developer Chelsea

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson visits Langtry Manor Hotel in Bournemouth, which was built in 1877 for King Edward VII in honour of his mistress Lillie Langtry, and later turned into a hotel. Ruth immediately cites the building's painted brickwork as off-putting, while outdated decor and imprudent budgeting add to her horror, as she points out why the business is squandering potential profits

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the sun rises on the Costa Blanca, the Hotel Benidorm Plaza's housekeeping staff spring into action. Meanwhile, expat Jessie has an audition to be the new hotel entertainer, and cameras follow the Parrot family as they head to Benidorm to spend some precious family time together

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Staff at Hotel Benidorm Plaza are trying to keep their cool as the reception air conditioning has a temporary malfunction, while guest experience manager Rosanna sweeps Holly and Dan off to one of Benidorm's best-kept secrets. Owner Fede and his wife are turning 40 and have challenged hotel manager Angel to throw them a party at the hotel for the great and the good of Benidorm.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to the rivers of India's Himalayan region to investigate a fresh spate of attacks by a type of catfish, but his plans to catch a specimen run into trouble when locals reveal it is sacred and he will be cursed if it is touched. As the hunt progresses, Wade's single-minded determination leads him into a psychological battle with the creature

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke help Claire and Richard in Barnsley. They are desperate for somewhere to socialise and reconnect with friends and family, following difficult fertility treatment. After a day of hard work, hopefully their revamped garden will work wonders

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The infamous surf and turf relay challenge splits the chefs as they taste and identify mystery proteins as quickly as possible.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The chefs go head-to-head in a football-themed cuisine challenge and try not to fall flat for legendary fusion chef and judge Tony Messina.

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: A new issue with the engine forces Captain Glenn to modify the plan and keep the returning guests entertained, while also pointing out the crew's mistakes

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New York City
programme.desc: Luann tells Dorinda about her wedding plans, and confronts Ramona about her meddling. Bethenny tries to sell her apartment, Carole makes some changes to her living arrangements

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean
programme.desc: Kiko faces his biggest challenge yet when demanding charter guests invite friends to join a six-course dinner. Hannah confronts Bugsy about their past, while Rob and Jess find their budding relationship threatened by a new revelation

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, Security Officer Estefania investigates an oddly shaped object, and Passenger Experience Manager Harry deals with a medical emergency. Elsewhere, Border Force Bob helps a circus performer with visa troubles

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport. This time, staff at the airport deal with the aftermath of a polar vortex. Meanwhile, officers Dave and Terry are on the hunt for a wanted man who has gone missing on a plane, and Ian and Steve battle in a snow blizzard as they attempt to keep the runways open

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: It is the height of summer and as the temperature soars, tensions rise as commuters face delays caused by a broken down train outside central London. In severe heat, the tracks can expand and with fears of them buckling, it falls to the team in Network Rail's Swindon control centre to manage the situation, and they are left with little choice but to issue speed restrictions of just 5mph

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: From a record-breaking skyscraper to one of the world's most ambitious mega roads, this landmark series returns to reveal the extraordinary innovations that enable engineers to build these remarkable superstructures, along with the inner secrets of how they work. Each episode charts the construction of one epic project from start to finish, with a mix of CGI animation, character-driven actuality, and bodycam, drone and time-lapse footage helping to break down the construction and transform our understanding of these incredible engineering wonders.

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three suitors in Manchester hope to win the affections of vintage clothes fan Tanya

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three single men battle to win the affections of Manchester-based YouTuber Zac, hoping their style choices will bag them a date

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson travels to Newquay in Cornwall in a bid to improve the Key West Hotel, owned by Brian and Gill Scott, who opened the establishment as part of their retirement plans. However, it is constantly overrun with stag and hen parties that leave a path of destruction in their wake, and the couple are reaching the end of their tether

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: With the Benidorm Plaza full to bursting, and housekeeping pushed to their limits, the clock is ticking as bride-to-be Hannah and her hen party are keen to check into their rooms to get the party started. Meanwhile, restaurant manager Pepe is challenged by new hotel manager Angel to revamp his weekly Gala Dinner

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: New receptionist Ruben is about to experience his first unhappy customer as he works his first solo shift, while groom-to-be Dwain is whisked away from his stag do to get wedding-ready with fitness instructor Sarah. Also checking in today are two friends, with Michael treating his old pal JJ to a weekend away at their favourite holiday hotspot, and the pair's tickets aren't the only thing he booked

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: An elite handful of fish are capable of living and thriving in both fresh and saltwater. Among these specialists are aggressive man-eaters, venomous giants and a wickedly armed beast that hails from a bygone age. In this special edition of River Monsters, biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade relives his thrilling encounters with some of these fearsome intruders that swim in from beyond the surf. Jeremy reveals how our understanding of these predators has shifted dramatically in recent years and answers pertinent questions about what draws these ocean-dwelling hunters into freshwater in the first place and how they are capable of crossing what is normally an impenetrable barrier

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke have just one day to transform a grey and windswept roof terrace in Basingstoke, Hampshire into a cosy and colourful outdoor room for owner Aimee

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: An intense BBQ challenge is judged by pitmaster Aaron Franklin and is followed with a star-studded dinner service attended by music superstar G-Eazy, Pawn Stars host Rick Harrison, Miss Idaho and Miss California that sees both teams fighting to come out on top

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: Tensions rise as the chefs face their most difficult challenge yet - communication - and are each assigned to cook one of the top five cuisines in America. As the challenge gets turned on its head and shines the spotlight on a select few contestants, the losing team sifts through trash and the winning team enjoy massages and a surprise meal

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Chase confides in Gary about Alex's lacklustre work ethic, but Gary destroys Chase's trust by telling Alex about their conversation. The crew members pull off a second talent show for the returning guests and surprise themselves with their interesting skills. Chase has a little too much to drink and upsets some of the crew, pushing Lucy to lose her cool and her place at the dinner table. Daisy and Colin get closer than ever as they share an unexpected kiss

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: Danielle throws Valentina an over-the-top Barbie-themed birthday party, and Teresa and her daughters go wedding veil shopping

programme.title: Harrods: The Rise & Fall of a British Institution
programme.desc: The history of arguably the most famous shop in the world, which has been based on Brompton Road in London for more than 175 years, employs more than 6,000 people and still welcomes 15 million customers every year. This documentary tells the story of the people behind the department store, including Robin Harrod, the great-great-grandson of the store's founder, and culminates with the recent allegations against former chairman Mohamed Al-Fayed

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of Warwick Hall in Cumbria, one of her grandest hotels yet, but one suffering from low occupancy and negative feedback. While Alex is initially impressed with what she finds, she soon begins to discover the cracks and realises that she has a few harsh home truths to deliver - can she convince the owners that they need to rebrand themselves as a B&B instead of a hotel?

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Former Miss England Danielle Lloyd puts on a dinner party at her palatial home as she plays mystery host to four strangers. Hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former Crystal Palace and Republic of Ireland defender, who discusses the highs and lows of his career

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner present the perfect menu for that time in-between Christmas and New Year, whether for a relaxed family meet-up or a big New Year's Eve party. They demonstrate a clever and versatile way to use shop-bought dough balls, as well as a Mexican-inspired chipotle pot roast chicken with spiced beans. There is another new creation from Lisa in the form of a Christmas stuffing baked ziti, and a spiced apple and ginger cheesecake with popping candy to start 2026 with a bang

programme.title: Live Racing
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin present coverage of the 4.00 Betfred Derby from Epsom, plus races at 1.30, 2.05, 2.40, 3.15 and 4.40. With analysis from Ruby Walsh, Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad, Oli Bell and Mick FitzGerald, betting news from Brian Gleeson, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts as a contestant tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Toby Anstis, Paula Radcliffe, Rav Wilding and Vanessa Feltz pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Limitless Win
programme.desc: Steve is back to finish his game with son-in-law Jordan, hoping to win enough money to get him and step-daughter Megan out of the house and set them up with a home of their own. Husband-and-wife team Richard and Lorna then face the Limitless Ladder looking to win some major money - they've been doing some proper homework, but can they translate it into hard cash?

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by double Oscar nominee Keira Knightley, now turned children's author with I Love You Just the Same. Plus, female education activist Malala Yousafzai talks about her new memoir Finding My Way, Aimee Lou Wood reflects on her role in BBC comedy series Daddy Issues and comedian Chris McCausland recounts his journey from comic to Strictly winner in Keep Laughing.There's music from Mumford & Sons with Hozier, who perform their new single Rubber Band Man

programme.title: In Her Shoes (2005)
programme.desc: Two sisters have little in common - one is a financially irresponsible flirt and the other is a mature and staid lawyer. However, the pair are brought together when a dark family secret comes to light. Curtis Hanson's comedy drama, based on a novel by Jennifer Weiner, starring Cameron Diaz, Toni Collette and Shirley MacLaine

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi sets out to reverse the fortunes of the Grove Arms in Wiltshire. Three years ago, Nina Bartlett gave up a successful legal career in the City to fulfil her dream of running a dog-friendly village pub. However, despite her best efforts and last year's good turnover, the Grove in Ludwell is currently making a loss, leaving her unsure which way to turn. Nina admits she is afraid to rely on her own staff, which leaves her doing the bulk of the work herself and on the brink of burnout. Alex takes Nina a lesson in getting the basics right, but does the former law specialist have the energy to see this through?

programme.title: Protection
programme.desc: Liz is sabotaged while trying to prove foul play and comes under scrutiny from Wheatley. A new suspect emerges, bringing the danger closer to home

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Actress, comedian and writer Helen Lederer throws today's dinner party, and Brian McFadden provides another four diners with cryptic clues hinting at the identity of their mystery hostess

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Ireland's most decorated cerebral palsy footballer talks to Tommy Martin about his successful career and the personal challenges he overcame

programme.title: Stephen Kenny: Big Interview
programme.desc: Ann-Marie Keegan chats to the then Republic of Ireland manager, recorded ahead of his first match in charge on the men's national team. From August 2020

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner revisit some of their most popular brunch recipes from over the years, starting with a takeaway-inspired egg and sausage muffin, and chorizo and broken-yolk smash. They follow these up with dishes including a haddock and sweetcorn chowder bake, a veggie cheat's lasagne, Lisa's honey and coconut cake, and a decadent burnt Basque cheesecake

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of two men, both of them born in Ireland to unmarried mothers at a time when having children outside of marriage was seen as sinful and the Catholic Church went to great lengths to cover it up. When Arthur Fitzharris's adoptive parents died he decided to find out about his birth family, and was dismayed to discover that his birth certificate falsely recorded early foster parents as though they were his biological parents. Bernard McGrath discovered his adoptive parents were falsely named as his biological parents on his birth certificate and no other records seemed available to him

programme.title: Call the Cleaners
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of extreme cleaners. In London, sisters Yvonne and Angela tackle the dirtiest flat they have ever encountered in nearly 20 years on the job, while Maxine and her daughter Jasmine deal with a hoarder who has 20 years' worth of possessions cluttering his home. In Somerset, Shaun and his team specialise in full house clearances and hey are sorting out a flat for a landlord, but the resident appears not to have thrown anything away in years and rotting household rubbish almost fills the rooms

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts as a contestant tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Olivia Attwood, Jack P Shepherd and Adam Woodyatt all trying to say what they see as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to play for the £50,000 jackpot

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Limitless Win
programme.desc: Steve is back to finish his game with son-in-law Jordan, hoping to win enough money to get him and step-daughter Megan out of the house and set them up with a home of their own. Husband-and-wife team Richard and Lorna then face the Limitless Ladder looking to win some major money - they've been doing some proper homework, but can they translate it into hard cash?

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Ollie Locke, Denise Van Outen and Axel Blake answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Motsi Mabuse, Rob Delaney and Amber Rose Gill all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity desperately tries to find a way to pay off Todd, whose malicious nature resurfaces. Kim tells Lydia her suspicions about Sam

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Summer resorts to drastic measures to escape incarceration, Daniel guesses who called in the social worker, David accuses Jodie of ruining Harper's milestone, and Hope gives Will a pep talk

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Does My Kid Have ADHD?
programme.desc: Documentary following three families as they navigate the emotional and medical challenges of raising children with ADHD. With expert insights from paediatricians, parenting coaches and researchers, the film cuts through stigma and confusion to offer real help. From medication to forest schools, it provides a practical look at raising a child with ADHD in the modern day

programme.title: The Way
programme.desc: Family tensions erupt and home truths are voiced as the Driscolls continue on their journey. Geoff's past actions catch up with him when they reach a refugee camp. The family has been traced there by Hogwood, who presents Geoff with an impossible choice - and trying to escape puts them all at risk. Owen makes an unexpected decision, and Geoff takes a step that has devastating consequences

programme.title: My Roommate, My Murderer: The Death of Stephanie Hansen
programme.desc: On December 30, 2022, 39-year-old Stephanie Hansen was stabbed to death in her London home. Her housemate Sheldon Rodrigues initially appeared grief-stricken - but as this documentary reveals, behind his facade lay a chilling obsession. In the months before Stephanie's death, Rodrigues planted secret cameras and recording devices in their home and bombarded her with coercive WhatsApp messages. And when Stephanie began a new relationship, his jealousy and obsession turned deadly

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector

programme.desc: Documentary following the work of extreme cleaners. In London, sisters Yvonne and Angela tackle the dirtiest flat they have ever encountered in nearly 20 years on the job, while Maxine and her daughter Jasmine deal with a hoarder who has 20 years' worth of possessions cluttering his home. In Somerset, Shaun and his team specialise in full house clearances and hey are sorting out a flat for a landlord, but the resident appears not to have thrown anything away in years and rotting household rubbish almost fills the rooms

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts as a contestant tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Olivia Attwood, Jack P Shepherd and Adam Woodyatt all trying to say what they see as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to play for the £50,000 jackpot

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Limitless Win
programme.desc: Steve is back to finish his game with son-in-law Jordan, hoping to win enough money to get him and step-daughter Megan out of the house and set them up with a home of their own. Husband-and-wife team Richard and Lorna then face the Limitless Ladder looking to win some major money - they've been doing some proper homework, but can they translate it into hard cash?

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Ollie Locke, Denise Van Outen and Axel Blake answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Motsi Mabuse, Rob Delaney and Amber Rose Gill all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity desperately tries to find a way to pay off Todd, whose malicious nature resurfaces. Kim tells Lydia her suspicions about Sam

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Summer resorts to drastic measures to escape incarceration, Daniel guesses who called in the social worker, David accuses Jodie of ruining Harper's milestone, and Hope gives Will a pep talk

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Does My Kid Have ADHD?
programme.desc: Documentary following three families as they navigate the emotional and medical challenges of raising children with ADHD. With expert insights from paediatricians, parenting coaches and researchers, the film cuts through stigma and confusion to offer real help. From medication to forest schools, it provides a practical look at raising a child with ADHD in the modern day

programme.title: In Her Shoes (2005)
programme.desc: Two sisters have little in common - one is a financially irresponsible flirt and the other is a mature and staid lawyer. However, the pair are brought together when a dark family secret comes to light. Curtis Hanson's comedy drama, based on a novel by Jennifer Weiner, starring Cameron Diaz, Toni Collette and Shirley MacLaine

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alison is invited on a lavish trip to a luxurious hotel with Liam and soon discovers that he is using her as cover to spy on Amir Joshi and his family. She is convinced she can walk the fine line between her duty to the police and their undeniable connection, but she wavers when Liam opens up about his troubled past and she realises she is falling in love

programme.title: My Roommate, My Murderer: The Death of Stephanie Hansen
programme.desc: On December 30, 2022, 39-year-old Stephanie Hansen was stabbed to death in her London home. Her housemate Sheldon Rodrigues initially appeared grief-stricken - but as this documentary reveals, behind his facade lay a chilling obsession. In the months before Stephanie's death, Rodrigues planted secret cameras and recording devices in their home and bombarded her with coercive WhatsApp messages. And when Stephanie began a new relationship, his jealousy and obsession turned deadly

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Duncan, Tracey, Val, Frank, Freddie, Diana, Kate, Pierrette and Lawrence are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker and Miller struggle to decide whether an ex-Army major is guilty of murder after shooting a youth who broke into his home, while Carol tries to get through to a pregnant teenager. Rosie learns the garage is in financial trouble - unaware Peggy and a salesman with big ideas (impressionist Jon Culshaw) have already dreamed up a plan that could save it. Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A beauty queen is targeted by mysterious attackers ahead of the Miss Yorkshire contest, which attracts more than the usual share of attention when busloads of female protesters turn up to picket the event. Ordered to boost his arrest record, Younger finds a novel way of raising the village's crime rate, while Phil spies an opportunity to get closer to Gina. Steven Blakeley and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker manages to arrest one member of a robber gang when a sting operation goes wrong - but ends up being taken hostage by the man's brother. The PC's disappearance is bad news for Blaketon, who's frantic to pull together a winning side for the annual cricket match against rivals Scarsdale. Cricketers Martin Bicknell, Ray Illingworth and Brian Close guest star, alongside Dickie Bird as the umpire

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a woman is found strangled, a pathologist detects chilling similarities with a number of unsolved murders - prompting Wycliffe and his team to reopen 11-year-old case files. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Tim Wylton and Helen Masters

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team investigate a series of bizarre events in the aftermath of a wedding which boasted a most unusual guest list. It transpires the couple had only met three weeks before the ceremony, and the honeymoon in a luxury hotel ends in a brutal killing. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, with Ronald Hines and Josephine Butler

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel succumbs to her passion for Ross, while Kerry and Pollard present a united front and Sam is desperate for more dance lessons.

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ross is chuffed to contemplate another night with Laurel, while Kim's suspicions about Sam's behaviour continue to grow. Chas and Charity hatch a plan to burgle the Home Farm safe

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Liam gives Cain some advice, while Laurel is enjoying the thrill of her secret affair and Gabby looks into giving Hide a new healthy menu

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is convinced Laurel is having an affair with Sam, while Todd gives Charity an ultimatum and Ross and Laurel are eager to plan another hook-up

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir responds to an urgent call from a pedigree sheep breeder whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, and has to choose between a challenging amputation or euthanasia. Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian helps out at Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, as well as visiting the charity's sister sites at Brands Hatch in Kent and Old Windsor in Berkshire. Today, he meets four Labrador cross puppies, an overweight Jack Russell cross with a love of cheese, and a family who think their missing pet may have ended up in Battersea's care. Leoni, Adam and their daughter Madison came across a photo of a familiar-looking dog on the home's website that they are hoping is the Rottweiler cross they lost a year earlier

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Marcus Wareing's Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: The chef is thinking of expanding his herd, so he visits the nearby Buckhurst estate to learn about traditional Sussex cattle. Marcus also learns about pond management, and investigates how to encourage more wildlife onto the farm

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King are in north-west Wales, discovering a host of inspiring local producers who typify the area's rich diversify. They meet Maggie, a Nigerian kitchen goddess whose spicy West African dishes are heating up the village of Penygroes, and Steff, whose love of travel inspired him to open his own speciality coffee shop 10 years ago. There are also visits to farmers Matt and Jenny, who grow food by replicating the ecosystems found in the wild, and a bakery that produces Kurdish pasties

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson meets novice hoteliers Elly Koopman and Rob Farrow and assesses their nine-bedroom Haven Hotel in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk. Bought only four months prior to Ruth's arrival, the establishment has left the pair out of their depth and desperately in need of a crash course in running a hotel

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past, focusing here on 2024

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Chronicling the highs and lows of 2025 and all the chaos in between

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v New Zealand (Kick-off 9.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida. This is the first of two warm-up games for England before their FIFA World Cup campaign begins on 17th June against Croatia. Their opponents also qualified for the finals and they begin their tournament two days earlier against Iran. These teams last met back in 1991, when England prevailed 1-0 in Auckland and 2-0 in Wellington

programme.title: Karen Pirie
programme.desc: Damning forensic evidence leads to a shocking arrest and Karen brings the suspect in for questioning. But with only 24 hours to a build a case, is time running out? Bel's call-out on the podcast brings forward an eye witness whose testimony leads Karen and The Mint to a horrifying possibility. With a new lead to pursue that could change the course of the entire investigation, Karen is forced to confront why she was chosen to lead the case and a version of the truth she'd never thought possible. With everything at stake, will Karen prove she's up to the job and finally achieve justice for Rosie after 25 years? Crime drama based on Val McDermid's novels, starring Lauren Lyle

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Heartbreak continues to dog Rachel and Adam as a false alarm prompts them to seek specialist advice, concerned cancer treatment may have left him infertile, while Jenny's life takes an upward turn as she succumbs to the potent charms of Internet millionaire Robert. Pete, however, finds himself out on his ear, his misery compounded by a tardy revelation about his new landlord, and David discovers a cause worth fighting for

programme.title: Watermelon
programme.desc: Anna Friel stars in this charming, funny story of what happens when a tangled web of deceit pitches one woman, two men and a baby into a romantic minefield.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police search for a boy who went missing after discovering his mother was having an affair. Younger strikes up an unlikely friendship as he fills in for the local lollipop lady, while Walker finds he isn't over Helen during a date with one of Gina's friends, and Carol's fiance Frank gets time off from the Navy to visit her. Sixties-set drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Chaos reigns after an unknown sniper shoots a woman as she leaves a meeting of the flower show committee. DS Rachel Dawson's enquiries reveal the gunman may have missed his intended target - and when Blaketon spots an article in the local paper, he realises the bullet might have been aimed at him. Meanwhile, Peggy buys an ice-cream van to take advantage of the hot weather, but makes the mistake of leaving David in charge of the project. Lindsey Coulson guests alongside Derek Fowlds and Clare Wille

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Younger is gutted to discover Rosie has feelings for new vet Fergus, who is the indirect cause of chaos when a cobra escapes from his car at the garage. Irritated that nobody's taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Charles investigates the fatal stabbing of a businessman in a pub car park - and gradually discovers the victim was not the upstanding pillar of the community he appeared to be. Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel suffers an emotional meltdown upon his return to work, Carl's suspicion unnerves Tyrone, Idris suggests another night out to Leanne, and Summer's dire situation upsets Todd

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel unmasks his online troll, but neglects Bertie in the fallout, Lisa makes a breakthrough, David is thrown by Jodie's claim, and Alya encourages Adam to take on Idris as a client

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken gets caught in the crossfire of Adam and Daniel's showdown, Maria advises David to come clean to Shona, Leanne questions the nature of Brody's work for Idris, and Debbie asks a favour of Glenda

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Barlows convene to address Daniel's drinking, Lisa arrests Brody on the strength of forensic evidence, Fiz despairs as Hope snubs Sam, and the Platts celebrate Bethany's birthday

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie drives into the mountains to help with a case that reminds her that being a vet is anything but glamorous, as she has to lance a giant boil the size of a watermelon on the leg of a cow. Julie, Castle Vets' head nurse, has seen most things in her long career, but today is a first for her, as a rare wild animal has been brought in, found lying at the side of the road and before assessing what's wrong with it, she must first find out what it is

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headlines from 1998

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: As the war in Europe draws closer to home and the nation faces its darkest hour, the members of the WI find they have their own battles to fight. Alison looks for a way out of her desperate situation, and Frances tries to help Pat while she still can. Miriam keeps an important piece of news from Bryn, and Richard and Laura realise it may not be possible to keep their affair a secret any longer. Conclusion of the Second World War drama, starring Fenella Woolgar and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The race to build a massive new rollercoaster in Canada's biggest theme park before it opens its gates for the summer - a ride with a record-breaking 90-degree vertical drop

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In rural Oxfordshire, Simon Parfett and his team meet Asiyla and her family. It's a beautiful spot where the three generations of her family live and farm together. Among the free-roaming geese sits an old sailing boat which Asiyla thinks could make unique glamping accommodation with stunning views of the pasture. Once Simon makes it water tight, he sets about creating a field kitchen for alfresco cooking, while designer Verity restores the boat's nautical feel

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson assesses a 16-bedroom country hotel set in the heart of the New Forest, and learns its restaurant has recently lost its coveted AA rosette and the decor is beginning to look dated. Already falling well short of its projected annual turnover, the business looks set to come under further pressure from a new multimillion-pound hotel in the area

programme.title: A Cruel Love: The Ruth Ellis Story
programme.desc: Found guilty and condemned to hang in three weeks' time, Ruth initially accepts her fate, but becomes more desperate as her time draws near, and her lawyer puts pressure on the Home Office for a reprieve

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Summer resorts to drastic measures to escape incarceration, Daniel guesses who called in the social worker, David accuses Jodie of ruining Harper's milestone, and Hope gives Will a pep talk

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity desperately tries to find a way to pay off Todd, whose malicious nature resurfaces. Kim tells Lydia her suspicions about Sam

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Main suspect Nick goes missing, and Lisa is concerned about Sean's possible involvement in the disappearance. The discovery of a key piece of evidence provokes a number of new theories, and the police begin to look more closely at other members of the Meredith family. Elsewhere, Abbie falls deeper into Vincent's world, bringing Sam with her, while Sean's vigilante justice leads him down a dangerous path. Drama, starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong, Tracie Bennett, Lindsey Coulson and Chanel Cresswell

programme.title: The Virtues
programme.desc: As he tries to come to terms with his past, Joseph decides to revisit a place he has confused and fractured memories of, with tough consequences. There is a spark between Joseph and Dinah, and Dinah is inspired to address some painful memories of her own. Powerful four-part drama tackling themes of repressed memory, revenge and the hope of redemption, directed by Shane Meadows and co-written by Jack Thorne. Starring Stephen Graham and Niamh Algar

programme.title: The Virtues
programme.desc: Recovering alcoholic Joseph falls into despair when his ex Debbie decides to move to Australia in a bid to start a new life, and takes their nine-year-old son Shea with her. Joseph decides to walk away from his present life and boards a boat bound for Ireland to confront hazy, fear-inducing memories from a childhood spent in the care system. Drama co-written and directed by Shane Meadows, starring Stephen Graham, Helen Behan and Niamh Algar

programme.title: The Virtues
programme.desc: After a long journey Joseph arrives in Ballybraigh, where he has an emotional reunion with an important person from his childhood, and is introduced to a new family. Powerful four-part drama tackling themes of repressed memory, revenge and the hope of redemption, directed by Shane Meadows and co-written by Jack Thorne. Starring Stephen Graham, Helen Behan, Niamh Algar, Frank Laverty and Mark O'Halloran

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Iain Stirling shares exclusive content from an action-packed first week in the villa.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Moneyball
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ian Wright, in which four contestants step up and take on The Launcher - a massive money-making machine - in the studio. The first four to take part are Holly, Dom, Steven and Ben. Can Holly win big and take her kids on the holiday of a lifetime? Can Steven walk away with what he needs for an amazing wedding? And can Ben win enough to fulfil his dream of becoming an adoptive dad?

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The sports broadcaster talks to Tommy Martin about his childhood, his life as a footballer and his successful career as a journalist, radio host and TV pundit

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Magazine Show
programme.desc: All the latest from the competition, featuring highlights and interviews

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: American soul singer Alexander O'Neal puts on a dinner party as he plays mystery host to four strangers, who have to try and work out his identity using cryptic clues. Hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Aiden Byrne puts three home cooks through a series of culinary challenges, one of which is to fillet a piece of fish perfectly. Sheree Murphy hosts

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Omid Djalili hosts the quiz in which nine contestants are each assigned a number between one and nine, with the four that make the final trying to win the jackpot defined by those numbers. Each player's number is also the total of questions they must answer correctly in the final game against the clock

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Nick, Helen, Sean, Nu and Gary race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: South London mother of triplets Emma Campbell is surprised with a makeover of her lounge and children's bedroom. However, designer Ben Hillman takes a big risk replacing her sofa with wooden pallets

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Stripper Darren hopes to be impressed by the style choices of three potential suitors, Sade, Shannon and Kalisha

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Behind-the-scenes look at one of the UK's busiest transport hubs, chronicling the issues that face station staff, transport workers, emergency services, commuters and tourists

programme.title: Help Me Buy a Home
programme.desc: Buyers agent Liz O'Kane tries to help a young couple see beyond their beloved Palmerstown and get a single mother into her 'forever home'. Plus, PJ and Kevin confront their mortgage fears

programme.title: Made in Korea: The K-Pop Experience
programme.desc: The band enter their final week of training in South Korea and have to perform for two very different audiences. First up is a crowd of 700 dignitaries at the British Embassy as part of King Charles's birthday celebrations. Then their toughest audience yet, a group of eager K-pop fans who are ready to pass judgement on Blaise, Dexter, James, Olly and Reese. What will they make of the pop world's newest band?

programme.title: Georgia Harrison: Porn, Power, Profit
programme.desc: Part one of two. Reporter Georgia Harrison dives into the secretive world of online pornography to find out how this industry works and how non-consensual pornographic images are being created and used to help drive profits and power to individuals

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Iain Stirling shares exclusive content from an action-packed first week in the villa.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Footballer-turned-presenter Gary Lineker gets the grilling of a lifetime as he faces questions from autistic and learning-disabled interviewers, asking if he was forced out by the BBC and who the worst person he has worked with is. From the Hand of God to his first love, no question is off the table and no subject out of bounds when it comes to the Assembly's line of enquiry - expect revelations, chaos, and a lot of laughs

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Actor Michael Sheen is interviewed by a cast of 35 individuals who are neurodivergent or learning disabled. No subject is out of bounds and no question is off the table as the Good Omens star is quizzed on everything from ex-girlfriends and on-screen kisses to the OBE he returned and even his favourite motorway

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: Layton, Josie, and Liana sit stranded in the middle of nowhere, freezing to death, when a masked soldier appears

programme.title: Dublin After Dark
programme.desc: Efforts of members of An Garda Síochána to combat growing levels of anti-social behaviours on the streets of the capital. Garda Shauna Naughton and Garda Jun Han monitor the streets of Dublin on their nightly patrols in some of the worst hot spots for crime

programme.title: After the Party
programme.desc: Grace is terrified that Phil will leave again when an anonymous accusation is revealed, and accuses Penny of being behind it. Penny takes it as proof that she was right and steps up her campaign against Phil. Grace finally confronts Phil for leaving her years earlier and he promises to stay and fight this time. Grace then cuts Penny out of her life and tells Ollie that they can't be friends. New Zealand-set thriller, starring Robyn Malcolm and Peter Mullan

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Moneyball

The money up for grabs continues to climb but so does the pressure as one final ball needs to be played - the Moneyball - that could see a player leaving with their hard-earned prize or going home with nothing

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Former swimmer Mark Foster hosts a dinner party and hopes his cryptic clues will prevent his guests from guessing his identity. Game show, hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Inappropriate Sister
programme.desc: Ross struggles to come to terms with life as one of the unemployed, and turns to Joey for advice. Rachel is disturbed by her new boyfriend's uncomfortably close relationship with his sister. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Resolutions
programme.desc: The pals announce resolutions for the coming year and Chandler enlists Joey's help so he can kiss Monica at midnight during a New Year's Eve party. American comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Courtney Cox

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Chandler's Work Laugh
programme.desc: Monica is annoyed by the fake laugh Chandler uses at work whenever his boss cracks a joke and the gang gets a shock when Ross introduces his new girlfriend

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe meets her biological father for the first time and Rachel helps Joey look a little more sophisticated with the addition of a trendy shoulder bag - which everyone else mistakes for a women's handbag

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica's relationship finally becomes public knowledge to all but Ross, who is starting to get his life back on track by renting a flat opposite the girls. Comedy, starring Courteney Cox and Matthew Perry

programme.sub-title: The One with the Girl Who Hits Joey
programme.desc: Ross makes a bad impression on his new neighbours, and Monica piles the pressure on Chandler, demanding more commitment than he feels able to give. Joey's latest girlfriend proves a little too fiery - but he is afraid to end their relationship

programme.title: Live Athletics: Diamond League
programme.desc: The BAUHAUS-galan. Coverage of the fifth meeting of the campaign, held at Stockholm Olympic Stadium in Sweden. Home favourite Armand Dublantis broke his own world record here last year, clearing 6.28m in the pole vault. He has since added another 3cm to the record in Uppsala and he will be looking to break the record once again this evening. On the track, Duplantis' compatriot Andreas Almgren was victorious in the women's 5,000m, and USA's Olympic champion Rai Benjamin prevailed in the men's 400m hurdles

programme.title: The Voice USA
programme.desc: The Voice USA is back with a new format. Kelly Clarkson, John Legend and Adam Levine all return to the Judge's chairs for this Battle of Champions.

programme.desc: The host is joined by Loki and Night Manager star Tom Hiddleston, then playing Benedick in Shakespeare's Much Ado About Nothing, and Oscar-winner Brie Larson, who was making her West End debut in Elektra. His other guests are Kinky Boots and Pose star Billy Porter, who took the Emcee role in Cabaret and Strictly and Traitors Claudia host Winkleman. Plus, singer-songwriter Myles Smith performs Nice to Meet You
